narrative: **Age 18 (720 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (720 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories.
name: Talar Edanel

narrative: **Age 19 (669 TR)** — He waited for a ferry for most of a morning. He had nothing to do. He discovered he did not mind.

**Age 20 (670 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it.
**Age 21 (671 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Sira Oramin (noble, age 18)
**Age 22 (672 TR)** — He watched a true expert do something he had done himself for years. He understood the difference immediately.

**Age 23 (673 TR)** — The wound had gone bad by the time they brought him to the hospital sisters. He did not remember most of the following week. What he remembered afterward was the smell of herbs and the sound of someone — it might have been the same woman each time — sitting with him in the dark hours and not speaking. He recovered. He did not take this for granted.
**Age 24 (674 TR)** — He could not decide between two options and tossed a coin. The coin showed one side and he immediately knew he had wanted the other.
**Age 24 (674 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Roric Oramin. week 40 Roric Oramin born (male)
**Age 25 (675 TR)** — Loss drove him inward; he spent months in solitary reflection.
**Age 25 (675 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later.
**Age 26 (676 TR)** — He found his first grey hair. He pulled it out, then felt foolish, then stopped caring.
**Age 27 (677 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories.
**Age 27 (677 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 28 (678 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 28 (678 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 28 (678 TR)** — Roric Oramin died young — a child still. Roric Oramin died (age 4)
**Age 29 (679 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.

**Age 29 (679 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting.
**Age 30 (680 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own.
**Age 30 (680 TR)** — One of his children overheard a conversation and formed a conclusion so wrong it took months to correct. By then the wrong version had spread to the neighbours.
**Age 31 (681 TR)** — He misjudged someone's state entirely. He approached in the wrong way at the wrong time and the conversation went badly.
**Age 31 (681 TR)** — His wife lost the child before its time. He did not know what to do with his grief. week 14

**Age 32 (682 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy.
**Age 32 (682 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 33 (683 TR)** — He kept company he should not have. Whether it meant anything, only he knew.
**Age 33 (683 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 34 (684 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 34 (684 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Dara Oramin — survived. week 40 Dara Oramin born (female)
**Age 35 (685 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.

**Age 35 (685 TR)** — She was ill for three weeks before she died. He had not understood until the second week what the illness was going to become. He had time to understand afterward — the empty space in the work, the meals, the bed — and he understands it still. He is doing what people do. He is continuing.
**Age 36 (686 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.
**Age 37 (687 TR)** — A tutor, a priest, or simply a talented neighbour opened music to him.
**Age 37 (687 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it.
**Age 38 (688 TR)** — He found someone who knew more than he did and paid in time and deference. Studied under Horic
**Age 38 (688 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 20
**Age 39 (689 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 39 (689 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 40 (690 TR)** — The letter arrived with no signature. It described, in precise and accurate detail, something he had done three years ago that he had believed no one knew. The terms were not unreasonable. That was almost the worst part — whoever had sent it understood exactly what the information was worth and had priced it accordingly.
**Age 40 (690 TR)** — Sira Oramin died. They had been married 19 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Sira Oramin died (approx. age 37)
**Age 41 (691 TR)** — A smell he encountered in passing — bread, woodsmoke, something sharper — put him back somewhere he had not thought of in years.
**Age 41 (691 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it.
**Age 42 (692 TR)** — Something he expected to take an hour took most of the day. He was not angry about it. It was simply what it was.
**Age 43 (693 TR)** — He kept waiting for the right moment to say something and the moment passed. The thing went unsaid.
**Age 44 (694 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 44 (694 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.
**Age 44 (694 TR)** — His son Tovan Oramin married Fara Oramin. Tovan Oramin married Fara Oramin (noble, age 16)
**Age 45 (695 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 45 (695 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 45 (695 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Tovan Oramin's child born (female, Tovan Oramin age 18)
**Age 46 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 46 (696 TR)** — The pregnancy ended before it had properly begun. He did not know what to say, so he said nothing useful. week 6
**Age 46 (696 TR)** — Tovan Oramin left home to make his own way. Tovan Oramin left home (age 19)
**Age 47 (697 TR)** — Determined to regain his strength, he threw himself into physical discipline.
**Age 47 (697 TR)** — One of his children said something at the table — a mishearing, a confusion, a small profound mistake — that became the family's private language for years.

**Age 48 (698 TR)** — He had been waiting months for access to a particular merchant. The introduction came from an unexpected direction — a Halean Shenasene at a private gathering who mentioned his name to the right person with the right word attached. He never learned what the priestess received in return. He assumed the accounting was accurate.
**Age 48 (698 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 49 (699 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 49 (699 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 50 (700 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave.
**Age 50 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 51 (701 TR)** — The engagement was the following morning and everyone who was paying attention knew the numbers. He found the Matakea near the supply wagons at dusk and said what he needed to say. He had not spoken to a priest since boyhood. It was not as difficult as he had expected. He slept better than he had in weeks.
**Age 52 (702 TR)** — Someone thanked him for something he had completely forgotten doing. He accepted it. He tried to remember what it had been.
**Age 52 (702 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (702 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (702 TR)** — His daughter Dara Oramin married Corin Oramin. Dara Oramin married Corin Oramin (noble, age 16)

**Age 53 (703 TR)** — His wife had a habit — a sound she made, a thing she did with her hands, a phrase she repeated — that had not bothered him for years and then, one day, bothered him enormously. He said nothing.

**Age 54 (704 TR)** — He would not call it a miracle to someone he did not know. He would say: the standard had fallen and the line was breaking, and then the light changed, and the line held. He had seen men stand who had been running a moment before and he could not explain it. He did not try to explain it in the report. He wrote that the line held.
**Age 54 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 55 (705 TR)** — He sat in the chapel for the first time in years and found it unchanged. The stone, the sword-altar, the quality of light through the narrow windows. He had fought once in the goddess's name, more or less, or had told himself he had. He was not sure what he thought of that now.

**Age 56 (706 TR)** — The Argent Order knights were not what the stories suggested — they were quieter, more methodical, and considerably more frightening than the stories suggested. He served under their command for six weeks and learned more about the Navehan church than he had wanted to know. Their discipline was absolute. They did not celebrate kills.
**Age 56 (706 TR)** — Something was said — or done — that could not easily be undone. He and his child no longer spoke.
**Age 56 (706 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 56 (706 TR)** — His daughter Dara Oramin remarried. Dara Oramin remarried Evan Karsen (peasant, age 22)

**Age 57 (707 TR)** — The Matakea walked the line in the hour before dawn, touching each soldier's shoulder. He had heard the Alamirata a hundred times in temple. Here it meant something different — the same words, the same cadence, but spoken over men who might be dead by midday. He had not expected to find it comforting. He found it comforting.
**Age 57 (707 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved.
**Age 57 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 57 (707 TR)** — Dara Oramin left home to make his own way. Dara Oramin left home (age 23)
**Age 58 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 58 (708 TR)** — Dara Oramin's infant died. He grieved for the child and for what he saw in Dara Oramin's face. Dara Oramin's child died (age 1)
**Age 59 (709 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour.
**Age 60 (710 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the head or face that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 60 (710 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 60 (710 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 61 (711 TR)** — He recognised something in the young and invested time in it. Mentored Beira Ilenkar
**Age 61 (711 TR)** — Varath Oramin died. They had been married 17 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Varath Oramin died (approx. age 61)
**Age 61 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 62 (712 TR)** — He began to doubt the teachings he had followed without question.
**Age 62 (712 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 63 (713 TR)** — He looked around at a feast and realised he knew almost no one his own age. The men he had grown up with, trained with, quarrelled with — most of them were gone. He was still here. He was not sure what that meant.

**Age 64 (714 TR)** — He handed the last of it over — the keys, the ledgers, the daily decisions — and did not immediately know what to do with himself. The first month was strange. The second was less so. By the third he had discovered that there were things he had wanted to read for thirty years and had not, and that the garden was more interesting than he had appreciated when he was always somewhere else. He does not miss the work. He is surprised he does not miss it more.
**Age 64 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 65 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 65 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 65 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 66 (716 TR)** — It had begun as professional acquaintance. Somewhere in the course of years it became something else — not sentimentally, but in the way that trust accumulates. Beira Ilenkar became a close friend
**Age 66 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 67 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 67 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 68 (718 TR)** — Time or a change of lord brought word that he might return.
**Age 68 (718 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 69 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 70 (720 TR)** — Time had softened what once seemed unforgivable. They made their peace. Reconciled with Kuric Elenshen
**Age 70 (720 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

name: Laric Oramin

narrative: **Age 18 (674 TR)** — He kept waiting for the right moment to say something and the moment passed. The thing went unsaid.

**Age 19 (675 TR)** — The establishment was well run and the Shenasene who presided had a gift for making a man feel that losing money was a cultivated pleasure. He came out ahead, which he suspected was not the usual outcome and which made him more cautious rather than less on subsequent visits.
**Age 20 (676 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 21 (677 TR)** — He said exactly the right thing at the right moment. He would not have been able to do it again.
**Age 22 (678 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him.
**Age 23 (679 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them.

**Age 24 (680 TR)** — The knife came out of the dark on the Kolisa bridge. He was lucky — the cobblestones were wet and he slipped at the wrong moment, which turned out to be the right moment. He did not see the face. He lay in the mud listening to footsteps retreating and understood that someone had spent money to end his life and received inadequate value.
**Age 25 (681 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 26 (682 TR)** — He married. The arrangement made sense practically; whether it would make sense in other ways remained to be seen. Married Kela Walendi (noble, age 22)
**Age 27 (683 TR)** — A door he expected to open did not. He pulled, then pushed, then pulled again, then read the situation correctly. There were witnesses.
**Age 27 (683 TR)** — His wife began to snore. He discovered he could sleep through it after a few months. This became a thing he was quietly proud of.
**Age 28 (684 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 28 (684 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Pedar Walendi. week 40 Pedar Walendi born (male)

**Age 29 (685 TR)** — The letter arrived with no signature. It described, in precise and accurate detail, something he had done three years ago that he had believed no one knew. The terms were not unreasonable. That was almost the worst part — whoever had sent it understood exactly what the information was worth and had priced it accordingly.
**Age 29 (685 TR)** — He watched his father — or mother — work through something difficult without complaining. He understood something that he could not have put into words.

**Age 30 (686 TR)** — Another family's child came to live under his roof — to learn, to serve as a page, or simply as a pledge of alliance. He found himself responsible for someone else's son. Took in Pedar Garekar as ward
**Age 31 (687 TR)** — He tried something on a whim and found he was good at it. Not exceptional — just good. It surprised him.
**Age 31 (687 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 32 (688 TR)** — Something struck him as funny at completely the wrong moment. He was unable to stop laughing for far longer than the situation called for.

**Age 33 (689 TR)** — The knight crossed the market square in full harness and people made way without being asked to. He was not young — the grey was visible where the coif was pushed back — and he moved as if the armour weighed nothing, which it did not. He watched him pass and thought something complicated about vocation.
**Age 34 (690 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 34 (690 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Calla Walendi born (female)
**Age 35 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 35 (691 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 36 (692 TR)** — The years finally told. He moved more carefully, tired more quickly. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 36 (692 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Geda Walendi came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Geda Walendi born (female)
**Age 37 (693 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.
**Age 37 (693 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 37 (693 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Gareth Walendi died (age 0)

**Age 38 (694 TR)** — The Serolan heard both parties in the chapter house. He was fair in the sense that neither of them got what they wanted. The settlement was binding by custom if not by law and cost him less than a court would have and rather more than he thought the matter was worth.
**Age 39 (695 TR)** — He heard midnight bells from a temple he did not know, in a town he was passing through. The sound carried a long way.
**Age 39 (695 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.
**Age 40 (696 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 40 (696 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 41 (697 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour.

**Age 41 (697 TR)** — She was ill for three weeks before she died. He had not understood until the second week what the illness was going to become. He had time to understand afterward — the empty space in the work, the meals, the bed — and he understands it still. He is doing what people do. He is continuing.

**Age 42 (698 TR)** — He says the Tirannon at dawn. Not every day — but on days when it seems to matter, when something is owed. He does not know exactly when he started. He knows it became a habit on campaign and that the habit outlasted the campaign by fifteen years and counting.
**Age 42 (698 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (699 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 43 (699 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it.
**Age 44 (700 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.
**Age 44 (700 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer.
**Age 44 (700 TR)** — His son Pedar Walendi married Rira Walendi. Pedar Walendi married Rira Walendi (noble, age 16)

**Age 45 (701 TR)** — With his second parent gone he was, in some sense he had not felt before, finally alone in the world. The house he grew up in would be sold or given over. He found he was not ready for that.
**Age 45 (701 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.
**Age 46 (702 TR)** — Someone he loved called for a priest at the end. It changed how he thought about faith.
**Age 46 (702 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.

**Age 47 (703 TR)** — The inquiry was conducted by a knight of the Argent Order — courteous, precise, and entirely certain of what he was looking for. They had received a report. He answered every question honestly and was cleared, which left him with the durable knowledge that someone had put his name before the Order and had done so deliberately.
**Age 47 (703 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 5
**Age 48 (704 TR)** — Decades of reliability were acknowledged with words and perhaps a small grant.

**Age 48 (704 TR)** — His wife had a habit — a sound she made, a thing she did with her hands, a phrase she repeated — that had not bothered him for years and then, one day, bothered him enormously. He said nothing.
**Age 49 (705 TR)** — He stood in the crowd and watched a man hang. It was meant to be a warning; he was not sure what lesson he took from it.
**Age 49 (705 TR)** — The child came too still. She had no husband to share the weight of it. week 39
**Age 50 (706 TR)** — He remembered a place from his childhood he had completely forgotten. The memory arrived without warning and with unusual clarity.
**Age 50 (706 TR)** — One of his children overheard a conversation and formed a conclusion so wrong it took months to correct. By then the wrong version had spread to the neighbours.
**Age 51 (707 TR)** — He received news he expected to be devastated by and found he was oddly calm. He was not sure whether to be relieved or worried by this.
**Age 51 (707 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 51 (707 TR)** — His daughter Calla Walendi married Gareth Walendi. Calla Walendi married Gareth Walendi (noble, age 16)
**Age 51 (707 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Calla Walendi's child born (female, Calla Walendi age 17)
**Age 51 (707 TR)** — Calla Walendi's infant died. He grieved for the child and for what he saw in Calla Walendi's face. Calla Walendi's child died (age 0)
**Age 52 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 52 (708 TR)** — Kela Walendi died. They had been married 26 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Kela Walendi died (approx. age 48)
**Age 52 (708 TR)** — Pedar Walendi left home to make his own way. Pedar Walendi left home (age 24)
**Age 53 (709 TR)** — The illness had stripped him down. He spent months rebuilding, finding it slower than he had expected.

**Age 54 (710 TR)** — He came home to the door off its hinges and the strongbox open. They had taken the silver plate, his wife's jewellery, and the tools he had been saving for three years. The worst of it was not the loss — it was the knowledge that someone had been through everything, had touched everything, had made themselves at home in his absence.
**Age 54 (710 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 55 (711 TR)** — He sat up reading, or thinking, or doing nothing in particular, until the candle went out. He sat in the dark for a while before going to bed.
**Age 55 (711 TR)** — His daughter Geda Walendi married Marek Skinner. Geda Walendi married Marek Skinner (artisan, age 19)
**Age 56 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 57 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 57 (713 TR)** — Something was said — or done — that could not easily be undone. He and his child no longer spoke.
**Age 57 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 58 (714 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 58 (714 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 58 (714 TR)** — Geda Walendi left home to make his own way. Geda Walendi left home (age 22)
**Age 59 (715 TR)** — It had not started as enmity. By the end, it was nothing else. Pedar Garekar became an enemy
**Age 59 (715 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning.
**Age 59 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 60 (716 TR)** — His lord had given an order he could not follow. Not would not — could not, in the sense that following it would have made him someone he did not want to be and could not have looked at in still water. He refused, formally, with witnesses. He was not punished. That surprised him more than the order had.
**Age 61 (717 TR)** — A boundary marker, a contested will, a neighbour's greed — the dispute dragged on.
**Age 61 (717 TR)** — Calla Walendi left home to make his own way. Calla Walendi left home (age 27)
**Age 61 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 62 (718 TR)** — Time had softened what once seemed unforgivable. They made their peace. Reconciled with Pedar Garekar
**Age 63 (719 TR)** — He reached out, or they did. The thing between them was old enough to look smaller than it had. Reconciled with Pedar Garekar
**Age 63 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 63 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 64 (720 TR)** — He did not handle the loss publicly. He handled it alone, over a long time, in ways that left marks.
**Age 64 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

name: Seric Walendi

narrative: **Age 18 (680 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.
**Age 19 (681 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave.
**Age 20 (682 TR)** — A tutor, a priest, or simply a talented neighbour opened music to him.
**Age 21 (683 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Erith Falendi (noble, age 16)
**Age 21 (683 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 22 (684 TR)** — Through deed or patience, his name was spoken without scorn once more.
**Age 22 (684 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it.
**Age 23 (685 TR)** — He heard midnight bells from a temple he did not know, in a town he was passing through. The sound carried a long way.
**Age 23 (685 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Ulda Falendi — survived. week 40 Ulda Falendi born (female)
**Age 24 (686 TR)** — The dice or the wager — it did not matter which. He lost more than he had meant to stake and called it bad luck rather than bad judgment.
**Age 24 (686 TR)** — His wife's labour was difficult. She was weakened for months, and the birth aged them both.
**Age 25 (687 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Gorin Salendi (noble)
**Age 25 (687 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years.
**Age 25 (687 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Ulda Falendi died (age 2)
**Age 26 (688 TR)** — He fell over in a public place in a way that was difficult to explain. He recovered quickly and moved on without making eye contact.
**Age 26 (688 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Delia Falendi. week 40 Delia Falendi born (female)
**Age 27 (689 TR)** — He stopped at a temple he had not intended to visit. He sat there for longer than made sense.
**Age 28 (690 TR)** — His name was called from the gallery and he wore the favour of victory.
**Age 28 (690 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment.
**Age 29 (691 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 29 (691 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Inda Falendi born (female)
**Age 30 (692 TR)** — The journey took him far enough from home that home became an abstraction he could think about rather than simply inhabit.
**Age 30 (692 TR)** — Delia Falendi died young — a child still. Delia Falendi died (age 4)
**Age 31 (693 TR)** — He kept company he should not have. Whether it meant anything, only he knew.
**Age 31 (693 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer.
**Age 32 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 32 (694 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning.

**Age 34 (696 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them.
**Age 34 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 35 (697 TR)** — He queued for a long time and reached the front just as the last of what he wanted ran out.
**Age 36 (698 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 37 (699 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories.
**Age 37 (699 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 38 (700 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the upper arm that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.

**Age 39 (701 TR)** — The establishment was well run and the Shenasene who presided had a gift for making a man feel that losing money was a cultivated pleasure. He came out ahead, which he suspected was not the usual outcome and which made him more cautious rather than less on subsequent visits.
**Age 40 (702 TR)** — Someone he had trusted turned his confidence against him — for coin, for advantage, or from spite he never understood. Betrayed by Ulric Olekar
**Age 41 (703 TR)** — He began to doubt the teachings he had followed without question.
**Age 41 (703 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 42 (704 TR)** — The engagement was the following morning and everyone who was paying attention knew the numbers. He found the Matakea near the supply wagons at dusk and said what he needed to say. He had not spoken to a priest since boyhood. It was not as difficult as he had expected. He slept better than he had in weeks.
**Age 42 (704 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 43 (705 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected.
**Age 43 (705 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.
**Age 44 (706 TR)** — He recognised something in the young and invested time in it. Mentored Davin Ulekar
**Age 44 (706 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 45 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 45 (707 TR)** — Inda Falendi was nearly grown when she died. Inda Falendi died (age 16)
**Age 46 (708 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; he left a man bloodied in the dirt.
**Age 47 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 48 (710 TR)** — The court found him guilty. What followed was swift and public.
**Age 48 (710 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later.
**Age 49 (711 TR)** — He saw a man die by violence — not on a battlefield, but in the ordinary world: a tavern, a street, a market square. It changed how he looked at strangers.
**Age 49 (711 TR)** — His wife lost the child early. Neither of them spoke much about it. week 4
**Age 50 (712 TR)** — He fell asleep somewhere he should not have — a meeting, a ceremony, a watch — and was woken ungently.
**Age 51 (713 TR)** — The years finally told. He moved more carefully, tired more quickly. The illness left a lasting mark: strength −1, stamina −1.

**Age 51 (713 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting.
**Age 51 (713 TR)** — His daughter Leira Falendi married Talar Arman. Leira Falendi married Talar Arman (peasant, age 16)
**Age 51 (713 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Leira Falendi's child born (male, Leira Falendi age 17)
**Age 52 (714 TR)** — A secret entrusted to him found its way to those who would pay for it.
**Age 53 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 53 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 54 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 54 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 54 (716 TR)** — Leira Falendi left home to make his own way. Leira Falendi left home (age 20)
**Age 55 (717 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved.
**Age 56 (718 TR)** — Determined to regain his strength, he threw himself into physical discipline.
**Age 57 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 57 (719 TR)** — His wife lost the child before its time. He did not know what to do with his grief. week 11
**Age 58 (720 TR)** — Shamed by his helplessness, he paid for lessons with a man-at-arms.
**Age 58 (720 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (680 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected. **Age 19 (681 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave. **Age 20 (682 TR)** — A tutor, a priest, or simply a talented neighbour opened music to him. **Age 21 (683 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Erith Falendi (noble, age 16) **Age 21 (683 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors. **Age 22 (684 TR)** — Through deed or patience, his name was spoken without scorn once more. **Age 22 (684 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it. **Age 23 (685 TR)** — He heard midnight bells from a temple he did not know, in a town he was passing through. The sound carried a long way. **Age 23 (685 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Ulda Falendi — survived. week 40 Ulda Falendi born (female) **Age 24 (686 TR)** — The dice or the wager — it did not matter which. He lost more than he had meant to stake and called it bad luck rather than bad judgment. **Age 24 (686 TR)** — His wife's labour was difficult. She was weakened for months, and the birth aged them both. **Age 25 (687 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Gorin Salendi (noble) **Age 25 (687 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years. **Age 25 (687 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Ulda Falendi died (age 2) **Age 26 (688 TR)** — He fell over in a public place in a way that was difficult to explain. He recovered quickly and moved on without making eye contact. **Age 26 (688 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Delia Falendi. week 40 Delia Falendi born (female) **Age 27 (689 TR)** — He stopped at a temple he had not intended to visit. He sat there for longer than made sense. **Age 28 (690 TR)** — His name was called from the gallery and he wore the favour of victory. **Age 28 (690 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment. **Age 29 (691 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation. **Age 29 (691 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Inda Falendi born (female) **Age 30 (692 TR)** — The journey took him far enough from home that home became an abstraction he could think about rather than simply inhabit. **Age 30 (692 TR)** — Delia Falendi died young — a child still. Delia Falendi died (age 4) **Age 31 (693 TR)** — He kept company he should not have. Whether it meant anything, only he knew. **Age 31 (693 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer. **Age 32 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 32 (694 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning. **Age 33 (695 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 34 (696 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them. **Age 34 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 35 (697 TR)** — He queued for a long time and reached the front just as the last of what he wanted ran out. **Age 36 (698 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues. **Age 37 (699 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories. **Age 37 (699 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it. **Age 38 (700 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the upper arm that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 39 (701 TR)** — The establishment was well run and the Shenasene who presided had a gift for making a man feel that losing money was a cultivated pleasure. He came out ahead, which he suspected was not the usual outcome and which made him more cautious rather than less on subsequent visits. **Age 40 (702 TR)** — Someone he had trusted turned his confidence against him — for coin, for advantage, or from spite he never understood. Betrayed by Ulric Olekar **Age 41 (703 TR)** — He began to doubt the teachings he had followed without question. **Age 41 (703 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 42 (704 TR)** — The engagement was the following morning and everyone who was paying attention knew the numbers. He found the Matakea near the supply wagons at dusk and said what he needed to say. He had not spoken to a priest since boyhood. It was not as difficult as he had expected. He slept better than he had in weeks. **Age 42 (704 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 43 (705 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected. **Age 43 (705 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered. **Age 44 (706 TR)** — He recognised something in the young and invested time in it. Mentored Davin Ulekar **Age 44 (706 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years. **Age 45 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 45 (707 TR)** — One of his children said something at the table — a mishearing, a confusion, a small profound mistake — that became the family's private language for years. **Age 45 (707 TR)** — Inda Falendi was nearly grown when she died. Inda Falendi died (age 16) **Age 46 (708 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; he left a man bloodied in the dirt. **Age 47 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 47 (709 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong. **Age 48 (710 TR)** — The court found him guilty. What followed was swift and public. **Age 48 (710 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later. **Age 49 (711 TR)** — He saw a man die by violence — not on a battlefield, but in the ordinary world: a tavern, a street, a market square. It changed how he looked at strangers. **Age 49 (711 TR)** — His wife lost the child early. Neither of them spoke much about it. week 4 **Age 50 (712 TR)** — He fell asleep somewhere he should not have — a meeting, a ceremony, a watch — and was woken ungently. **Age 51 (713 TR)** — The years finally told. He moved more carefully, tired more quickly. The illness left a lasting mark: strength −1, stamina −1. **Age 51 (713 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting. **Age 51 (713 TR)** — His daughter Leira Falendi married Talar Arman. Leira Falendi married Talar Arman (peasant, age 16) **Age 51 (713 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Leira Falendi's child born (male, Leira Falendi age 17) **Age 52 (714 TR)** — A secret entrusted to him found its way to those who would pay for it. **Age 53 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 53 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 54 (716 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Nora Chelkar **Age 54 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 54 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 54 (716 TR)** — Leira Falendi left home to make his own way. Leira Falendi left home (age 20) **Age 55 (717 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved. **Age 56 (718 TR)** — Determined to regain his strength, he threw himself into physical discipline. **Age 57 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 57 (719 TR)** — His wife lost the child before its time. He did not know what to do with his grief. week 11 **Age 58 (720 TR)** — Shamed by his helplessness, he paid for lessons with a man-at-arms. **Age 58 (720 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.
name: Fardan Falendi

narrative: **Age 18 (686 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him.
**Age 19 (687 TR)** — He could not decide between two options and tossed a coin. The coin showed one side and he immediately knew he had wanted the other.
**Age 20 (688 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the head or face that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 21 (689 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Sara Garekar (noble, age 18)
**Age 21 (689 TR)** — His wife lost the child before its time. He did not know what to do with his grief. week 16
**Age 22 (690 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 22 (690 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 23 (691 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Helra Oakbarrel (merchant)
**Age 24 (692 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.
**Age 25 (693 TR)** — He noticed, around the middle of the year, that things had been going well. He decided not to think about this too much.
**Age 25 (693 TR)** — The pregnancy ended before it had properly begun. He did not know what to say, so he said nothing useful. week 6
**Age 26 (694 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 26 (694 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 27 (695 TR)** — Through deed or patience, his name was spoken without scorn once more.

**Age 27 (695 TR)** — The child lived, but it was plain from the first days that something was different. He loved the child completely and did not know what the future held. week 40 — blind Celyn Garekar born (female, blind)
**Age 28 (696 TR)** — The death of a relative brought unexpected property or coin into his hands.
**Age 28 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 29 (697 TR)** — Court protocol required it. He submitted, and was surprised to find he enjoyed it.
**Age 29 (697 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 30 (698 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 31 (699 TR)** — It had not started as enmity. By the end, it was nothing else. Helra Oakbarrel became an enemy
**Age 31 (699 TR)** — He lay awake thinking about what would happen to the child after he was gone. There was no answer he could land on.
**Age 32 (700 TR)** — He was taken into a lord's household, a position of modest honour and real obligation.
**Age 32 (700 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment.
**Age 33 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 33 (701 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Seric Garekar came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Seric Garekar born (male)
**Age 34 (702 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 34 (702 TR)** — The child came at term and did not live. He had thought the danger past. week 38
**Age 34 (702 TR)** — Celyn Garekar died — the blind child he had worried over for years. Celyn Garekar died (age 7)
**Age 35 (703 TR)** — He heard footsteps behind him stop when his stopped. He turned. There was no one there.
**Age 35 (703 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.
**Age 36 (704 TR)** — The accolade fell on his shoulder. He rose a knight.
**Age 36 (704 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Uther Garekar born (male)
**Age 37 (705 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 37 (705 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it.
**Age 38 (706 TR)** — Conscience or ambition — perhaps both — moved him to give generously.
**Age 38 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 39 (707 TR)** — The letter arrived with no signature. It described, in precise and accurate detail, something he had done three years ago that he had believed no one knew. The terms were not unreasonable. That was almost the worst part — whoever had sent it understood exactly what the information was worth and had priced it accordingly.
**Age 39 (707 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years.
**Age 40 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 40 (708 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 41 (709 TR)** — He did not handle the loss publicly. He handled it alone, over a long time, in ways that left marks.
**Age 41 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 42 (710 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy.
**Age 42 (710 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (711 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 43 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 44 (712 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour.
**Age 44 (712 TR)** — She lost the child. He learned the limits of what he could do for her grief, which was most of it. week 11
**Age 45 (713 TR)** — A tutor, a priest, or simply a talented neighbour opened music to him.
**Age 45 (713 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Mora Garekar. week 40 Mora Garekar born (female)
**Age 46 (714 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Larith Haramin
**Age 46 (714 TR)** — Sara Garekar died. They had been married 25 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Sara Garekar died (approx. age 43)
**Age 47 (715 TR)** — He finally tried something he had refused for years and found it was fine. This was annoying.
**Age 48 (716 TR)** — A disagreement that should have been small became something permanent. He was not sure who was more at fault. Fell out with Larith Haramin
**Age 49 (717 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 50 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 50 (718 TR)** — One of his children said something at the table — a mishearing, a confusion, a small profound mistake — that became the family's private language for years.
**Age 51 (719 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour.
**Age 51 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 52 (720 TR)** — He almost missed it — a seam in the floor, a weight in the soil that wasn't right. What he found there changed his year, and perhaps more than his year.
**Age 52 (720 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.
**Age 52 (720 TR)** — His son Seric Garekar married Estra Garekar. Seric Garekar married Estra Garekar (noble, age 18)
**Age 52 (720 TR)** — Seric Garekar left home to make his own way. Seric Garekar left home (age 19)

narrative: **Age 18 (692 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave.
**Age 19 (693 TR)** — He sparred with a stranger on a wager and came off worse than expected. He practiced harder the following month.

**Age 20 (694 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it.
**Age 21 (695 TR)** — He said one thing, thought about it, and then said he had been wrong. This was noticed and not forgotten.
**Age 22 (696 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; he left a man bloodied in the dirt.
**Age 23 (697 TR)** — Court protocol required it. He submitted, and was surprised to find he enjoyed it.
**Age 24 (698 TR)** — He saw two men fighting in the street. He watched to see if he should intervene. He did not intervene. He thought about this later.
**Age 25 (699 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Elda Aldane (noble, age 21)
**Age 25 (699 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later.
**Age 26 (700 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.
**Age 26 (700 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved.
**Age 27 (701 TR)** — He kept company he should not have. Whether it meant anything, only he knew.
**Age 27 (701 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Tarath Aldane born (female)
**Age 28 (702 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.
**Age 28 (702 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.
**Age 28 (702 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Tarath Aldane died (age 1)
**Age 29 (703 TR)** — He told a small lie that had no purpose. The truth would have been fine. He did not know why he had said it.
**Age 29 (703 TR)** — The child came still. They had felt it move. He had no words for what followed. week 25
**Age 30 (704 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 30 (704 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Elith Aldane. week 40 Elith Aldane born (female)
**Age 31 (705 TR)** — He spent evenings on the tower roof, mapping stars and reading futures.
**Age 31 (705 TR)** — His child decided to do something — cook, chop, mend — without being asked. The result was memorable. He cleaned it up without making them feel bad.
**Age 32 (706 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Farath (clergy)
**Age 33 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 33 (707 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer.
**Age 34 (708 TR)** — When the coin was offered, he found reasons to look the other way.

**Age 34 (708 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting.
**Age 35 (709 TR)** — He began to doubt the teachings he had followed without question.
**Age 36 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 36 (710 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 37 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 38 (712 TR)** — He touched something — rough stone, worn leather, woven cloth — and found himself somewhere else entirely.
**Age 38 (712 TR)** — One of his children said something at the table — a mishearing, a confusion, a small profound mistake — that became the family's private language for years.

**Age 39 (713 TR)** — He left. There was someone else — or there had been for long enough that this felt inevitable. He packed what he could carry and did not look back, though he thought about it for the rest of his life.
**Age 39 (713 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Elara Aldane came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Elara Aldane born (female)
**Age 41 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 41 (715 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment.
**Age 42 (716 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 42 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (718 TR)** — He was ill enough that people began making arrangements. He did not die, which surprised several of them. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 44 (718 TR)** — He arranged for the child to be placed in another household — to learn, to serve, or simply to find a path he could not give them himself. Elith Aldane sent as ward (age 14)

**Age 45 (719 TR)** — It had begun as professional acquaintance. Somewhere in the course of years it became something else — not sentimentally, but in the way that trust accumulates. Durst Karamin became a close friend
**Age 45 (719 TR)** — One of his children overheard a conversation and formed a conclusion so wrong it took months to correct. By then the wrong version had spread to the neighbours.
**Age 46 (720 TR)** — What he had kept hidden was hidden no longer. The consequences arrived.
**Age 46 (720 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Torin Aldane came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Torin Aldane born (male)
**Age 46 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (692 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave. **Age 19 (693 TR)** — He sparred with a stranger on a wager and came off worse than expected. He practiced harder the following month. **Age 20 (694 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it. **Age 21 (695 TR)** — He said one thing, thought about it, and then said he had been wrong. This was noticed and not forgotten. **Age 22 (696 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; he left a man bloodied in the dirt. **Age 23 (697 TR)** — Court protocol required it. He submitted, and was surprised to find he enjoyed it. **Age 24 (698 TR)** — He saw two men fighting in the street. He watched to see if he should intervene. He did not intervene. He thought about this later. **Age 25 (699 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Elda Aldane (noble, age 21) **Age 25 (699 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later. **Age 26 (700 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected. **Age 26 (700 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved. **Age 27 (701 TR)** — He kept company he should not have. Whether it meant anything, only he knew. **Age 27 (701 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Tarath Aldane born (female) **Age 28 (702 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking. **Age 28 (702 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered. **Age 28 (702 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Tarath Aldane died (age 1) **Age 29 (703 TR)** — He told a small lie that had no purpose. The truth would have been fine. He did not know why he had said it. **Age 29 (703 TR)** — The child came still. They had felt it move. He had no words for what followed. week 25 **Age 30 (704 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him. **Age 30 (704 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Elith Aldane. week 40 Elith Aldane born (female) **Age 31 (705 TR)** — He spent evenings on the tower roof, mapping stars and reading futures. **Age 31 (705 TR)** — His child decided to do something — cook, chop, mend — without being asked. The result was memorable. He cleaned it up without making them feel bad. **Age 32 (706 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Farath (clergy) **Age 33 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 33 (707 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer. **Age 34 (708 TR)** — When the coin was offered, he found reasons to look the other way. **Age 34 (708 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting. **Age 35 (709 TR)** — He began to doubt the teachings he had followed without question. **Age 36 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 36 (710 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong. **Age 37 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 38 (712 TR)** — He touched something — rough stone, worn leather, woven cloth — and found himself somewhere else entirely. **Age 38 (712 TR)** — One of his children said something at the table — a mishearing, a confusion, a small profound mistake — that became the family's private language for years. **Age 39 (713 TR)** — He left. There was someone else — or there had been for long enough that this felt inevitable. He packed what he could carry and did not look back, though he thought about it for the rest of his life. **Age 39 (713 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Elara Aldane came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Elara Aldane born (female) **Age 41 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 41 (715 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment. **Age 42 (716 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike. **Age 42 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. He did not die, which surprised several of them. Somewhere in the course of years it became something else — not sentimentally, but in the way that trust accumulates. Durst Karamin became a close friend **Age 45 (719 TR)** — One of his children overheard a conversation and formed a conclusion so wrong it took months to correct. By then the wrong version had spread to the neighbours. **Age 46 (720 TR)** — What he had kept hidden was hidden no longer. The consequences arrived. **Age 46 (720 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Torin Aldane came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Torin Aldane born (male) **Age 46 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

narrative: **Age 18 (698 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Sirath Lalendi (noble, age 16)
**Age 18 (698 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 19 (699 TR)** — The disaster came without warning. What had taken years to build was gone in hours.
**Age 19 (699 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Dera Lalendi came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Dera Lalendi born (female)

**Age 20 (700 TR)** — The establishment was well run and the Shenasene who presided had a gift for making a man feel that losing money was a cultivated pleasure. He came out ahead, which he suspected was not the usual outcome and which made him more cautious rather than less on subsequent visits.
**Age 20 (700 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 21 (701 TR)** — He finally tried something he had refused for years and found it was fine. This was annoying.
**Age 22 (702 TR)** — Court protocol required it. He submitted, and was surprised to find he enjoyed it.
**Age 22 (702 TR)** — The child came still. They had felt it move. He had no words for what followed. week 30

**Age 23 (703 TR)** — A killing in the neighbourhood pulled him in — as witness, as kin, as someone who knew both sides. He had to choose where he stood, or at least appear to. Feud with Uther Nalendi
**Age 23 (703 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 24 (704 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.
**Age 24 (704 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Tera Lalendi born (female)
**Age 25 (705 TR)** — He watched a true expert do something he had done himself for years. He understood the difference immediately.
**Age 26 (706 TR)** — He recovered by degrees, marking progress in what he could do that he had not managed the week before.
**Age 26 (706 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Tera Lalendi died (age 2)

**Age 27 (707 TR)** — Another family's child came to live under his roof — to learn, to serve as a page, or simply as a pledge of alliance. He found himself responsible for someone else's son. Took in Ronan Elenshen as ward
**Age 27 (707 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved.

**Age 28 (708 TR)** — The letter arrived with no signature. It described, in precise and accurate detail, something he had done three years ago that he had believed no one knew. The terms were not unreasonable. That was almost the worst part — whoever had sent it understood exactly what the information was worth and had priced it accordingly.
**Age 28 (708 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.
**Age 29 (709 TR)** — He fell over in a public place in a way that was difficult to explain. He recovered quickly and moved on without making eye contact.
**Age 30 (710 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 30 (710 TR)** — One of his children overheard a conversation and formed a conclusion so wrong it took months to correct. By then the wrong version had spread to the neighbours.
**Age 31 (711 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 31 (711 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Yorin Lalendi. week 40 Yorin Lalendi born (male)
**Age 32 (712 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 33 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 33 (713 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 34 (714 TR)** — He kept waiting for the right moment to say something and the moment passed. The thing went unsaid.
**Age 35 (715 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 35 (715 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 36 (716 TR)** — He was caught in a small lie — not an important one, but it was noticed and the noticing was worse than the lie.
**Age 36 (716 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Edda Lalendi — survived. week 40 Edda Lalendi born (female)

**Age 37 (717 TR)** — His father spent the last months in the Peoni house on the river quarter. He visited twice a week. The place was clean and quiet and smelled of herbs and candle wax, and the sisters were matter-of-fact about dying in a way he found first shocking and then steadying. His father was comfortable. It was not nothing.
**Age 37 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (718 TR)** — A fever kept him bedridden for weeks; he emerged gaunt but alive. The illness caused serious permanent damage: stamina −3.
**Age 38 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 39 (719 TR)** — Determined to regain his strength, he threw himself into physical discipline.
**Age 39 (719 TR)** — She lost the child. He learned the limits of what he could do for her grief, which was most of it. week 18
**Age 39 (719 TR)** — His daughter Dera Lalendi married Seric Lalendi. Dera Lalendi married Seric Lalendi (noble, age 21)
**Age 40 (720 TR)** — Time had softened what once seemed unforgivable. They made their peace. Reconciled with Uther Nalendi

narrative: **Age 18 (704 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 19 (705 TR)** — The disaster came without warning. What had taken years to build was gone in hours.
**Age 20 (706 TR)** — He found someone who knew more than he did and paid in time and deference. Studied under Tovan Lalekar
**Age 21 (707 TR)** — He went somewhere that was muddy and came back in a state that was worse than he had anticipated.
**Age 22 (708 TR)** — He walked out early into thick mist. The world shrank to a few yards. He found he did not mind.
**Age 23 (709 TR)** — A finger was pointed at him — whether justly or not, his name was tainted.
**Age 24 (710 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 25 (711 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.
**Age 26 (712 TR)** — He spent evenings on the tower roof, mapping stars and reading futures.
**Age 27 (713 TR)** — His name was called from the gallery and he wore the favour of victory.
**Age 28 (714 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.

**Age 29 (715 TR)** — The engagement was the following morning and everyone who was paying attention knew the numbers. He found the Matakea near the supply wagons at dusk and said what he needed to say. He had not spoken to a priest since boyhood. It was not as difficult as he had expected. He slept better than he had in weeks.
**Age 30 (716 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 31 (717 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Rhoric Taramin (noble)

**Age 32 (718 TR)** — The Argent Order knight identified the man as Navehan, which explained several things about the last two weeks. He killed him in the tunnel mouth with a hand-axe in less than thirty seconds. The killing felt different from others he had done. Not better, exactly — but more contained, more purposeful. He did not examine this feeling very closely.
**Age 33 (719 TR)** — He overheard a conversation he was not meant to hear. He pretended otherwise and spent the next year wondering what to do with it.
**Age 34 (720 TR)** — The death of a relative brought unexpected property or coin into his hands.

name: Brand Elenshen

narrative: **Age 18 (710 TR)** — He stayed in an inn so bad he could not tell which noises were rats and which were other guests. He spoke of it for years.
**Age 19 (711 TR)** — He ended up in conversation at an inn that lasted until the fire died. He could not remember most of what was said but remembered the feeling of it.

**Age 20 (712 TR)** — He reached for it out of habit — the third hook by the door, the left-hand drawer, the box that used to sit on the shelf — and found nothing. The absence is more present to him than the object ever was.
**Age 21 (713 TR)** — He found someone who knew more than he did and paid in time and deference. Studied under Rorik Barend
**Age 22 (714 TR)** — The death of a relative brought unexpected property or coin into his hands.
**Age 23 (715 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Britta Nalendi (noble, age 16)
**Age 23 (715 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer.

**Age 24 (716 TR)** — He came home to the door off its hinges and the strongbox open. They had taken the silver plate, his wife's jewellery, and the tools he had been saving for three years. The worst of it was not the loss — it was the knowledge that someone had been through everything, had touched everything, had made themselves at home in his absence.
**Age 24 (716 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Hrolf Nalendi came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Hrolf Nalendi born (male)
**Age 25 (717 TR)** — He almost missed it — a seam in the floor, a weight in the soil that wasn't right. What he found there changed his year, and perhaps more than his year.
**Age 25 (717 TR)** — She lost the child. He learned the limits of what he could do for her grief, which was most of it. week 15
**Age 26 (718 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 27 (719 TR)** — A swarm of bees settled on his fence post. The beekeeper said it was good luck. He was not reassured.
**Age 28 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 28 (720 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
