He has prepared an appeal to the shire assize on the assumption it won't, which is not pessimism but preparation. The moot is a public proceeding and everyone in the room knows everyone else and their business. This is simultaneously the moot's strength and its limitation. **Age 52 (716 TR)** — His son Levan Faramin married Tala Alman. Levan Faramin married Tala Alman (unguilded, age 16) **Age 53 (717 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike. **Age 54 (718 TR)** — He recovered by degrees, marking progress in what he could do that he had not managed the week before. **Age 54 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 55 (719 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike. **Age 55 (719 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Levan Faramin's child born (male, Levan Faramin age 20) **Age 56 (720 TR)** — Shamed by his helplessness, he paid for lessons with a man-at-arms. **Age 30** *(class change)* — Lost livelihood to debt — engine routes urban→unguilded, rural→peasant [noble → unguilded]
name: Ranulf Faramin

narrative: **Age 18 (688 TR)** — A fox sat at the edge of the field and watched him work. Neither moved for a long time.
**Age 19 (689 TR)** — He received news that was good, weeks after it would have mattered. He was glad anyway.
**Age 20 (690 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 21 (691 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 22 (692 TR)** — A friend — soldier, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark. Beira Zaramin died
**Age 23 (693 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.
**Age 24 (694 TR)** — He lost a piece of equipment through his own inattention. He replaced it before anyone noticed. The cost annoyed him for months.
**Age 25 (695 TR)** — He found a letter in a borrowed book. It was clearly personal. He read only the first line before folding it closed. Then read the rest.
**Age 26 (696 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.

**Age 27 (697 TR)** — He made a vow at the chapel before the campaign: if he returned, he would give a year's service in Larani's name. He returned. He spent considerable time afterward determining what a year's service meant and whether he was fulfilling it adequately.
**Age 28 (698 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own.
**Age 29 (699 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the head or face that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 30 (700 TR)** — He gave a traveller confident directions and realised immediately after that he had them backwards. He could not find the man again.

**Age 31 (701 TR)** — Another family's child came to live under his roof — to learn, to serve as a page, or simply as a pledge of alliance. He found himself responsible for someone else's son. Took in Varath Aldane as ward

**Age 32 (702 TR)** — He had been waiting months for access to a particular merchant. The introduction came from an unexpected direction — a Halean Shenasene at a private gathering who mentioned his name to the right person with the right word attached. He never learned what the priestess received in return. He assumed the accounting was accurate.
**Age 33 (703 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Geda Naramin (noble, age 33)
**Age 34 (704 TR)** — Something moved him to tears he had not expected. He was alone, which was fortunate.
**Age 34 (704 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Elric Naramin came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Elric Naramin born (male)
**Age 35 (705 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 35 (705 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Andar Naramin came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Andar Naramin born (female)
**Age 36 (706 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them.
**Age 36 (706 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Rhoric Naramin came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Rhoric Naramin born (male)
**Age 37 (707 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 37 (707 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Edris Naramin — survived. week 40 Edris Naramin born (female)
**Age 38 (708 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Beira Salekar (noble)
**Age 38 (708 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years.
**Age 39 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 39 (709 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 41 (711 TR)** — A killing in the neighbourhood pulled him in — as witness, as kin, as someone who knew both sides. He had to choose where he stood, or at least appear to. Feud with Harlan Amble
**Age 41 (711 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved.
**Age 42 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 43 (713 TR)** — He was taken into a lord's household, a position of modest honour and real obligation.

**Age 44 (714 TR)** — The wound had gone bad by the time they brought him to the hospital sisters. He did not remember most of the following week. What he remembered afterward was the smell of herbs and the sound of someone — it might have been the same woman each time — sitting with him in the dark hours and not speaking. He recovered. He did not take this for granted.
**Age 44 (714 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning.
**Age 45 (715 TR)** — He completed a day's work and stood back and thought: yes, that is done properly.
**Age 46 (716 TR)** — A fever kept him bedridden for weeks; he emerged gaunt but alive. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 46 (716 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 46 (716 TR)** — Elric Naramin was nearly grown when he died. Elric Naramin died (age 12)
**Age 47 (717 TR)** — The accolade fell on his shoulder. He rose a knight.
**Age 48 (718 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 48 (718 TR)** — His wife lost the child before its time. He did not know what to do with his grief. week 12
**Age 49 (719 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 50 (720 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 50 (720 TR)** — His child decided to do something — cook, chop, mend — without being asked. The result was memorable. He cleaned it up without making them feel bad.

name: Morin Naramin

narrative: **Age 18 (694 TR)** — He was caught in a small lie — not an important one, but it was noticed and the noticing was worse than the lie.

**Age 19 (695 TR)** — The establishment was well run and the Shenasene who presided had a gift for making a man feel that losing money was a cultivated pleasure. He came out ahead, which he suspected was not the usual outcome and which made him more cautious rather than less on subsequent visits.
**Age 20 (696 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own.
**Age 21 (697 TR)** — The grief took longer than he had thought it would. He emerged from it changed in ways he could not fully name.
**Age 22 (698 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 23 (699 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Sera Ulendi (noble, age 16)
**Age 23 (699 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Elara Ulendi born (female)
**Age 24 (700 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 24 (700 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Kavan Ulendi. week 40 Kavan Ulendi born (male)
**Age 25 (701 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 25 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 26 (702 TR)** — The wound had gone bad by the time they brought him to the hospital sisters. He did not remember most of the following week. What he remembered afterward was the smell of herbs and the sound of someone — it might have been the same woman each time — sitting with him in the dark hours and not speaking. He recovered. He did not take this for granted.
**Age 27 (703 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 28 (704 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 28 (704 TR)** — Rodric Ulendi died young — a child still. Rodric Ulendi died (age 3)

**Age 29 (705 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it.
**Age 29 (705 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 30 (706 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 30 (706 TR)** — She lost the child. He learned the limits of what he could do for her grief, which was most of it. week 12
**Age 31 (707 TR)** — He kept company he should not have. Whether it meant anything, only he knew.
**Age 31 (707 TR)** — His wife lost the child before its time. He did not know what to do with his grief. week 13
**Age 32 (708 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.
**Age 32 (708 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it.
**Age 33 (709 TR)** — He ruined something he had spent a long time making. He did not speak for the rest of that day.
**Age 33 (709 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 34 (710 TR)** — The dice or the wager — it did not matter which. He lost more than he had meant to stake and called it bad luck rather than bad judgment.
**Age 34 (710 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 35 (711 TR)** — Figures were altered, documents forged — profit at the cost of someone else's ruin.
**Age 35 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 36 (712 TR)** — He set down what he remembered, for whoever might read it after him.
**Age 36 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 37 (713 TR)** — He marched under the sword-banner with a Matakea at the column's head and the Tirannon sung at dawn. It was harder than secular service — the discipline was absolute and the expectation of conduct was higher than any lord he had served — and there were moments he understood, without being able to explain, what the church was for.
**Age 38 (714 TR)** — He was taken into a lord's household, a position of modest honour and real obligation.
**Age 38 (714 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.
**Age 39 (715 TR)** — He was ill enough that people began making arrangements. He did not die, which surprised several of them. The illness left a lasting mark: stamina −1.
**Age 39 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 40 (716 TR)** — He overheard a conversation he was not meant to hear. He pretended otherwise and spent the next year wondering what to do with it.
**Age 40 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 40 (716 TR)** — His son Kavan Ulendi married Deva Horson. Kavan Ulendi married Deva Horson (peasant, age 16)
**Age 41 (717 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 41 (717 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 42 (718 TR)** — He saw two men fighting in the street. He watched to see if he should intervene. He did not intervene. He thought about this later.

**Age 42 (718 TR)** — She was ill for three weeks before she died. He had not understood until the second week what the illness was going to become. He had time to understand afterward — the empty space in the work, the meals, the bed — and he understands it still. He is doing what people do. He is continuing.
**Age 42 (718 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Kavan Ulendi's child born (male, Kavan Ulendi age 18)
**Age 42 (718 TR)** — Disa Ulendi died young — a child still. Disa Ulendi died (age 3)

**Age 43 (719 TR)** — The Matakea walked the line in the hour before dawn, touching each soldier's shoulder. He had heard the Alamirata a hundred times in temple. Here it meant something different — the same words, the same cadence, but spoken over men who might be dead by midday. He had not expected to find it comforting. He found it comforting.
**Age 43 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 43 (719 TR)** — His daughter Elara Ulendi married Deric Skinner. Elara Ulendi married Deric Skinner (artisan, age 21)
**Age 43 (719 TR)** — Kavan Ulendi died. He had not thought a parent could grieve an adult child as much as an infant. He had been wrong. Kavan Ulendi died (age 19)
**Age 44 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (720 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it.

narrative: **Age 18 (700 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it.
**Age 19 (701 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 20 (702 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.
**Age 21 (703 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.

**Age 22 (704 TR)** — He saw the face across the market — older, different clothes, but the same hands, the same set of the jaw. The man who had robbed him on the Tashal road three years ago was buying onions from a stall ten feet away. He stood very still for a moment and then made a decision about what kind of person he was.
**Age 23 (705 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Gorin Keenscale (merchant)
**Age 24 (706 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him.
**Age 25 (707 TR)** — He did something he had been told as a child to ward off bad luck. He was aware of how it looked. He did it anyway.
**Age 26 (708 TR)** — He tried something on a whim and found he was good at it. Not exceptional — just good. It surprised him.
**Age 27 (709 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 28 (710 TR)** — The death of a relative brought unexpected property or coin into his hands.
**Age 29 (711 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.
**Age 30 (712 TR)** — Someone he had trusted turned his confidence against him — for coin, for advantage, or from spite he never understood. Betrayed by Gorin Keenscale

**Age 31 (713 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy.

**Age 32 (714 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected.
**Age 33 (715 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Alara Garekar
**Age 34 (716 TR)** — He married. The arrangement made sense practically; whether it would make sense in other ways remained to be seen. Married Varath Edanel (noble, age 29)

**Age 35 (717 TR)** — Another family's child came to live under his roof — to learn, to serve as a page, or simply as a pledge of alliance. He found himself responsible for someone else's son. Took in Dorin Carveth as ward
**Age 35 (717 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Elora Edanel — survived. week 40 Elora Edanel born (female)
**Age 36 (718 TR)** — Determined to regain his strength, he threw himself into physical discipline.
**Age 37 (719 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. He paid the price.
**Age 37 (719 TR)** — The child came still. They had felt it move. He had no words for what followed. week 26

**Age 38 (720 TR)** — He was given a year and a Pagaelin informant — a slave, technically, who was paid in better food and occasional freedom of movement for cooperating. He took notes. His informant found the exercise baffling and said so, repeatedly. He kept taking notes.

narrative: **Age 18 (706 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.

**Age 19 (707 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected.
**Age 20 (708 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 21 (709 TR)** — He went somewhere that was muddy and came back in a state that was worse than he had anticipated.
**Age 22 (710 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.

**Age 23 (711 TR)** — With his second parent gone he was, in some sense he had not felt before, finally alone in the world. The house he grew up in would be sold or given over. He found he was not ready for that.
**Age 24 (712 TR)** — The death of a relative brought unexpected property or coin into his hands.
**Age 25 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 26 (714 TR)** — The Matakea walked the line in the hour before dawn, touching each soldier's shoulder. He had heard the Alamirata a hundred times in temple. Here it meant something different — the same words, the same cadence, but spoken over men who might be dead by midday. He had not expected to find it comforting. He found it comforting.
**Age 27 (715 TR)** — He said something he had intended only for one person at a volume that reached several others. He is still not certain how much they heard.
**Age 28 (716 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 29 (717 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour.
**Age 30 (718 TR)** — The court found him guilty. What followed was swift and public.
**Age 31 (719 TR)** — Through deed or patience, his name was spoken without scorn once more.
**Age 32 (720 TR)** — He found a coin in the road unlike any he had seen before. The merchant he showed it to grew oddly quiet.

**Age 21 (715 TR)** — He saw a man die by violence — not on a battlefield, but in the ordinary world: a tavern, a street, a market square. It changed how he looked at strangers.
**Age 22 (716 TR)** — The saddle became as natural as walking.
**Age 23 (717 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 24 (718 TR)** — Through deed or patience, his name was spoken without scorn once more.

**Age 26 (720 TR)** — The Matakea had gone down fifty yards out and the line was already pulling back. narrative: **Age 18 (666 TR)** — He borrowed something and never returned it. He still has it. He is aware of this.
**Age 19 (667 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the chest or torso that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 20 (668 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him.
**Age 21 (669 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 22 (670 TR)** — He saw light under a closed door late at night when no one should have been awake. He knocked. There was a reasonable explanation.
**Age 23 (671 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Seric Lalekar (noble)

**Age 24 (672 TR)** — He marched under the sword-banner with a Matakea at the column's head and the Tirannon sung at dawn. It was harder than secular service — the discipline was absolute and the expectation of conduct was higher than any lord he had served — and there were moments he understood, without being able to explain, what the church was for.
**Age 25 (673 TR)** — He read something — a text, a letter, a notice on a board — that he found himself returning to for years.
**Age 26 (674 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 27 (675 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Vara Valendi
**Age 28 (676 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.

**Age 29 (677 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected.
**Age 30 (678 TR)** — A tutor, a priest, or simply a talented neighbour opened music to him.
**Age 31 (679 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Inda Meleken (noble, age 31)
**Age 31 (679 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later.
**Age 32 (680 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 32 (680 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 33 (681 TR)** — The journey took him far enough from home that home became an abstraction he could think about rather than simply inhabit.
**Age 33 (681 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Roric Meleken. week 40 Roric Meleken born (male)

**Age 34 (682 TR)** — The wound had gone bad by the time they brought him to the hospital sisters. He did not remember most of the following week. What he remembered afterward was the smell of herbs and the sound of someone — it might have been the same woman each time — sitting with him in the dark hours and not speaking. He recovered. He did not take this for granted.
**Age 34 (682 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 35 (683 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them.
**Age 35 (683 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.
**Age 36 (684 TR)** — The disaster came without warning. What had taken years to build was gone in hours.
**Age 36 (684 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning.
**Age 37 (685 TR)** — The grief took longer than he had thought it would. He emerged from it changed in ways he could not fully name.
**Age 38 (686 TR)** — She was gone. A note, or nothing — it did not matter much which. He was left with the children and whatever explanations he could give the neighbours.
**Age 38 (686 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years.

**Age 39 (687 TR)** — He made a vow at the chapel before the campaign: if he returned, he would give a year's service in Larani's name. He returned. He spent considerable time afterward determining what a year's service meant and whether he was fulfilling it adequately.

**Age 39 (687 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting.
**Age 40 (688 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 40 (688 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 41 (689 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories.
**Age 41 (689 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment.
**Age 42 (690 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 42 (690 TR)** — His child decided to do something — cook, chop, mend — without being asked. The result was memorable. He cleaned it up without making them feel bad.
**Age 43 (691 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 44 (692 TR)** — It had not started as enmity. By the end, it was nothing else. Iric Kalekar became an enemy
**Age 44 (692 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 44 (692 TR)** — Roric Meleken died young — a child still. Roric Meleken died (age 11)
**Age 45 (693 TR)** — The injury was too deep. He would never move as freely again. A minor wound to the hip or groin that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.

**Age 46 (694 TR)** — The Matakea had gone down fifty yards out and the line was already pulling back. He did not make a decision, precisely — he moved before he had decided to move and the thinking happened afterward. He got her back to the line. She was dead. He had not known she was dead when he went for her. He has not entirely resolved how he feels about that.
**Age 46 (694 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 47 (695 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 48 (696 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A serious wound to the head or face. It healed, but left a permanent reduction in hearing (−1).

**Age 49 (697 TR)** — His lord had given an order he could not follow. Not would not — could not, in the sense that following it would have made him someone he did not want to be and could not have looked at in still water. He refused, formally, with witnesses. He was not punished. That surprised him more than the order had.
**Age 49 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 50 (698 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own.
**Age 50 (698 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. He could not say why she had never mentioned it.
**Age 51 (699 TR)** — He recognised something in the young and invested time in it. Mentored Narith Caldane
**Age 51 (699 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer.
**Age 51 (699 TR)** — His daughter Beira Meleken married Noral Meleken. Beira Meleken married Noral Meleken (noble, age 21)
**Age 52 (700 TR)** — He found someone who knew more than he did and paid in time and deference. Studied under Erlan Garekar
**Age 52 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 53 (701 TR)** — Determined to regain his strength, he threw himself into physical discipline.
**Age 53 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 54 (702 TR)** — The first real cold came and he realised summer was over. He stood in it for a moment before going inside.

**Age 54 (702 TR)** — She was ill for three weeks before she died. He had not understood until the second week what the illness was going to become. He had time to understand afterward — the empty space in the work, the meals, the bed — and he understands it still. He is doing what people do. He is continuing.
**Age 55 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 55 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 56 (704 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 7
**Age 57 (705 TR)** — He stood in the crowd and watched a man hang. It was meant to be a warning; he was not sure what lesson he took from it.
**Age 57 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 58 (706 TR)** — He used the last of something — a jar of something, a length of material, a substance he had kept for years — and found he was more sorry about it than he had expected.
**Age 58 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 58 (706 TR)** — Beira Meleken left home to make his own way. Beira Meleken left home (age 24)

**Age 70 (718 TR)** — His wife had a habit — a sound she made, a thing she did with her hands, a phrase she repeated — that had not bothered him for years and then, one day, bothered him enormously. He said nothing.
**Age 71 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 72 (720 TR)** — He came home to the door off its hinges and the strongbox open. They had taken the silver plate, his wife's jewellery, and the tools he had been saving for three years. narrativeText: **Age 18 (666 TR)** — He borrowed something and never returned it. He still has it. He is aware of this. **Age 19 (667 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the chest or torso that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 20 (668 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him. **Age 21 (669 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike. **Age 22 (670 TR)** — He saw light under a closed door late at night when no one should have been awake. He knocked. There was a reasonable explanation. **Age 23 (671 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Seric Lalekar (noble) **Age 24 (672 TR)** — He marched under the sword-banner with a Matakea at the column's head and the Tirannon sung at dawn. It was harder than secular service — the discipline was absolute and the expectation of conduct was higher than any lord he had served — and there were moments he understood, without being able to explain, what the church was for. **Age 25 (673 TR)** — He read something — a text, a letter, a notice on a board — that he found himself returning to for years. **Age 26 (674 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 27 (675 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Vara Valendi **Age 28 (676 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community. **Age 29 (677 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected. **Age 30 (678 TR)** — A tutor, a priest, or simply a talented neighbour opened music to him. **Age 31 (679 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Inda Meleken (noble, age 31) **Age 31 (679 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later. **Age 32 (680 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 32 (680 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong. **Age 33 (681 TR)** — The journey took him far enough from home that home became an abstraction he could think about rather than simply inhabit. **Age 33 (681 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Roric Meleken. week 40 Roric Meleken born (male) **Age 34 (682 TR)** — The wound had gone bad by the time they brought him to the hospital sisters. He did not remember most of the following week. What he remembered afterward was the smell of herbs and the sound of someone — it might have been the same woman each time — sitting with him in the dark hours and not speaking. He recovered. He did not take this for granted. **Age 34 (682 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 35 (683 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them. **Age 35 (683 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered. **Age 36 (684 TR)** — The disaster came without warning. What had taken years to build was gone in hours. **Age 36 (684 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning. **Age 37 (685 TR)** — The grief took longer than he had thought it would. He emerged from it changed in ways he could not fully name. **Age 38 (686 TR)** — She was gone. A note, or nothing — it did not matter much which. He was left with the children and whatever explanations he could give the neighbours. **Age 38 (686 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years. **Age 39 (687 TR)** — He made a vow at the chapel before the campaign: if he returned, he would give a year's service in Larani's name. He returned. He spent considerable time afterward determining what a year's service meant and whether he was fulfilling it adequately. **Age 39 (687 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting. **Age 40 (688 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues. **Age 40 (688 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 41 (689 TR)** — Lineage mattered. He learned to read shields and know their histories. **Age 41 (689 TR)** — His spouse produced a meal so badly wrong that it became a story they told for decades. He ate it without comment. **Age 42 (690 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 42 (690 TR)** — His child decided to do something — cook, chop, mend — without being asked. The result was memorable. He cleaned it up without making them feel bad. **Age 43 (691 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community. **Age 44 (692 TR)** — It had not started as enmity. By the end, it was nothing else. Iric Kalekar became an enemy **Age 44 (692 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it. **Age 44 (692 TR)** — Roric Meleken died young — a child still. Roric Meleken died (age 11) **Age 45 (693 TR)** — The injury was too deep. He would never move as freely again. A minor wound to the hip or groin that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 46 (694 TR)** — The Matakea had gone down fifty yards out and the line was already pulling back. He did not make a decision, precisely — he moved before he had decided to move and the thinking happened afterward. He got her back to the line. She was dead. He had not known she was dead when he went for her. He has not entirely resolved how he feels about that. **Age 46 (694 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors. **Age 47 (695 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together. **Age 48 (696 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A serious wound to the head or face. It healed, but left a permanent reduction in hearing (−1). **Age 49 (697 TR)** — His lord had given an order he could not follow. Not would not — could not, in the sense that following it would have made him someone he did not want to be and could not have looked at in still water. He refused, formally, with witnesses. He was not punished. That surprised him more than the order had. **Age 49 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 50 (698 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own. **Age 50 (698 TR)** — He discovered that his wife could do something he had never known — sing, draw, whistle like a bird, fix a wheel. name: Garan Meleken

narrative: **Age 18 (672 TR)** — The illness had stripped him down. He spent months rebuilding, finding it slower than he had expected.
**Age 20 (674 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own.
**Age 21 (675 TR)** — He tried something on a whim and found he was good at it. Not exceptional — just good. It surprised him.
**Age 22 (676 TR)** — The dice or the wager — it did not matter which. He lost more than he had meant to stake and called it bad luck rather than bad judgment.
**Age 23 (677 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave.
**Age 24 (678 TR)** — He said something that needed saying when everyone else was quiet. He would rather someone else had said it.
**Age 25 (679 TR)** — When the coin was offered, he found reasons to look the other way.
**Age 26 (680 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; he left a man bloodied in the dirt.
**Age 27 (681 TR)** — He was ill enough that people began making arrangements. He did not die, which surprised several of them. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 28 (682 TR)** — He recovered by degrees, marking progress in what he could do that he had not managed the week before.
**Age 29 (683 TR)** — A crow appeared every morning for a month on the same post. He began to look for it. One day it was gone.
**Age 30 (684 TR)** — Shamed by his helplessness, he paid for lessons with a man-at-arms.
**Age 31 (685 TR)** — A secret entrusted to him found its way to those who would pay for it.
**Age 32 (686 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 33 (687 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Tala Ralekar (noble, age 32)
**Age 33 (687 TR)** — He held the infant and understood something new about the world. He had not expected to feel it so immediately. week 40 Morath Ralekar born (female)

**Age 34 (688 TR)** — The establishment was well run and the Shenasene who presided had a gift for making a man feel that losing money was a cultivated pleasure. He came out ahead, which he suspected was not the usual outcome and which made him more cautious rather than less on subsequent visits.
**Age 34 (688 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 35 (689 TR)** — Court protocol required it. He submitted, and was surprised to find he enjoyed it.
**Age 35 (689 TR)** — His child decided to do something — cook, chop, mend — without being asked. The result was memorable. He cleaned it up without making them feel bad.
**Age 36 (690 TR)** — The years finally told. He moved more carefully, tired more quickly. The illness passed without lasting harm.

**Age 36 (690 TR)** — She was ill for three weeks before she died. He had not understood until the second week what the illness was going to become. He had time to understand afterward — the empty space in the work, the meals, the bed — and he understands it still. He is doing what people do. He is continuing.

**Age 37 (691 TR)** — The letter arrived with no signature. It described, in precise and accurate detail, something he had done three years ago that he had believed no one knew. The terms were not unreasonable. That was almost the worst part — whoever had sent it understood exactly what the information was worth and had priced it accordingly.
**Age 37 (691 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 38 (692 TR)** — One of his children brought home an animal no one had agreed to keep. Everyone pretended not to be fond of it. It stayed for years.
**Age 39 (693 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 39 (693 TR)** — Tala Ralekar died. They had been married 6 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Tala Ralekar died (approx. age 38)
**Age 40 (694 TR)** — The court found him guilty. What followed was swift and public.
**Age 40 (694 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning.
**Age 41 (695 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 41 (695 TR)** — One of his children asked him about death — calmly, as children do — and he found he did not have an answer he was happy with.

**Age 42 (696 TR)** — He reached the temple doors ahead of the men pursuing him and the priest let him in. Whether it was the law or simply that the priest had seen his face and made a judgment, he was never certain. He stayed three days on a pallet in the vestibule and left by the back when they told him it was clear.

**Age 43 (697 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it.
**Age 44 (698 TR)** — Figures were altered, documents forged — profit at the cost of someone else's ruin.
**Age 45 (699 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 46 (700 TR)** — He did not handle the loss publicly. He handled it alone, over a long time, in ways that left marks.
**Age 46 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 47 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 48 (702 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 49 (703 TR)** — The grief took longer than he had thought it would. He emerged from it changed in ways he could not fully name.
**Age 49 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 49 (703 TR)** — His daughter Morath Ralekar married Noral Ralekar. Morath Ralekar married Noral Ralekar (noble, age 20)
**Age 50 (704 TR)** — A stranger at the inn paid his bill without explanation and left before he could ask why.
**Age 50 (704 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Morath Ralekar's child born (female, Morath Ralekar age 17)
**Age 51 (705 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the shoulder that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 52 (706 TR)** — He set down what he remembered, for whoever might read it after him.

**Age 53 (707 TR)** — The Matakea had gone down fifty yards out and the line was already pulling back. He did not make a decision, precisely — he moved before he had decided to move and the thinking happened afterward. He got her back to the line. She was dead. He had not known she was dead when he went for her. He has not entirely resolved how he feels about that.
**Age 54 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 55 (709 TR)** — A finger was pointed at him — whether justly or not, his name was tainted.
**Age 56 (710 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour.

**Age 57 (711 TR)** — The Matakea walked the line in the hour before dawn, touching each soldier's shoulder. He had heard the Alamirata a hundred times in temple. Here it meant something different — the same words, the same cadence, but spoken over men who might be dead by midday. He had not expected to find it comforting. He found it comforting.
**Age 57 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 58 (712 TR)** — The Argent Order knight identified the man as Navehan, which explained several things about the last two weeks. He killed him in the tunnel mouth with a hand-axe in less than thirty seconds. The killing felt different from others he had done. Not better, exactly — but more contained, more purposeful. He did not examine this feeling very closely.
**Age 58 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 58 (712 TR)** — Morath Ralekar's infant died. He grieved for the child and for what he saw in Morath Ralekar's face. Morath Ralekar's child died (age 0)
**Age 59 (713 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 59 (713 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 60 (714 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 60 (714 TR)** — Morath Ralekar left home to make his own way. Morath Ralekar left home (age 27)
**Age 61 (715 TR)** — People started asking his opinion before acting. He noticed when this began. He did not comment on it.
**Age 61 (715 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it.
**Age 61 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 62 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 63 (717 TR)** — He looked around at a feast and realised he knew almost no one his own age. The men he had grown up with, trained with, quarrelled with — most of them were gone. He was still here. He was not sure what that meant.
**Age 64 (718 TR)** — He stopped fighting what he could not change. It was, strangely, freeing.

**Age 65 (719 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy.
**Age 66 (720 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the upper arm that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.

He stayed three days on a pallet in the vestibule and left by the back when they told him it was clear. **Age 43 (697 TR)** — The purse was gone before he reached for it. He had been in the market, in a crowd — a festival, probably, or a hanging-day when the press was thickest. He was annoyed rather than frightened. He knew the city well enough to know this was the cost of moving through it. **Age 44 (698 TR)** — Figures were altered, documents forged — profit at the cost of someone else's ruin. **Age 45 (699 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 46 (700 TR)** — He did not handle the loss publicly. He handled it alone, over a long time, in ways that left marks. **Age 46 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 47 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 48 (702 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 49 (703 TR)** — The grief took longer than he had thought it would. He emerged from it changed in ways he could not fully name. **Age 49 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 49 (703 TR)** — His daughter Morath Ralekar married Noral Ralekar. Morath Ralekar married Noral Ralekar (noble, age 20) **Age 50 (704 TR)** — A stranger at the inn paid his bill without explanation and left before he could ask why. **Age 50 (704 TR)** — He held the child of his child and understood that time had passed. Morath Ralekar's child born (female, Morath Ralekar age 17) **Age 51 (705 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the shoulder that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 52 (706 TR)** — He set down what he remembered, for whoever might read it after him. **Age 53 (707 TR)** — The Matakea had gone down fifty yards out and the line was already pulling back. He did not make a decision, precisely — he moved before he had decided to move and the thinking happened afterward. He got her back to the line. She was dead. He had not known she was dead when he went for her. He has not entirely resolved how he feels about that. **Age 54 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 55 (709 TR)** — A finger was pointed at him — whether justly or not, his name was tainted. **Age 56 (710 TR)** — Crisis drew him back to his faith with new fervour. **Age 57 (711 TR)** — The Matakea walked the line in the hour before dawn, touching each soldier's shoulder. He had heard the Alamirata a hundred times in temple. Here it meant something different — the same words, the same cadence, but spoken over men who might be dead by midday. He had not expected to find it comforting. He found it comforting. **Age 57 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 58 (712 TR)** — The Argent Order knight identified the man as Navehan, which explained several things about the last two weeks. He killed him in the tunnel mouth with a hand-axe in less than thirty seconds. The killing felt different from others he had done. Not better, exactly — but more contained, more purposeful. He did not examine this feeling very closely. **Age 58 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 58 (712 TR)** — Morath Ralekar's infant died. He grieved for the child and for what he saw in Morath Ralekar's face. Morath Ralekar's child died (age 0) **Age 59 (713 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him. **Age 59 (713 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years. **Age 60 (714 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation. **Age 60 (714 TR)** — Morath Ralekar left home to make his own way. Morath Ralekar left home (age 27) **Age 61 (715 TR)** — People started asking his opinion before acting. He noticed when this began. He did not comment on it. **Age 61 (715 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it. **Age 61 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 62 (716 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 63 (717 TR)** — He looked around at a feast and realised he knew almost no one his own age. The men he had grown up with, trained with, quarrelled with — most of them were gone. He was still here. He was not sure what that meant. **Age 64 (718 TR)** — He stopped fighting what he could not change. It was, strangely, freeing. **Age 65 (719 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy. **Age 66 (720 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the upper arm that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
name: Elric Ralekar

narrative: **Age 18 (678 TR)** — He marched under the sword-banner with a Matakea at the column's head and the Tirannon sung at dawn. It was harder than secular service — the discipline was absolute and the expectation of conduct was higher than any lord he had served — and there were moments he understood, without being able to explain, what the church was for.
**Age 19 (679 TR)** — He found someone who knew more than he did and paid in time and deference. Studied under Ranulf Keenscale
**Age 20 (680 TR)** — He stopped eating a particular food one day and would not explain it. No one pressed him.
**Age 21 (681 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them.
**Age 22 (682 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A minor wound to the chest or torso that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 23 (683 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the chest or torso that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.

**Age 24 (684 TR)** — The factor he had trusted with his accounts for four years had been selling information about his business to a competitor — or to someone who had hired the competitor as a front. He did not know where the thread ultimately led. He knew that every conversation in that office had been heard by someone else.
**Age 25 (685 TR)** — He overheard a conversation he was not meant to hear. He pretended otherwise and spent the next year wondering what to do with it.
**Age 26 (686 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Ista Palekar (noble, age 19)
**Age 26 (686 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 27 (687 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 27 (687 TR)** — The child came still. They had felt it move. He had no words for what followed. week 24
**Age 28 (688 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.
**Age 28 (688 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later.
**Age 29 (689 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 29 (689 TR)** — The child came, and with it something he had not prepared for — an attachment that rearranged everything else. Estra Palekar. week 40 Estra Palekar born (female)
**Age 30 (690 TR)** — He thought of exactly the right thing to say to someone, a week after the conversation was over.
**Age 31 (691 TR)** — Brigands relieved him of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 31 (691 TR)** — His child fell into the river. He went in after them. Everyone was fine. He did not stop shaking until the next morning.
**Age 32 (692 TR)** — Steel found him. The wound healed, after a fashion.
**Age 33 (693 TR)** — Steel found him. The wound healed, after a fashion.
**Age 34 (694 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 35 (695 TR)** — Through deed or patience, his name was spoken without scorn once more.
**Age 36 (696 TR)** — He got caught in the rain far from shelter. By the time he gave up and stood still, he was already soaked. He let it finish.
**Age 36 (696 TR)** — His wife's family connections opened new doors.
**Age 37 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (698 TR)** — The fever lasted three weeks. He spent the fourth week deciding what the experience had changed, if anything. The illness passed without lasting harm.

**Age 39 (699 TR)** — His lord had given an order he could not follow. Not would not — could not, in the sense that following it would have made him someone he did not want to be and could not have looked at in still water. He refused, formally, with witnesses. He was not punished. That surprised him more than the order had.

**Age 40 (700 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy.
**Age 41 (701 TR)** — Decades of reliability were acknowledged with words and perhaps a small grant.
**Age 42 (702 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 42 (702 TR)** — One of his children became convinced that something entirely harmless was dangerous and refused to go near it. The fear lasted two years.
**Age 42 (702 TR)** — Estra Palekar was nearly grown when she died. Estra Palekar died (age 13)
**Age 43 (703 TR)** — Conscience or ambition — perhaps both — moved him to give generously.
**Age 43 (703 TR)** — His wife began to snore. He discovered he could sleep through it after a few months. This became a thing he was quietly proud of.
**Age 44 (704 TR)** — He spent evenings on the tower roof, mapping stars and reading futures.
**Age 45 (705 TR)** — Younger officers sought him out before the engagement. He told them what he knew.
**Age 45 (705 TR)** — He and his spouse had an argument about something so minor he could not remember it two weeks later. The atmosphere lasted much longer.
**Age 46 (706 TR)** — He left the range of people who knew his name and spent several months in that condition, which suited him better than expected.

**Age 46 (706 TR)** — His wife found something he had kept from before they met — a letter, a token, an old drawing — and asked about it. He told her the truth. It was not interesting. She had expected it to be interesting.
**Age 47 (707 TR)** — He recovered by degrees, marking progress in what he could do that he had not managed the week before.
**Age 48 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 49 (709 TR)** — Something moved him to tears he had not expected. He was alone, which was fortunate.
**Age 49 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 50 (710 TR)** — They found her on the Kolisa bridge, which meant nothing — the Kolisa bridge was where bodies were found. No robbery. No obvious reason. The constable made notes and offered his condolences and the investigation ended in the usual way. He spent years trying to find a reason. He found several possibilities, none of them provable, all of them worse than ignorance.
**Age 51 (711 TR)** — He set out to do something and forgot what it was before he arrived. He stood there and tried to remember. He went back.

**Age 51 (711 TR)** — She was ill for three weeks before she died. He had not understood until the second week what the illness was going to become. He had time to understand afterward — the empty space in the work, the meals, the bed — and he understands it still. He is doing what people do. He is continuing.
**Age 52 (712 TR)** — A widowed sister, an aged parent, a cousin with nowhere else to go.
**Age 53 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 54 (714 TR)** — He was given a year and a Pagaelin informant — a slave, technically, who was paid in better food and occasional freedom of movement for cooperating. He took notes. His informant found the exercise baffling and said so, repeatedly. He kept taking notes.
**Age 54 (714 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 16
**Age 55 (715 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Vorin Casendi
**Age 55 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 57 (717 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected.
**Age 57 (717 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it.
**Age 58 (718 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 58 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 59 (719 TR)** — The grief took longer than he had thought it would. He emerged from it changed in ways he could not fully name.
**Age 59 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 60 (720 TR)** — Time had softened what once seemed unforgivable. They made their peace. Reconciled with Ulra Elenkar

name: Yorin Palekar

narrative: **Age 18 (684 TR)** — He rode out whenever duty permitted. The forest taught him patience and observation.
**Age 19 (685 TR)** — He saw a man die by violence — not on a battlefield, but in the ordinary world: a tavern, a street, a market square. It changed how he looked at strangers.
**Age 20 (686 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 21 (687 TR)** — He travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 22 (688 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Delia Valekar (noble)
**Age 23 (689 TR)** — He attempted something he was not qualified for and did it well. He put this down to luck and moved on.
**Age 24 (690 TR)** — It had not started as enmity. By the end, it was nothing else. Delia Valekar became an enemy

**Age 25 (691 TR)** — A killing in the neighbourhood pulled him in — as witness, as kin, as someone who knew both sides. He had to choose where he stood, or at least appear to. Feud with Hadric Marekar
**Age 26 (692 TR)** — A fever kept him bedridden for weeks; he emerged gaunt but alive. The illness left a lasting mark: stamina −1.

**Age 28 (694 TR)** — The factor he had trusted with his accounts for four years had been selling information about his business to a competitor — or to someone who had hired the competitor as a front. He did not know where the thread ultimately led. He knew that every conversation in that office had been heard by someone else.
**Age 29 (695 TR)** — Circumstance put him in front of an audience. He found he could hold them.
**Age 30 (696 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. He paid the price.
**Age 31 (697 TR)** — He recovered by degrees, marking progress in what he could do that he had not managed the week before.

**Age 32 (698 TR)** — He reached the temple doors ahead of the men pursuing him and the priest let him in. Whether it was the law or simply that the priest had seen his face and made a judgment, he was never certain. He stayed three days on a pallet in the vestibule and left by the back when they told him it was clear.
**Age 33 (699 TR)** — He took a wife, binding two households together. Married Marta Galenkar (noble, age 32)
**Age 33 (699 TR)** — The child came still. They had felt it move. He had no words for what followed. week 21
**Age 34 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 34 (700 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved.

**Age 35 (701 TR)** — He had been waiting months for access to a particular merchant. The introduction came from an unexpected direction — a Halean Shenasene at a private gathering who mentioned his name to the right person with the right word attached. He never learned what the priestess received in return. He assumed the accounting was accurate.
**Age 35 (701 TR)** — She lost the child. He learned the limits of what he could do for her grief, which was most of it. week 11
**Age 36 (702 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community.
**Age 36 (702 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 37 (703 TR)** — __colour__
**Age 37 (703 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong.
**Age 38 (704 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him.
**Age 38 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 39 (705 TR)** — The child had been burning for three days when the Peoni sister came. She said little, worked steadily, and left instructions that his wife followed exactly. By the end of the week the fever was gone. He made a donation to the temple that he could not really afford and did not regret it.
**Age 40 (706 TR)** — Someone he loved called for a priest at the end. It changed how he thought about faith.
**Age 40 (706 TR)** — Marta Galenkar died. They had been married 7 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Marta Galenkar died (approx. age 39)
**Age 41 (707 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 42 (708 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Ulan Dalendi
**Age 43 (709 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him.
**Age 44 (710 TR)** — He made a throw — of a stone, a knife, a horseshoe — that was perfect and that he was never able to repeat. There were witnesses.
**Age 45 (711 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 45 (711 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it.
**Age 46 (712 TR)** — He was taken into a lord's household, a position of modest honour and real obligation.
**Age 47 (713 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together.
**Age 48 (714 TR)** — He was ill enough that people began making arrangements. He did not die, which surprised several of them. The illness left a lasting mark: stamina −1.
**Age 49 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 50 (716 TR)** — He reached out, or they did. The thing between them was old enough to look smaller than it had. Reconciled with Ulan Dalendi
**Age 51 (717 TR)** — Someone was unexpectedly kind to him at a moment when he had not expected it. He was more affected by this than he showed.

**Age 52 (718 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy.
**Age 53 (719 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the abdomen that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 54 (720 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.

The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 34 (700 TR)** — Someone in the household was sick through the night. He stayed up. By morning they were better and he was exhausted and quietly relieved. **Age 35 (701 TR)** — He had been waiting months for access to a particular merchant. The introduction came from an unexpected direction — a Halean Shenasene at a private gathering who mentioned his name to the right person with the right word attached. He never learned what the priestess received in return. He assumed the accounting was accurate. **Age 35 (701 TR)** — She lost the child. He learned the limits of what he could do for her grief, which was most of it. week 11 **Age 36 (702 TR)** — An incident in public damaged his name in the community. **Age 36 (702 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 37 (703 TR)** — __colour__ **Age 37 (703 TR)** — He and his family took the wrong road on a journey together and were lost for half a day. Everyone had a different idea of where they had gone wrong. **Age 38 (704 TR)** — Word came that his brother was dead and had left no sons. The succession fell to him. **Age 38 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 39 (705 TR)** — The child had been burning for three days when the Peoni sister came. She said little, worked steadily, and left instructions that his wife followed exactly. By the end of the week the fever was gone. He made a donation to the temple that he could not really afford and did not regret it. **Age 40 (706 TR)** — Someone he loved called for a priest at the end. It changed how he thought about faith. **Age 40 (706 TR)** — Marta Galenkar died. They had been married 7 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Marta Galenkar died (approx. age 39) **Age 41 (707 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 42 (708 TR)** — Someone chose him as the person they could tell. He was not sure it was a compliment. Confided in Ulan Dalendi **Age 43 (709 TR)** — The accounts and obligations of the estate fell to him. **Age 44 (710 TR)** — He made a throw — of a stone, a knife, a horseshoe — that was perfect and that he was never able to repeat. There were witnesses. **Age 45 (711 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 45 (711 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it. **Age 46 (712 TR)** — He was taken into a lord's household, a position of modest honour and real obligation. **Age 47 (713 TR)** — His father was dead and the land was his. Grief and obligation arrived together. **Age 48 (714 TR)** — He was ill enough that people began making arrangements. He did not die, which surprised several of them. The illness left a lasting mark: stamina −1. **Age 49 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 50 (716 TR)** — He reached out, or they did. The thing between them was old enough to look smaller than it had. Reconciled with Ulan Dalendi **Age 51 (717 TR)** — Someone was unexpectedly kind to him at a moment when he had not expected it. He was more affected by this than he showed. **Age 52 (718 TR)** — Three priests, four acolytes, six guardsmen. They were told about the feigned conversions afterward, by the sole survivor who had been away for supplies. He had memorised the number. It was the kind of number that explained an entire policy. **Age 53 (719 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the abdomen that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 54 (720 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
name: Koral Galenkar

narrative: **Age 18 (690 TR)** — He rode at the lists and acquitted himself respectably.
**Age 19 (691 TR)** — A well-dressed stranger asked his full name, wrote nothing down, and moved on without explanation.
**Age 20 (692 TR)** — He struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Lara Walendi (noble)

**Age 21 (693 TR)** — He was taken. Ransom, eventually, freed him — but the months in captivity left a mark. A serious wound to the head or face. It healed, but left a permanent reduction in eyesight (−1).
**Age 22 (694 TR)** — He overheard a conversation he was not meant to hear. He pretended otherwise and spent the next year wondering what to do with it.
**Age 23 (695 TR)** — He stopped waiting for land that would never be his and made something of his own.
**Age 24 (696 TR)** — The journey took him far enough from home that home became an abstraction he could think about rather than simply inhabit.
**Age 25 (697 TR)** — He made something — a wooden figure, a knotted rope, a small carving — with no purpose in mind. He gave it away.
**Age 26 (698 TR)** — An accident or fight left him with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A serious wound to the head or face. It healed, but left a permanent reduction in eyesight (−1).
**Age 27 (699 TR)** — He woke up one morning certain of something he had been unable to decide. He never understood why sleep had solved it.
**Age 28 (700 TR)** — He sat through a ceremony so lengthy and so without incident that he lost count of how many times he had nearly fallen asleep.

**Age 29 (701 TR)** — A killing in the neighbourhood pulled him in — as witness, as kin, as someone who knew both sides. He had to choose where he stood, or at least appear to. Feud with Noric Maramin
**Age 30 (702 TR)** — A wife came into his household. He was not certain what he had expected, but he had not expected how much adjustment it required. Married Valith Ilenshen (noble, age 29)
**Age 30 (702 TR)** — His wife's labour was hard, and she was weakened for months. The child — Ronan Ilenshen — survived. week 40 Ronan Ilenshen born (male)
**Age 31 (703 TR)** — He found himself navigating allegiances at court that could make or ruin him.
**Age 32 (704 TR)** — Someone he knew — not a close friend, but a familiar face from the guild, the village, the neighbourhood — was killed. The world felt less safe after that.
**Age 32 (704 TR)** — One of his children overheard a conversation and formed a conclusion so wrong it took months to correct. By then the wrong version had spread to the neighbours.
**Age 32 (704 TR)** — The child did not survive its first years. He buried it and said nothing. Ronan Ilenshen died (age 2)
**Age 33 (705 TR)** — A friend — soldier, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark. Borik Darekar died
**Age 34 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 34 (706 TR)** — His wife fell ill — not the brief fever of a week, but something that lingered and frightened. She recovered, mostly. He did not forget the months of it.
**Age 35 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 36 (708 TR)** — He stood in the crowd and watched a man hang. It was meant to be a warning; he was not sure what lesson he took from it.
**Age 36 (708 TR)** — His wife bore him a child. Dalla Ilenshen came into the world, and the house changed around that fact. week 40 Dalla Ilenshen born (female)
**Age 37 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 37 (709 TR)** — Something happened — he could never explain what to outsiders — that had the whole household laughing until they couldn't stand. He still smiled at the memory years later.
**Age 38 (710 TR)** — He was certain of something and wrong. He argued his position at length before the evidence became impossible to ignore.
**Age 38 (710 TR)** — He walked somewhere with his spouse or a child and they said almost nothing the whole way. It was one of the better journeys he remembered.

**Age 39 (711 TR)** — The child had been burning for three days when the Peoni sister came. She said little, worked steadily, and left instructions that his wife followed exactly. By the end of the week the fever was gone. He made a donation to the temple that he could not really afford and did not regret it.
**Age 39 (711 TR)** — Valith Ilenshen died. They had been married 9 years. He had not thought enough about what that meant until it was over. Valith Ilenshen died (approx. age 38)
**Age 40 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 40 (712 TR)** — Dalla Ilenshen died young — a child still. Dalla Ilenshen died (age 4)

**Age 41 (713 TR)** — It had begun as professional acquaintance. Somewhere in the course of years it became something else — not sentimentally, but in the way that trust accumulates. Elith Keenscale became a close friend
**Age 42 (714 TR)** — Decades of reliability were acknowledged with words and perhaps a small grant.
**Age 43 (715 TR)** — A feud, a failed gamble at court, or a lord's displeasure — he was told to leave.
**Age 43 (715 TR)** — His parent told a story he had heard dozens of times as if it were new. He listened as if he had never heard it.

**Age 44 (716 TR)** — The engagement was the following morning and everyone who was paying attention knew the numbers. He found the Matakea near the supply wagons at dusk and said what he needed to say. He had not spoken to a priest since boyhood. It was not as difficult as he had expected. He slept better than he had in weeks.
**Age 45 (717 TR)** — Time and distance did what deeds could not. The old story lost its hold on people's tongues.
**Age 46 (718 TR)** — A secret entrusted to him found its way to those who would pay for it.
**Age 47 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 48 (720 TR)** — He reached out, or they did. The thing between them was old enough to look smaller than it had. Reconciled with Noric Maramin
