name: Lothar Ilenshen

narrative: **Age 18 (719 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Kelan Packworth (merchant, age 16)
**Age 19 (720 TR)** — She woke knowing what she needed to do. She had gone to sleep with no idea. She did not question it.
**Age 19 (720 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Delyn Packworth — lived. week 40 Delyn Packworth born (female)
narrativeText: **Age 18 (719 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Kelan Packworth (merchant, age 16) **Age 19 (720 TR)** — She woke knowing what she needed to do. She had gone to sleep with no idea. She did not question it. **Age 19 (720 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Delyn Packworth — lived. week 40 Delyn Packworth born (female)
name: Selda Goldcloak

narrative: **Age 18 (667 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Harlan Amberton (merchant, age 17)
**Age 18 (667 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Edris Amberton lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Edris Amberton born (female)
**Age 19 (668 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway.
**Age 19 (668 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 16
**Age 20 (669 TR)** — She called someone by their dead sibling's name. The silence that followed lasted a long time.
**Age 21 (670 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 21 (670 TR)** — He was among the men taken. She had no formal standing to object, no right to see him off. She stood at the edge of the road and watched the column go.
**Age 22 (671 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.

**Age 22 (671 TR)** — Something became funny in a way that built and built until everyone was helpless with it. She remembered it for the rest of her life without being able to say what exactly had been funny.
**Age 23 (672 TR)** — Moving goods past the toll-men was profitable work, if dangerous.
**Age 23 (672 TR)** — Another loss before it had become a child in any way others would recognise. She grieved it nonetheless. week 18

**Age 24 (673 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 24 (673 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 25 (674 TR)** — She was caught in a downpour with nowhere to go. She stood in it until it passed and felt, obscurely, better for it.
**Age 25 (674 TR)** — He came back. The relief she felt told her things about herself she had not quite admitted before.
**Age 26 (675 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community.

**Age 26 (675 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Eara Amberton. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Eara Amberton born (female)
**Age 27 (676 TR)** — She held back what she wanted to say for too long. By the time she was ready to say it, the occasion had closed.
**Age 28 (677 TR)** — She promised to do something she expected to regret. Keeping it cost her, and the cost was worth it.
**Age 28 (677 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself.
**Age 29 (678 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 30 (679 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 30 (679 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 31 (680 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.

**Age 31 (680 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Zara Amberton. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Zara Amberton born (female)
**Age 32 (681 TR)** — She did not hide it. The village noticed. She decided the village could form its own conclusions.
**Age 32 (681 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 33 (682 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 33 (682 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time. Zara Amberton died (age 2)
**Age 34 (683 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 34 (683 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 34 (683 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Edris Amberton's illegitimate child born (female, Edris Amberton age 16)

**Age 35 (684 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 35 (684 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Eara Amberton apprenticed (age 9)

**Age 36 (685 TR)** — He was found in the counting house. A professional killing — the constable said as much and then appeared to regret having said it. She spent the years afterward unwinding his affairs and finding, layer by layer, which of his activities had made him worth a contract.
**Age 36 (685 TR)** — Her daughter Edris Amberton married Levan Boring. Edris Amberton married Levan Boring (peasant, age 22)
**Age 36 (685 TR)** — Edris Amberton left to make her own way. Edris Amberton left home (age 18)
**Age 37 (686 TR)** — She pulled a door that pushed and then pushed it and it still did not open and someone eventually told her it was bolted.
**Age 37 (686 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (687 TR)** — She fought for what was hers through every channel available to her.
**Age 38 (687 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 39 (688 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 39 (688 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 40 (689 TR)** — She heard steps behind her that went quiet when she slowed. There was no one when she looked.
**Age 40 (689 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 41 (690 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that.
**Age 42 (691 TR)** — She was seized by laughter at the worst possible time. She managed to contain it. Barely.
**Age 42 (691 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 43 (692 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Malik Oakbarrel
**Age 43 (692 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 44 (693 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild.
**Age 44 (693 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.

**Age 45 (694 TR)** — The approach was made in person, by a woman she had never seen, who knew things about her that no one had any business knowing. The price was stated plainly. She paid it. She is still paying it. She has not yet determined who is behind the approach, which tells her they are very good at this.
**Age 45 (694 TR)** — Her child carried something home — a dog, a cat, a bird with a broken wing — that no one had said yes to. It lived in the house for a long time.
**Age 46 (695 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back.
**Age 46 (695 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 47 (696 TR)** — Her quality of work was recognised within the guild.
**Age 47 (696 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough.
**Age 48 (697 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time.
**Age 48 (697 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.
**Age 48 (697 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Edris Amberton's child born (female, Edris Amberton age 30)
**Age 49 (698 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Gara Barrelworth
**Age 49 (698 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 50 (699 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 50 (699 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.
**Age 50 (699 TR)** — Her daughter Eara Amberton married Yorin Edson. Eara Amberton married Yorin Edson (peasant, age 29)
**Age 51 (700 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 51 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 52 (701 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 52 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 52 (701 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 53 (702 TR)** — She went down in full view of several people. She stood up, straightened herself, and continued. No one mentioned it. She mentioned it to herself regularly.
**Age 54 (703 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 54 (703 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it.
**Age 55 (704 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 55 (704 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (705 TR)** — She put out the washing. It began to rain before she reached the door. She stood for a moment and then went back for it.
**Age 56 (705 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 57 (706 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Theric
**Age 57 (706 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 58 (707 TR)** — She ate something — plain food, ordinary occasion — that tasted better than anything she had eaten before or since.
**Age 58 (707 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 58 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 59 (708 TR)** — Someone had told the Matakea that she had been seen at a location associated with Navehan activity. She had not been there. The interview was thorough. She was believed, eventually. She spent some time afterward identifying who might have made the report and why.

**Age 60 (709 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
**Age 60 (709 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 60 (709 TR)** — Eara Amberton's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Eara Amberton's child died (age 2)
**Age 61 (710 TR)** — A fever kept her bedridden for weeks; she emerged gaunt but alive. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 61 (710 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 62 (711 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 62 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 63 (712 TR)** — She opened what she thought was the right door. It was not. She apologised to the people inside and withdrew.
**Age 63 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 63 (712 TR)** — Edris Amberton lost Tera Amberton. She watched her child grieve and did not know how to carry both sorrows at once. Edris Amberton's child died (age 11)
**Age 64 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 64 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 65 (714 TR)** — She found a stone that was the wrong shape for a stone. She kept it. It is still somewhere in the house.
**Age 65 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 66 (715 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 66 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 67 (716 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five.
**Age 67 (716 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.

**Age 68 (717 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere.
**Age 68 (717 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 69 (718 TR)** — She had confided things to the woman — things about her household, her finances, her husband's debts. She learned later, and by accident, that those confidences had been accurately relayed somewhere she would not have chosen. The friendship was real. The other work was also real. Both things were true at once.
**Age 69 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 70 (719 TR)** — The secret came out. She faced the judgment of everyone she knew.
**Age 71 (720 TR)** — Figures were altered, documents forged — profit at the cost of someone else's ruin.
**Age 71 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (667 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Harlan Amberton (merchant, age 17) **Age 18 (667 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Edris Amberton lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Edris Amberton born (female) **Age 19 (668 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway. **Age 19 (668 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 16 **Age 20 (669 TR)** — She called someone by their dead sibling's name. The silence that followed lasted a long time. **Age 21 (670 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her. **Age 21 (670 TR)** — He was among the men taken. She had no formal standing to object, no right to see him off. She stood at the edge of the road and watched the column go. **Age 22 (671 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it. **Age 22 (671 TR)** — Something became funny in a way that built and built until everyone was helpless with it. She remembered it for the rest of her life without being able to say what exactly had been funny. **Age 23 (672 TR)** — Moving goods past the toll-men was profitable work, if dangerous. **Age 23 (672 TR)** — Another loss before it had become a child in any way others would recognise. She grieved it nonetheless. week 18 **Age 24 (673 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice. **Age 24 (673 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took. **Age 25 (674 TR)** — She was caught in a downpour with nowhere to go. She stood in it until it passed and felt, obscurely, better for it. **Age 25 (674 TR)** — He came back. The relief she felt told her things about herself she had not quite admitted before. **Age 26 (675 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community. **Age 26 (675 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Eara Amberton. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Eara Amberton born (female) **Age 27 (676 TR)** — She held back what she wanted to say for too long. By the time she was ready to say it, the occasion had closed. **Age 28 (677 TR)** — She promised to do something she expected to regret. Keeping it cost her, and the cost was worth it. **Age 28 (677 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself. **Age 29 (678 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 30 (679 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 30 (679 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected. **Age 31 (680 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously. **Age 31 (680 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Zara Amberton. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Zara Amberton born (female) **Age 32 (681 TR)** — She did not hide it. The village noticed. She decided the village could form its own conclusions. **Age 32 (681 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 33 (682 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues. **Age 33 (682 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time. Zara Amberton died (age 2) **Age 34 (683 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it. **Age 34 (683 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 34 (683 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Edris Amberton's illegitimate child born (female, Edris Amberton age 16) **Age 35 (684 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 35 (684 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Eara Amberton apprenticed (age 9) **Age 36 (685 TR)** — He was found in the counting house. A professional killing — the constable said as much and then appeared to regret having said it. She spent the years afterward unwinding his affairs and finding, layer by layer, which of his activities had made him worth a contract. **Age 36 (685 TR)** — Her daughter Edris Amberton married Levan Boring. Edris Amberton married Levan Boring (peasant, age 22) **Age 36 (685 TR)** — Edris Amberton left to make her own way. Edris Amberton left home (age 18) **Age 37 (686 TR)** — She pulled a door that pushed and then pushed it and it still did not open and someone eventually told her it was bolted. **Age 37 (686 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 38 (687 TR)** — She fought for what was hers through every channel available to her. **Age 38 (687 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 39 (688 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete. **Age 39 (688 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 40 (689 TR)** — She heard steps behind her that went quiet when she slowed. There was no one when she looked. **Age 40 (689 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 41 (690 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that. **Age 42 (691 TR)** — She was seized by laughter at the worst possible time. She managed to contain it. Barely. **Age 42 (691 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days. **Age 43 (692 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Malik Oakbarrel **Age 43 (692 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 44 (693 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild. **Age 44 (693 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left. **Age 45 (694 TR)** — The approach was made in person, by a woman she had never seen, who knew things about her that no one had any business knowing. The price was stated plainly. She paid it. She is still paying it. She has not yet determined who is behind the approach, which tells her they are very good at this. **Age 45 (694 TR)** — Her child carried something home — a dog, a cat, a bird with a broken wing — that no one had said yes to. It lived in the house for a long time. **Age 46 (695 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back. **Age 46 (695 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 47 (696 TR)** — Her quality of work was recognised within the guild. **Age 47 (696 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough. **Age 48 (697 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time. **Age 48 (697 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day. **Age 48 (697 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Edris Amberton's child born (female, Edris Amberton age 30) **Age 49 (698 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Gara Barrelworth **Age 49 (698 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 50 (699 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 50 (699 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine. **Age 50 (699 TR)** — Her daughter Eara Amberton married Yorin Edson. Eara Amberton married Yorin Edson (peasant, age 29) **Age 51 (700 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet. **Age 51 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 52 (701 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest. **Age 52 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 52 (701 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 52 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 53 (702 TR)** — She went down in full view of several people. She stood up, straightened herself, and continued. No one mentioned it. She mentioned it to herself regularly. **Age 54 (703 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets. **Age 54 (703 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it. **Age 55 (704 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option. **Age 55 (704 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 56 (705 TR)** — She put out the washing. It began to rain before she reached the door. She stood for a moment and then went back for it. **Age 56 (705 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 57 (706 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Theric **Age 57 (706 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 58 (707 TR)** — She ate something — plain food, ordinary occasion — that tasted better than anything she had eaten before or since. **Age 58 (707 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it. **Age 58 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 59 (708 TR)** — Someone had told the Matakea that she had been seen at a location associated with Navehan activity. She had not been there. The interview was thorough. She was believed, eventually. She spent some time afterward identifying who might have made the report and why. **Age 60 (709 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them. **Age 60 (709 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 60 (709 TR)** — Eara Amberton's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Eara Amberton's child died (age 2) **Age 61 (710 TR)** — A fever kept her bedridden for weeks; she emerged gaunt but alive. The illness passed without lasting harm. **Age 61 (710 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 62 (711 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 62 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 63 (712 TR)** — She opened what she thought was the right door. It was not. She apologised to the people inside and withdrew. **Age 63 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 63 (712 TR)** — Edris Amberton lost Tera Amberton. She watched her child grieve and did not know how to carry both sorrows at once. Edris Amberton's child died (age 11) **Age 64 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 64 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 65 (714 TR)** — She found a stone that was the wrong shape for a stone. She kept it. It is still somewhere in the house. **Age 65 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 66 (715 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness. **Age 66 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 67 (716 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five. **Age 67 (716 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it. **Age 68 (717 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere. **Age 68 (717 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 69 (718 TR)** — She had confided things to the woman — things about her household, her finances, her husband's debts. She learned later, and by accident, that those confidences had been accurately relayed somewhere she would not have chosen. The friendship was real. The other work was also real. Both things were true at once. **Age 69 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 70 (719 TR)** — The secret came out. She faced the judgment of everyone she knew. **Age 71 (720 TR)** — Figures were altered, documents forged — profit at the cost of someone else's ruin. **Age 71 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
name: Annis Oakbarrel

narrative: **Age 18 (673 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 19 (674 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 20 (675 TR)** — She heard of something happening somewhere distant and found it affected her more than it should have.
**Age 21 (676 TR)** — She told a joke she had been holding for the right moment. The right moment arrived. The joke was everything she had hoped.

**Age 22 (677 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 23 (678 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 24 (679 TR)** — She dreamed something that she decided not to speak of. She has never spoken of it.
**Age 25 (680 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 26 (681 TR)** — She lost a small thing she had carried for years. She did not speak of it but felt its absence for a long time.
**Age 27 (682 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Balar Rivergate
**Age 28 (683 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Tavin Walendi (noble)
**Age 29 (684 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 30 (685 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 31 (686 TR)** — The road was impassable. She turned back and took the long way. She had been in a hurry. She arrived late and said nothing about why.
**Age 32 (687 TR)** — She found it by accident. Old coin, mostly, wrapped in rotted cloth. More than she had expected. She told no one for a week.
**Age 33 (688 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Vara Barrelworth
**Age 34 (689 TR)** — She attempted something beyond her usual skill and succeeded. She was pleased and a little suspicious of this.
**Age 35 (690 TR)** — A task she had estimated at two hours became an entire day. She adjusted.
**Age 36 (691 TR)** — She heard of the death through others — there had been no warning, no leave-taking. Balar Rivergate has died
**Age 37 (692 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 38 (693 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 39 (694 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 40 (695 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read.

**Age 41 (696 TR)** — She would not have known him except that he laughed — the same short laugh, the kind that means nothing is funny. The cutpurse from the Endakil festival. She watched him move through the crowd with professional ease and felt something cold and very precise in her chest that was not exactly anger.
**Age 42 (697 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 43 (698 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 44 (699 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 45 (700 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 46 (701 TR)** — She fought for what was hers through every channel available to her.
**Age 47 (702 TR)** — She could not sleep. She lay still and listened to the night and tried to work out what was wrong. Nothing was.
**Age 48 (703 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Estra Oakbarrel

**Age 49 (704 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five.
**Age 50 (705 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 51 (706 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.

**Age 52 (707 TR)** — She had heard the Halean houses were different from the common gaming tables. They were. The company was better, the wine was better, and the odds, on examination, were about the same. She enjoyed herself and understood that enjoyment was what she had paid for.

**Age 53 (708 TR)** — The franchise had come available when old Maret died with no heirs. She had moved faster than the men who assumed she would not, and had the backing of two syndics before the others had finished shaking their heads. The vote was closer than she liked. She did not let this show.

**Age 54 (709 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at.

**Age 55 (710 TR)** — Someone had told the Matakea that she had been seen at a location associated with Navehan activity. She had not been there. The interview was thorough. She was believed, eventually. She spent some time afterward identifying who might have made the report and why.
**Age 56 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 57 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 58 (713 TR)** — She brought the complaint herself, which is her right as a freeholder. The lord's steward had assessed her labour obligation incorrectly and she had the documents to show it. She presented them clearly and without elaboration. The lord looked at the documents and corrected the assessment. She thanked him in the appropriate form and left. The whole proceeding took perhaps half an hour. She noted afterward that the lord had been fair and prompt and had not required her to argue for something the documents already demonstrated. She has a higher opinion of this particular lord than she expected to have.

**Age 60 (715 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.
**Age 61 (716 TR)** — She let go of what she could no longer do and found she still had plenty left.
**Age 62 (717 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A serious wound to the chest or torso. It healed, but left a permanent reduction in stamina (−1).
**Age 63 (718 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 64 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 65 (720 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back.

name: Nora Dustmantle

narrative: **Age 18 (679 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 19 (680 TR)** — She was given in marriage, binding two households together. Married Ruald Coppergate (merchant, age 20)

**Age 19 (680 TR)** — She bore twins — Tala Coppergate and Breda Coppergate. The birth was hard and left her exhausted for weeks, but both children lived. twins Tala Coppergate and Breda Coppergate born (twins)
**Age 20 (681 TR)** — She sat for her portrait. The result was technically accomplished and she disliked it intensely.
**Age 20 (681 TR)** — She watched her parent simply get on with things during a hard stretch. She learned something about it that stayed with her.
**Age 21 (682 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 22 (683 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 22 (683 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.
**Age 23 (684 TR)** — She stood somewhere and saw something beautiful. She kept the image of it longer than most things.
**Age 23 (684 TR)** — Another loss before it had become a child in any way others would recognise. She grieved it nonetheless. week 11
**Age 24 (685 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 25 (686 TR)** — She made a small thing out of available scraps — braided, sewn, shaped. She kept it without being able to say why.
**Age 25 (686 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 26 (687 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 26 (687 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 29
**Age 27 (688 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. She paid the price.
**Age 27 (688 TR)** — She lost Tala Coppergate — still a child, not yet grown. Tala Coppergate died (age 8)
**Age 28 (689 TR)** — She stood in the ruins and decided what came next.
**Age 28 (689 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty.
**Age 29 (690 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 29 (690 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 30 (691 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 31 (692 TR)** — She was ill enough to frighten the household. She recovered, though the recovery took longer than the illness. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 31 (692 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.
**Age 32 (693 TR)** — She waved at someone who looked at her with polite puzzlement. The wave had been for someone behind her.
**Age 32 (693 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 33 (694 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 33 (694 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 34 (695 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the thigh that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 34 (695 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 35 (696 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
**Age 35 (696 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier.
**Age 36 (697 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 36 (697 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough.

**Age 37 (698 TR)** — She had expected the priest to favour the other party, who was male and of better standing. He did not. She received a settlement that was just if not generous and was left with a more complicated view of the church than she had arrived with.
**Age 37 (698 TR)** — Ruald Coppergate died after 18 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Ruald Coppergate died (approx. age 38)
**Age 37 (698 TR)** — Breda Coppergate left to make her own way. Breda Coppergate left home (age 18)
**Age 38 (699 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 39 (700 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Deva Goldcloak
**Age 39 (700 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back.
**Age 40 (701 TR)** — The ferry was not running. She waited. Another passenger talked at her. She listened politely for two hours.
**Age 40 (701 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 41 (702 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 42 (703 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline.
**Age 43 (704 TR)** — She said something quietly to one person and realised from the silence around her that it had carried.
**Age 43 (704 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (705 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done.
**Age 44 (705 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular.
**Age 45 (706 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 45 (706 TR)** — Her child produced a phrase so perfectly wrong that the whole household adopted it. Visitors were puzzled. The family didn't explain.
**Age 46 (707 TR)** — She fought for what was hers through every channel available to her.

**Age 47 (708 TR)** — The old woman had been slowly handing over more of the work for three years. When she died, the guild transfer was contested by a cousin who knew nothing of the trade. She fought it through the Mangai and won on the merits. She paid the inheritance fee in full rather than in instalments, which she had not quite been able to afford, and which she did not regret.
**Age 47 (708 TR)** — Her daughter Breda Coppergate married Brand Ironworth. Breda Coppergate married Brand Ironworth (merchant, age 30)
**Age 48 (709 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 48 (709 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Breda Coppergate's child born (female, Breda Coppergate age 29)

**Age 49 (710 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like.
**Age 50 (711 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 51 (712 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild.
**Age 52 (713 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.

**Age 53 (714 TR)** — The arrangement was explained to her in pleasant terms by a woman she had not seen before. A contribution to neighbourhood safety. The woman smiled throughout. She paid and told herself it was the cost of doing business in this city, which was true but not the whole truth.
**Age 53 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 54 (715 TR)** — She had heard the Halean houses were different from the common gaming tables. They were. The company was better, the wine was better, and the odds, on examination, were about the same. She enjoyed herself and understood that enjoyment was what she had paid for.
**Age 55 (716 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back.
**Age 56 (717 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection.

**Age 57 (718 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.
**Age 57 (718 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 58 (719 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark. Tala Cargoham died
**Age 58 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
*(Ages 59–59: no notable events)*
