name: Eara Tradesworth

narrative: **Age 18 (685 TR)** — She was given in marriage, binding two households together. Married Cavan Deepsack (merchant, age 16)

**Age 18 (685 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.
**Age 19 (686 TR)** — She worked alongside a artisan through a season, watching and helping.

**Age 20 (687 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like.
**Age 20 (687 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Wela Deepsack lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Wela Deepsack born (female)

**Age 21 (688 TR)** — The tithe demand had been adjusted upward without explanation. She queried it in writing, which was her right, and received a written response explaining that the adjustment was correct. She paid it. She did not forget it.
**Age 21 (688 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Balar Deepsack — lived. week 40 Balar Deepsack born (male)
**Age 22 (689 TR)** — She found a coin in an unexpected place — old, worn, and minted somewhere far away.
**Age 22 (689 TR)** — She bore a child. Kelan Deepsack came into the world healthy. week 40 Kelan Deepsack born (male)

**Age 23 (690 TR)** — The franchise had come available when old Maret died with no heirs. She had moved faster than the men who assumed she would not, and had the backing of two syndics before the others had finished shaking their heads. The vote was closer than she liked. She did not let this show.
**Age 23 (690 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 24 (691 TR)** — She misjudged the ground and arrived somewhere in a condition that was difficult to explain.
**Age 25 (692 TR)** — She borrowed something meaning to return it. That was years ago. It has become hers through inertia.

**Age 25 (692 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Arith Deepsack. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Arith Deepsack born (female)
**Age 26 (693 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Gerin Packworth (merchant)
**Age 26 (693 TR)** — She bore a child. Estra Deepsack came into the world healthy. week 40 Estra Deepsack born (female)
**Age 27 (694 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 27 (694 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough.
**Age 28 (695 TR)** — A woman she had never met told her to stay home that day. She went anyway. Nothing happened.
**Age 28 (695 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Orin Deepsack lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Orin Deepsack born (male)
**Age 29 (696 TR)** — She saw something that had turned violent and was not her affair. She noted it and walked on. She checked behind her twice.
**Age 29 (696 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically.
**Age 30 (697 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 30 (697 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 8
**Age 31 (698 TR)** — A bird nested in her window ledge. She watched the eggs hatch and the chicks go.
**Age 31 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 32 (699 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
**Age 32 (699 TR)** — Her child produced a phrase so perfectly wrong that the whole household adopted it. Visitors were puzzled. The family didn't explain.

**Age 33 (700 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 33 (700 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt.
**Age 34 (701 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.

**Age 34 (701 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Edris Deepsack. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Edris Deepsack born (female)
**Age 35 (702 TR)** — She caught sight of something that was already past by the time she looked. She mentioned it to no one.

**Age 35 (702 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Selyn Deepsack. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Selyn Deepsack born (female)

**Age 36 (703 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal.
**Age 36 (703 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular.

**Age 37 (704 TR)** — The arrangement was explained to her in pleasant terms by a woman she had not seen before. A contribution to neighbourhood safety. The woman smiled throughout. She paid and told herself it was the cost of doing business in this city, which was true but not the whole truth.
**Age 37 (704 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 37 (704 TR)** — Her daughter Wela Deepsack married Orin Plaintrader. Wela Deepsack married Orin Plaintrader (merchant, age 16)
**Age 38 (705 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 38 (705 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Orin Deepsack apprenticed (age 10)
**Age 38 (705 TR)** — Her son Kelan Deepsack married Helda Slater. Kelan Deepsack married Helda Slater (artisan, age 16)
**Age 39 (706 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Morak
**Age 39 (706 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 39 (706 TR)** — Wela Deepsack left to make her own way. Wela Deepsack left home (age 19)
**Age 40 (707 TR)** — She began writing — not because anyone asked, but because it needed doing.
**Age 40 (707 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 41 (708 TR)** — A woman she knew was killed — a neighbour, a trader she dealt with, someone whose face she knew. It was the kind of death that made everyone quieter for a while.
**Age 41 (708 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.
**Age 41 (708 TR)** — Her daughter Arith Deepsack married Kuric Brightcoin. Arith Deepsack married Kuric Brightcoin (merchant, age 16)
**Age 42 (709 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back.
**Age 42 (709 TR)** — Kelan Deepsack left to make her own way. Kelan Deepsack left home (age 20)
**Age 42 (709 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Arith Deepsack's child born (female, Arith Deepsack age 17)

**Age 43 (710 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five.
**Age 43 (710 TR)** — Her son Balar Deepsack married Arwen Longhaul. Balar Deepsack married Arwen Longhaul (merchant, age 22)
**Age 43 (710 TR)** — Arith Deepsack left to make her own way. Arith Deepsack left home (age 18)
**Age 44 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (711 TR)** — Balar Deepsack left to make her own way. Balar Deepsack left home (age 23)
**Age 44 (711 TR)** — Estra Deepsack left to make her own way. Estra Deepsack left home (age 18)
**Age 45 (712 TR)** — She found a hidden place in a wall or floor that someone had used before her. Whatever had been kept there was long gone.
**Age 45 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 45 (712 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 45 (712 TR)** — Her daughter Estra Deepsack married Ronan Packworth. Estra Deepsack married Ronan Packworth (merchant, age 22)
**Age 45 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 46 (713 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done.
**Age 47 (714 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 47 (714 TR)** — Her daughter Wela Deepsack remarried. Wela Deepsack remarried Fenric Keenscale (merchant, age 26)
**Age 47 (714 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 47 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 47 (714 TR)** — Her son Orin Deepsack married Elith Turner. Orin Deepsack married Elith Turner (artisan, age 16)

**Age 48 (715 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at.
**Age 48 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 48 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 49 (716 TR)** — Her horse stopped dead on a familiar road and would not be moved for an hour.
**Age 49 (716 TR)** — Her husband had a habit she had not noticed for years that she suddenly could not unhear. She said nothing. It passed.

**Age 50 (717 TR)** — She brought the complaint herself, which is her right as a freeholder. The lord's steward had assessed her labour obligation incorrectly and she had the documents to show it. She presented them clearly and without elaboration. The lord looked at the documents and corrected the assessment. She thanked him in the appropriate form and left. The whole proceeding took perhaps half an hour. She noted afterward that the lord had been fair and prompt and had not required her to argue for something the documents already demonstrated. She has a higher opinion of this particular lord than she expected to have.
**Age 50 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 50 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 50 (717 TR)** — Her daughter Edris Deepsack married Wulf Blacksmith. Edris Deepsack married Wulf Blacksmith (artisan, age 16)
**Age 50 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 51 (718 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 51 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 51 (718 TR)** — Kelan Deepsack died before she had expected it. The grief of outliving a child did not diminish with their age. Kelan Deepsack died (age 29)

**Age 52 (719 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere.
**Age 52 (719 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 52 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (719 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Selyn Deepsack's illegitimate child born (female, Selyn Deepsack age 17)
**Age 53 (720 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Kavan Westmarket
**Age 53 (720 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 53 (720 TR)** — Selyn Deepsack left to make her own way. Selyn Deepsack left home (age 18)
narrativeText: **Age 18 (685 TR)** — She was given in marriage, binding two households together. Married Cavan Deepsack (merchant, age 16) **Age 18 (685 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes. **Age 19 (686 TR)** — She worked alongside a artisan through a season, watching and helping. **Age 20 (687 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like. **Age 20 (687 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Wela Deepsack lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Wela Deepsack born (female) **Age 21 (688 TR)** — The tithe demand had been adjusted upward without explanation. She queried it in writing, which was her right, and received a written response explaining that the adjustment was correct. She paid it. She did not forget it. **Age 21 (688 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Balar Deepsack — lived. week 40 Balar Deepsack born (male) **Age 22 (689 TR)** — She found a coin in an unexpected place — old, worn, and minted somewhere far away. **Age 22 (689 TR)** — She bore a child. Kelan Deepsack came into the world healthy. week 40 Kelan Deepsack born (male) **Age 23 (690 TR)** — The franchise had come available when old Maret died with no heirs. She had moved faster than the men who assumed she would not, and had the backing of two syndics before the others had finished shaking their heads. The vote was closer than she liked. She did not let this show. **Age 23 (690 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected. **Age 24 (691 TR)** — She misjudged the ground and arrived somewhere in a condition that was difficult to explain. **Age 25 (692 TR)** — She borrowed something meaning to return it. That was years ago. It has become hers through inertia. **Age 25 (692 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Arith Deepsack. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Arith Deepsack born (female) **Age 26 (693 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Gerin Packworth (merchant) **Age 26 (693 TR)** — She bore a child. Estra Deepsack came into the world healthy. week 40 Estra Deepsack born (female) **Age 27 (694 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 27 (694 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough. **Age 28 (695 TR)** — A woman she had never met told her to stay home that day. She went anyway. Nothing happened. **Age 28 (695 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Orin Deepsack lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Orin Deepsack born (male) **Age 29 (696 TR)** — She saw something that had turned violent and was not her affair. She noted it and walked on. She checked behind her twice. **Age 29 (696 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically. **Age 30 (697 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike. **Age 30 (697 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 8 **Age 31 (698 TR)** — A bird nested in her window ledge. She watched the eggs hatch and the chicks go. **Age 31 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 32 (699 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them. **Age 32 (699 TR)** — Her child produced a phrase so perfectly wrong that the whole household adopted it. Visitors were puzzled. The family didn't explain. **Age 33 (700 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 33 (700 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt. **Age 34 (701 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets. **Age 34 (701 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Edris Deepsack. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Edris Deepsack born (female) **Age 35 (702 TR)** — She caught sight of something that was already past by the time she looked. She mentioned it to no one. **Age 35 (702 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Selyn Deepsack. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Selyn Deepsack born (female) **Age 36 (703 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal. **Age 36 (703 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular. **Age 37 (704 TR)** — The arrangement was explained to her in pleasant terms by a woman she had not seen before. A contribution to neighbourhood safety. The woman smiled throughout. She paid and told herself it was the cost of doing business in this city, which was true but not the whole truth. **Age 37 (704 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it. **Age 37 (704 TR)** — Her daughter Wela Deepsack married Orin Plaintrader. Wela Deepsack married Orin Plaintrader (merchant, age 16) **Age 38 (705 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down. **Age 38 (705 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Orin Deepsack apprenticed (age 10) **Age 38 (705 TR)** — Her son Kelan Deepsack married Helda Slater. Kelan Deepsack married Helda Slater (artisan, age 16) **Age 39 (706 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Morak **Age 39 (706 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 39 (706 TR)** — Wela Deepsack left to make her own way. Wela Deepsack left home (age 19) **Age 40 (707 TR)** — She began writing — not because anyone asked, but because it needed doing. **Age 40 (707 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it. **Age 41 (708 TR)** — A woman she knew was killed — a neighbour, a trader she dealt with, someone whose face she knew. It was the kind of death that made everyone quieter for a while. **Age 41 (708 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day. **Age 41 (708 TR)** — Her daughter Arith Deepsack married Kuric Brightcoin. Arith Deepsack married Kuric Brightcoin (merchant, age 16) **Age 42 (709 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back. **Age 42 (709 TR)** — Kelan Deepsack left to make her own way. Kelan Deepsack left home (age 20) **Age 42 (709 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Arith Deepsack's child born (female, Arith Deepsack age 17) **Age 43 (710 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five. **Age 43 (710 TR)** — Her son Balar Deepsack married Arwen Longhaul. Balar Deepsack married Arwen Longhaul (merchant, age 22) **Age 43 (710 TR)** — Arith Deepsack left to make her own way. Arith Deepsack left home (age 18) **Age 44 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 44 (711 TR)** — Balar Deepsack left to make her own way. Balar Deepsack left home (age 23) **Age 44 (711 TR)** — Estra Deepsack left to make her own way. Estra Deepsack left home (age 18) **Age 45 (712 TR)** — She found a hidden place in a wall or floor that someone had used before her. Whatever had been kept there was long gone. **Age 45 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 45 (712 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 45 (712 TR)** — Her daughter Estra Deepsack married Ronan Packworth. Estra Deepsack married Ronan Packworth (merchant, age 22) **Age 45 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 46 (713 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done. **Age 47 (714 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 47 (714 TR)** — Her daughter Wela Deepsack remarried. Wela Deepsack remarried Fenric Keenscale (merchant, age 26) **Age 47 (714 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 47 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 47 (714 TR)** — Her son Orin Deepsack married Elith Turner. Orin Deepsack married Elith Turner (artisan, age 16) **Age 48 (715 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at. **Age 48 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 48 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 49 (716 TR)** — Her horse stopped dead on a familiar road and would not be moved for an hour. **Age 49 (716 TR)** — Her husband had a habit she had not noticed for years that she suddenly could not unhear. She said nothing. It passed. **Age 50 (717 TR)** — She brought the complaint herself, which is her right as a freeholder. The lord's steward had assessed her labour obligation incorrectly and she had the documents to show it. She presented them clearly and without elaboration. The lord looked at the documents and corrected the assessment. She thanked him in the appropriate form and left. The whole proceeding took perhaps half an hour. She noted afterward that the lord had been fair and prompt and had not required her to argue for something the documents already demonstrated. She has a higher opinion of this particular lord than she expected to have. **Age 50 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 50 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 50 (717 TR)** — Her daughter Edris Deepsack married Wulf Blacksmith. Edris Deepsack married Wulf Blacksmith (artisan, age 16) **Age 50 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 51 (718 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her. **Age 51 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 51 (718 TR)** — Kelan Deepsack died before she had expected it. The grief of outliving a child did not diminish with their age. Kelan Deepsack died (age 29) **Age 52 (719 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere. **Age 52 (719 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days. **Age 52 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 52 (719 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Selyn Deepsack's illegitimate child born (female, Selyn Deepsack age 17) **Age 53 (720 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Kavan Westmarket **Age 53 (720 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 53 (720 TR)** — Selyn Deepsack left to make her own way. Selyn Deepsack left home (age 18)
name: Sera Wealthgate

narrative: **Age 18 (691 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 19 (692 TR)** — The journey required more of her than she had expected and gave back more than she had hoped.

**Age 20 (693 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Kela Dustmantle. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Kela Dustmantle born (female)
**Age 21 (694 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 22 (695 TR)** — Through deed or patience, her name was spoken without scorn once more.
**Age 22 (695 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough.
**Age 23 (696 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 24 (697 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community.
**Age 24 (697 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty.
**Age 25 (698 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Deva Coinsworth (merchant)
**Age 25 (698 TR)** — Her child carried something home — a dog, a cat, a bird with a broken wing — that no one had said yes to. It lived in the house for a long time.
**Age 26 (699 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 27 (700 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 28 (701 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 28 (701 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt.
**Age 29 (702 TR)** — She was ill enough to frighten the household. She recovered, though the recovery took longer than the illness. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 30 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 31 (704 TR)** — The franchise had come available when old Maret died with no heirs. She had moved faster than the men who assumed she would not, and had the backing of two syndics before the others had finished shaking their heads. The vote was closer than she liked. She did not let this show.
**Age 31 (704 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Kela Dustmantle apprenticed (age 11)
**Age 32 (705 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 33 (706 TR)** — She waited in line. She was next. The thing she had been waiting for was gone.
**Age 34 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 35 (708 TR)** — She lost a small thing she had carried for years. She did not speak of it but felt its absence for a long time.
**Age 36 (709 TR)** — She stood in the ruins and decided what came next.
**Age 36 (709 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 36 (709 TR)** — Her daughter Kela Dustmantle married Tovan Warman. Kela Dustmantle married Tovan Warman (peasant, age 16)
**Age 37 (710 TR)** — The creditors came. What she had built was gone.
**Age 37 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (711 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 39 (712 TR)** — The gossip thinned. People forgot, or found newer griefs to occupy them. She was not forgiven so much as left alone.
**Age 39 (712 TR)** — Her child produced a phrase so perfectly wrong that the whole household adopted it. Visitors were puzzled. The family didn't explain.
**Age 40 (713 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time.
**Age 40 (713 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 40 (713 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Kela Dustmantle's child born (male, Kela Dustmantle age 20)
**Age 41 (714 TR)** — She kept to it when most would have stopped. The discipline became its own reward.
**Age 43 (716 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 44 (717 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection.

**Age 45 (718 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.

**Age 46 (719 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like.
**Age 46 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 46 (719 TR)** — Kela Dustmantle died before she had expected it. The grief of outliving a child did not diminish with their age. Kela Dustmantle died (age 26)
**Age 47 (720 TR)** — She looked for something she had known all her life and found it had been taken down. She stood where it had been.
**Age 37** *(class change)* — Lost livelihood to debt — engine routes urban→unguilded, rural→peasant [merchant → unguilded]

name: Aldis Dustmantle

narrative: **Age 18 (697 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 19 (698 TR)** — She found it by accident. Old coin, mostly, wrapped in rotted cloth. More than she had expected. She told no one for a week.

**Age 21 (700 TR)** — The old woman had been slowly handing over more of the work for three years. When she died, the guild transfer was contested by a cousin who knew nothing of the trade. She fought it through the Mangai and won on the merits. She paid the inheritance fee in full rather than in instalments, which she had not quite been able to afford, and which she did not regret.
**Age 22 (701 TR)** — She worked alongside a artisan through a season, watching and helping.
**Age 23 (702 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Meric Longhaul (merchant, age 26)
**Age 23 (702 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.

**Age 24 (703 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five.
**Age 25 (704 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 25 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 26 (705 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 26 (705 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 32
**Age 27 (706 TR)** — She tried something she had always declined and found she liked it. She said nothing to the people who had told her to try it.
**Age 27 (706 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Helra Longhaul lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Helra Longhaul born (female)
**Age 27 (706 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time. Helra Longhaul died (age 0)
**Age 28 (707 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Voran Oakbarrel
**Age 29 (708 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 30 (709 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Trovan Wealthgate (merchant)

**Age 30 (709 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Rorik Longhaul. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Rorik Longhaul born (male)
**Age 31 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 31 (710 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride.
**Age 32 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 32 (711 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 33 (712 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Delyn Goldcloak
**Age 33 (712 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 34 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 34 (713 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Sara Longhaul — lived. week 40 Sara Longhaul born (female)
**Age 35 (714 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.

**Age 35 (714 TR)** — Something became funny in a way that built and built until everyone was helpless with it. She remembered it for the rest of her life without being able to say what exactly had been funny.

narrative: **Age 18 (703 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Tovan Barrelworth (merchant)
**Age 19 (704 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting.
**Age 20 (705 TR)** — She meant to glance at the rain and check on it. She stood there watching it for a long time.
**Age 21 (706 TR)** — A task she had estimated at two hours became an entire day. She adjusted.
**Age 22 (707 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Tovan Barrelworth

**Age 23 (708 TR)** — The tithe demand had been adjusted upward without explanation. She queried it in writing, which was her right, and received a written response explaining that the adjustment was correct. She paid it. She did not forget it.
**Age 24 (709 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Varen
**Age 25 (710 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Theric Tradesworth (merchant, age 25)
**Age 26 (711 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 26 (711 TR)** — The child was lost before it had become real to anyone else. She marked it privately. week 5
**Age 27 (712 TR)** — She had to sleep outside. She had not planned for it. It was not as bad as she had expected and she was not going to say so.
**Age 28 (713 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Wulf Plaintrader
**Age 28 (713 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.
**Age 29 (714 TR)** — She heard the midnight hour ring from somewhere she had never been. She lay awake listening to the silence after.
**Age 29 (714 TR)** — Theric Tradesworth died before they had found their way into a life together. She had barely learned what she was losing. Theric Tradesworth died (approx. age 29)

**Age 30 (715 TR)** — She would not have known him except that he laughed — the same short laugh, the kind that means nothing is funny. The cutpurse from the Endakil festival. She watched him move through the crowd with professional ease and felt something cold and very precise in her chest that was not exactly anger.
**Age 31 (716 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.

**Age 32 (717 TR)** — She had known him for years before she realised she would do something difficult for him if he asked. He had, she suspected, reached the same conclusion about her. Wulf Plaintrader became a close friend
**Age 33 (718 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 34 (719 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 35 (720 TR)** — The cat left a mouse on her pillow. She accepted this as the highest compliment.

narrative: **Age 18 (715 TR)** — She would not have known him except that he laughed — the same short laugh, the kind that means nothing is funny. The cutpurse from the Endakil festival. She watched him move through the crowd with professional ease and felt something cold and very precise in her chest that was not exactly anger.

**Age 19 (716 TR)** — She brought the complaint herself, which is her right as a freeholder. The lord's steward had assessed her labour obligation incorrectly and she had the documents to show it. She presented them clearly and without elaboration. The lord looked at the documents and corrected the assessment. She thanked him in the appropriate form and left. The whole proceeding took perhaps half an hour. She noted afterward that the lord had been fair and prompt and had not required her to argue for something the documents already demonstrated. She has a higher opinion of this particular lord than she expected to have.
**Age 20 (717 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 21 (718 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting.

**Age 22 (719 TR)** — The arrangement was explained to her in pleasant terms by a woman she had not seen before. A contribution to neighbourhood safety. The woman smiled throughout. She paid and told herself it was the cost of doing business in this city, which was true but not the whole truth.
**Age 23 (720 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.

name: Ida Goldcloak

narrative: **Age 18 (663 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Sara Richgate
**Age 19 (664 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient.

**Age 20 (665 TR)** — She was not carrying a chivalric weapon. She was carrying her employer's letter in a sealed case, and the seal looked like a heraldic device to a guard who did not know what a heraldic device actually looked like, and the guard decided that the case itself constituted bearing arms without right and detained her for half a day. The alderman who resolved it apologised. The guard was reprimanded. She filed a complaint with the guild that employed the guard service. She received a written acknowledgment. She carries the employer's letter unsealed now, which the employer finds aesthetically displeasing and which she considers a reasonable trade.
**Age 21 (666 TR)** — The gossip thinned. People forgot, or found newer griefs to occupy them. She was not forgiven so much as left alone.
**Age 22 (667 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 23 (668 TR)** — She helped a woman she had never seen carry a load up a hill. The woman tried to pay her; she refused.
**Age 24 (669 TR)** — The debt that had dogged her was finally cleared.
**Age 25 (670 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting.
**Age 26 (671 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 27 (672 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Ronan Broadsale
**Age 28 (673 TR)** — A snake crossed her path and the old women of the village argued for a week about what it meant.
**Age 29 (674 TR)** — She worked alongside a artisan through a season, watching and helping.

**Age 30 (675 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 31 (676 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 32 (677 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Meric Amberton (merchant, age 29)

**Age 32 (677 TR)** — The child lived. She could see from the beginning that they would need more — more care, more patience, more of everything she had. She did not hesitate. week 40 — simple Erlan Amberton born (male, simple-minded)
**Age 33 (678 TR)** — She stopped at a familiar door and stood on the threshold. She could not have said what made her hesitate. She went in.

**Age 33 (678 TR)** — She brought the child to a house of God where they would be cared for. She walked home alone and told herself it was the right thing. Some days she believed it. Erlan Amberton placed in religious house (age 1, simple)
**Age 33 (678 TR)** — She lost Erlan Amberton — the simple-minded child she had given so much to. Erlan Amberton died (age 1)

**Age 34 (679 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination.
**Age 35 (680 TR)** — She heard something through a door that changed how she thought about two people she knew. She said nothing.
**Age 36 (681 TR)** — She had to sleep outside. She had not planned for it. It was not as bad as she had expected and she was not going to say so.
**Age 37 (682 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Melyn Marketham (merchant)
**Age 37 (682 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 18

**Age 38 (683 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that.
**Age 38 (683 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.

**Age 39 (684 TR)** — The old woman had been slowly handing over more of the work for three years. When she died, the guild transfer was contested by a cousin who knew nothing of the trade. She fought it through the Mangai and won on the merits. She paid the inheritance fee in full rather than in instalments, which she had not quite been able to afford, and which she did not regret.
**Age 39 (684 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 40 (685 TR)** — She opened her door to someone who needed it.
**Age 40 (685 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 41 (686 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 41 (686 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.
**Age 42 (687 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild.

**Age 43 (688 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five.
**Age 44 (689 TR)** — She watched her fire die. She had somewhere to be. She stayed until the last coal went grey.
**Age 45 (690 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 45 (690 TR)** — Her husband had a habit she had not noticed for years that she suddenly could not unhear. She said nothing. It passed.

**Age 46 (691 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like.

**Age 46 (691 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.

**Age 47 (692 TR)** — She brought the complaint herself, which is her right as a freeholder. The lord's steward had assessed her labour obligation incorrectly and she had the documents to show it. She presented them clearly and without elaboration. The lord looked at the documents and corrected the assessment. She thanked him in the appropriate form and left. The whole proceeding took perhaps half an hour. She noted afterward that the lord had been fair and prompt and had not required her to argue for something the documents already demonstrated. She has a higher opinion of this particular lord than she expected to have.
**Age 47 (692 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 48 (693 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark. Ronan Marketham died
**Age 48 (693 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier.
**Age 49 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 50 (695 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 50 (695 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back.
**Age 51 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 51 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 52 (697 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 53 (698 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal.
**Age 53 (698 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 54 (699 TR)** — Her quality of work was recognised within the guild.
**Age 55 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 56 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 57 (702 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
**Age 57 (702 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it.
**Age 58 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 58 (703 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 59 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 59 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 60 (705 TR)** — The mark was permanent — ear, thumb, or cheek. She would carry it the rest of her life.
**Age 61 (706 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 62 (707 TR)** — She was among the crowd when the sentence was carried out. The silence that fell at the end was not what she expected.
**Age 62 (707 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 63 (708 TR)** — She had learned to be careful. This time she had not been careful enough. The look on the stranger's face told her everything.
**Age 64 (709 TR)** — She caught the smell of smoke in a room where nothing had been lit. She looked and found nothing.
**Age 64 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 65 (710 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 65 (710 TR)** — Meric Amberton died after 33 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Meric Amberton died (approx. age 62)

**Age 66 (711 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.
**Age 68 (713 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 68 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 69 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 70 (715 TR)** — She had managed the household, the accounts, the children's marriages, and the succession of a substantial property for four decades, and she stopped. What she found on the other side of it was time. She had forgotten what time felt like when it belonged to her. She is still learning.
**Age 70 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 71 (716 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the chest or torso that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 72 (717 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 72 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 73 (718 TR)** — The inn was so terrible she slept sitting up and was grateful for the experience as a story if nothing else.
**Age 74 (719 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient.
**Age 74 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 75 (720 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Elara Broadsale
narrativeText: **Age 18 (663 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Sara Richgate **Age 19 (664 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient. **Age 20 (665 TR)** — She was not carrying a chivalric weapon. She was carrying her employer's letter in a sealed case, and the seal looked like a heraldic device to a guard who did not know what a heraldic device actually looked like, and the guard decided that the case itself constituted bearing arms without right and detained her for half a day. The alderman who resolved it apologised. The guard was reprimanded. She filed a complaint with the guild that employed the guard service. She received a written acknowledgment. She carries the employer's letter unsealed now, which the employer finds aesthetically displeasing and which she considers a reasonable trade. **Age 21 (666 TR)** — The gossip thinned. People forgot, or found newer griefs to occupy them. She was not forgiven so much as left alone. **Age 22 (667 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets. **Age 23 (668 TR)** — She helped a woman she had never seen carry a load up a hill. The woman tried to pay her; she refused. **Age 24 (669 TR)** — The debt that had dogged her was finally cleared. **Age 25 (670 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting. **Age 26 (671 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 27 (672 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Ronan Broadsale **Age 28 (673 TR)** — A snake crossed her path and the old women of the village argued for a week about what it meant. **Age 29 (674 TR)** — She worked alongside a artisan through a season, watching and helping. **Age 30 (675 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness. **Age 31 (676 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 32 (677 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Meric Amberton (merchant, age 29) **Age 32 (677 TR)** — The child lived. She could see from the beginning that they would need more — more care, more patience, more of everything she had. She did not hesitate. week 40 — simple Erlan Amberton born (male, simple-minded) **Age 33 (678 TR)** — She stopped at a familiar door and stood on the threshold. She could not have said what made her hesitate. She went in. **Age 33 (678 TR)** — She brought the child to a house of God where they would be cared for. She walked home alone and told herself it was the right thing. Some days she believed it. Erlan Amberton placed in religious house (age 1, simple) **Age 33 (678 TR)** — She lost Erlan Amberton — the simple-minded child she had given so much to. Erlan Amberton died (age 1) **Age 34 (679 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination. **Age 35 (680 TR)** — She heard something through a door that changed how she thought about two people she knew. She said nothing. **Age 36 (681 TR)** — She had to sleep outside. She had not planned for it. It was not as bad as she had expected and she was not going to say so. **Age 37 (682 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Melyn Marketham (merchant) **Age 37 (682 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 18 **Age 38 (683 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that. **Age 38 (683 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day. **Age 39 (684 TR)** — The old woman had been slowly handing over more of the work for three years. When she died, the guild transfer was contested by a cousin who knew nothing of the trade. She fought it through the Mangai and won on the merits. She paid the inheritance fee in full rather than in instalments, which she had not quite been able to afford, and which she did not regret. **Age 39 (684 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 40 (685 TR)** — She opened her door to someone who needed it. **Age 40 (685 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it. **Age 41 (686 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 41 (686 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine. **Age 42 (687 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild. **Age 43 (688 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five. **Age 44 (689 TR)** — She watched her fire die. She had somewhere to be. She stayed until the last coal went grey. **Age 45 (690 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 45 (690 TR)** — Her husband had a habit she had not noticed for years that she suddenly could not unhear. She said nothing. It passed. **Age 46 (691 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like. **Age 46 (691 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes. **Age 47 (692 TR)** — She brought the complaint herself, which is her right as a freeholder. The lord's steward had assessed her labour obligation incorrectly and she had the documents to show it. She presented them clearly and without elaboration. The lord looked at the documents and corrected the assessment. She thanked him in the appropriate form and left. The whole proceeding took perhaps half an hour. She noted afterward that the lord had been fair and prompt and had not required her to argue for something the documents already demonstrated. She has a higher opinion of this particular lord than she expected to have. **Age 47 (692 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took. **Age 48 (693 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark. Ronan Marketham died **Age 48 (693 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier. **Age 49 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 50 (695 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete. **Age 50 (695 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back. **Age 51 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 51 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 52 (697 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 53 (698 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal. **Age 53 (698 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 54 (699 TR)** — Her quality of work was recognised within the guild. **Age 55 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 56 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 57 (702 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them. **Age 57 (702 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it. **Age 58 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 58 (703 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 59 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 59 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 60 (705 TR)** — The mark was permanent — ear, thumb, or cheek. She would carry it the rest of her life. **Age 61 (706 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 62 (707 TR)** — She was among the crowd when the sentence was carried out. The silence that fell at the end was not what she expected. **Age 62 (707 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days. **Age 63 (708 TR)** — She had learned to be careful. This time she had not been careful enough. The look on the stranger's face told her everything. **Age 64 (709 TR)** — She caught the smell of smoke in a room where nothing had been lit. She looked and found nothing. **Age 64 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 65 (710 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 65 (710 TR)** — Meric Amberton died after 33 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it. **Age 68 (713 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it. **Age 68 (713 TR)** — He endured. She is still learning. **Age 70 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 71 (716 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly.
