The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 72 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 73 (718 TR)** — The inn was so terrible she slept sitting up and was grateful for the experience as a story if nothing else. **Age 74 (719 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient. **Age 74 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 75 (720 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Elara Broadsale
name: Sira Goodbarrel

narrative: **Age 18 (669 TR)** — The same dream visited her three nights. She woke each time unsettled and unable to say why.
**Age 19 (670 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting.
**Age 20 (671 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 21 (672 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Malik Strongpurse (merchant, age 22)
**Age 21 (672 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier.
**Age 22 (673 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question.
**Age 23 (674 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.

**Age 23 (674 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Perin Strongpurse. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Perin Strongpurse born (male)
**Age 24 (675 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway.
**Age 24 (675 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 25 (676 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; she left someone badly hurt.
**Age 25 (676 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back.
**Age 26 (677 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.
**Age 26 (677 TR)** — He was among the men taken. She had no formal standing to object, no right to see him off. She stood at the edge of the road and watched the column go.
**Age 27 (678 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline.
**Age 28 (679 TR)** — She attempted something she expected to fail at and did it adequately on the first try.
**Age 28 (679 TR)** — He came back. The relief she felt told her things about herself she had not quite admitted before.

**Age 29 (680 TR)** — The Matakea heard her story and asked no questions about whether it was complete. She was given a room in the lay quarters and told she could stay until she decided what she was doing. That was two weeks, and during that time no one came for her.
**Age 30 (681 TR)** — She caught sight of something that was already past by the time she looked. She mentioned it to no one.
**Age 30 (681 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 31 (682 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 32 (683 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Melyn Coinsworth (merchant)

**Age 32 (683 TR)** — Something became funny in a way that built and built until everyone was helpless with it. She remembered it for the rest of her life without being able to say what exactly had been funny.
**Age 33 (684 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 34 (685 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 34 (685 TR)** — She watched her parent simply get on with things during a hard stretch. She learned something about it that stayed with her.
**Age 35 (686 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community.
**Age 35 (686 TR)** — Her child produced a phrase so perfectly wrong that the whole household adopted it. Visitors were puzzled. The family didn't explain.
**Age 36 (687 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Yara Dustmantle
**Age 37 (688 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 37 (688 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty.
**Age 38 (689 TR)** — She gave directions with certainty and then spent an hour hoping the person had figured out the mistake.

**Age 38 (689 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.
**Age 39 (690 TR)** — She heard steps behind her that went quiet when she slowed. There was no one when she looked.
**Age 40 (691 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 5
**Age 41 (692 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 41 (692 TR)** — The child came too still. She had no husband to share the weight of it. week 34
**Age 42 (693 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.
**Age 42 (693 TR)** — Her son Perin Strongpurse married Rorath Doring. Perin Strongpurse married Rorath Doring (peasant, age 19)
**Age 43 (694 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (694 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 44 (695 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 45 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 45 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 46 (697 TR)** — A snake crossed her path and the old women of the village argued for a week about what it meant.
**Age 46 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 47 (698 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal.
**Age 47 (698 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Perin Strongpurse's child born (female, Perin Strongpurse age 24)

**Age 48 (699 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.

**Age 49 (700 TR)** — She had heard the Halean houses were different from the common gaming tables. They were. The company was better, the wine was better, and the odds, on examination, were about the same. She enjoyed herself and understood that enjoyment was what she had paid for.
**Age 49 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 49 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 49 (700 TR)** — Perin Strongpurse left to make her own way. Perin Strongpurse left home (age 26)
**Age 50 (701 TR)** — She was pulled into a quarrel between two people she liked equally. She attempted fairness. This satisfied no one.
**Age 50 (701 TR)** — Her husband had a habit she had not noticed for years that she suddenly could not unhear. She said nothing. It passed.
**Age 51 (702 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 51 (702 TR)** — Her son Perin Strongpurse remarried. Perin Strongpurse remarried Lara Plaintrader (merchant, age 28)
**Age 51 (702 TR)** — Perin Strongpurse's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Perin Strongpurse's child died (age 2)
**Age 52 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 53 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 53 (704 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 54 (705 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 54 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 55 (706 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time.
**Age 55 (706 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically.
**Age 56 (707 TR)** — She felt the change in the air before she saw it and understood that the year was turning. She had not been ready.
**Age 56 (707 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 57 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 57 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 58 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 58 (709 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride.
**Age 59 (710 TR)** — The court found her guilty. What followed was swift and public.
**Age 59 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 60 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 60 (711 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough.
**Age 61 (712 TR)** — She became the person others consulted. This had been happening for years; it was only recently that she recognised it as a pattern.
**Age 61 (712 TR)** — Malik Strongpurse died after 40 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Malik Strongpurse died (approx. age 62)

**Age 62 (713 TR)** — Her daughter's marriage was arranged and the merchet was due to the lord before the wedding. She sent the payment with the reeve and received the lord's written approval in return. The written approval is a document she keeps with the other land documents, because written approval means the marriage cannot later be contested on grounds of non-compliance with the lord's right. She is practical about the lord's rights. They exist. She pays them correctly and keeps the records.
**Age 62 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 63 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 64 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 65 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 65 (716 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 66 (717 TR)** — She had trusted this person. That trust had been a tool someone else picked up and used. Betrayed by Dera Longmarket
**Age 66 (717 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it.
**Age 67 (718 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 67 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 68 (719 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 68 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 69 (720 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.

She had no husband to share the weight of it. week 34 **Age 42 (693 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would. **Age 42 (693 TR)** — Her son Perin Strongpurse married Rorath Doring. Perin Strongpurse married Rorath Doring (peasant, age 19) **Age 43 (694 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 43 (694 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 44 (695 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 45 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 45 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 46 (697 TR)** — A snake crossed her path and the old women of the village argued for a week about what it meant. **Age 46 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 47 (698 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal. **Age 47 (698 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Perin Strongpurse's child born (female, Perin Strongpurse age 24) **Age 48 (699 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it. **Age 49 (700 TR)** — She had heard the Halean houses were different from the common gaming tables. They were. The company was better, the wine was better, and the odds, on examination, were about the same. She enjoyed herself and understood that enjoyment was what she had paid for. **Age 49 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 49 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 49 (700 TR)** — Perin Strongpurse left to make her own way. Perin Strongpurse left home (age 26) **Age 50 (701 TR)** — She was pulled into a quarrel between two people she liked equally. She attempted fairness. This satisfied no one. **Age 50 (701 TR)** — Her husband had a habit she had not noticed for years that she suddenly could not unhear. She said nothing. It passed. **Age 51 (702 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets. **Age 51 (702 TR)** — Her son Perin Strongpurse remarried. Perin Strongpurse remarried Lara Plaintrader (merchant, age 28) **Age 51 (702 TR)** — Perin Strongpurse's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Perin Strongpurse's child died (age 2) **Age 52 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 52 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 53 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 53 (704 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 54 (705 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 54 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 55 (706 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time. **Age 55 (706 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically. **Age 56 (707 TR)** — She felt the change in the air before she saw it and understood that the year was turning. She had not been ready. **Age 56 (707 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 57 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 57 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 58 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 58 (709 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride. **Age 59 (710 TR)** — The court found her guilty. What followed was swift and public. **Age 59 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 60 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 60 (711 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough. **Age 61 (712 TR)** — She became the person others consulted. This had been happening for years; it was only recently that she recognised it as a pattern. **Age 61 (712 TR)** — Malik Strongpurse died after 40 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Malik Strongpurse died (approx. age 62) **Age 62 (713 TR)** — Her daughter's marriage was arranged and the merchet was due to the lord before the wedding. She sent the payment with the reeve and received the lord's written approval in return. The written approval is a document she keeps with the other land documents, because written approval means the marriage cannot later be contested on grounds of non-compliance with the lord's right. She is practical about the lord's rights. They exist. She pays them correctly and keeps the records. **Age 62 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 63 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 64 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 65 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 65 (716 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left. **Age 66 (717 TR)** — She had trusted this person. That trust had been a tool someone else picked up and used. Betrayed by Dera Longmarket **Age 66 (717 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it. **Age 67 (718 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 67 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 68 (719 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues. **Age 68 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 69 (720 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
name: Melda Fairmarket

narrative: **Age 18 (675 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time.
**Age 19 (676 TR)** — She was given in marriage, binding two households together. Married Cavan Broadsale (merchant, age 17)
**Age 19 (676 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Celyn Broadsale lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Celyn Broadsale born (female)

**Age 20 (677 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it.
**Age 20 (677 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 21 (678 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 22 (679 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; she left someone badly hurt.
**Age 23 (680 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question.
**Age 24 (681 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway.
**Age 24 (681 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular.
**Age 25 (682 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 25 (682 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Elora Broadsale lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Elora Broadsale born (female)
**Age 26 (683 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community.
**Age 26 (683 TR)** — He was among the men taken. She had no formal standing to object, no right to see him off. She stood at the edge of the road and watched the column go.
**Age 27 (684 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 27 (684 TR)** — Her child asked her what happened after death. She gave the best answer she had. She was not sure it was good enough.

**Age 28 (685 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
**Age 28 (685 TR)** — She bore a child. Ida Broadsale came into the world healthy. week 40 Ida Broadsale born (female)
**Age 29 (686 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 29 (686 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Antor Broadsale lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Antor Broadsale born (male)
**Age 30 (687 TR)** — She found a coin in an unexpected place — old, worn, and minted somewhere far away.
**Age 30 (687 TR)** — He came back. The relief she felt told her things about herself she had not quite admitted before.
**Age 31 (688 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 31 (688 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Corin Broadsale lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Corin Broadsale born (male)
**Age 32 (689 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 33 (690 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at.

**Age 33 (690 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Levan Broadsale. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Levan Broadsale born (male)
**Age 34 (691 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.
**Age 34 (691 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 19
**Age 35 (692 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 35 (692 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically.
**Age 36 (693 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.

**Age 36 (693 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.

**Age 37 (694 TR)** — She woke and the cradle was empty. The baby had been there at the third-hour feed. She would never know, in a precise sense, what had happened to her child. She would spend years constructing theories, each one worse than the last, none of them helpful.
**Age 37 (694 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 37 (694 TR)** — She lost Levan Broadsale — still a child, not yet grown. Levan Broadsale died (age 4)
**Age 38 (695 TR)** — The journey required more of her than she had expected and gave back more than she had hoped.
**Age 38 (695 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 38 (695 TR)** — Celyn Broadsale left to make her own way. Celyn Broadsale left home (age 19)
**Age 39 (696 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 40 (697 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Edris Coinsworth
**Age 41 (698 TR)** — She had thought herself a better judge of odds than she was. The coin was gone before she had fully reckoned what she'd risked.
**Age 41 (698 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 42 (699 TR)** — She ducked into a temple to get out of the rain and stayed far longer than the rain lasted.
**Age 42 (699 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 43 (700 TR)** — Someone had told the Matakea that she had been seen at a location associated with Navehan activity. She had not been there. The interview was thorough. She was believed, eventually. She spent some time afterward identifying who might have made the report and why.
**Age 43 (700 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Malik Broadsale lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Malik Broadsale born (male)
**Age 44 (701 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community.
**Age 44 (701 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 44 (701 TR)** — Her daughter Celyn Broadsale married Fenric Toring. Celyn Broadsale married Fenric Toring (peasant, age 28)
**Age 44 (701 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Celyn Broadsale's child born (male, Celyn Broadsale age 25)
**Age 44 (701 TR)** — Elora Broadsale left to make her own way. Elora Broadsale left home (age 19)
**Age 45 (702 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 45 (702 TR)** — Cavan Broadsale died after 26 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Cavan Broadsale died (approx. age 43)
**Age 46 (703 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 46 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Arden Thickpurse
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — Celyn Broadsale lost Iric Broadsale. She watched her child grieve and did not know how to carry both sorrows at once. Celyn Broadsale's child died (age 3)
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — Her daughter Ida Broadsale married Gotar Fletcher. Ida Broadsale married Gotar Fletcher (artisan, age 16)
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — Her son Antor Broadsale married Berith Longhaul. Antor Broadsale married Berith Longhaul (merchant, age 16)
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — Her son Corin Broadsale married Selda Turner. Corin Broadsale married Selda Turner (artisan, age 16)
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 48 (705 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Horic Broadsale (merchant)
**Age 49 (706 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 49 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 50 (707 TR)** — A snake crossed her path and the old women of the village argued for a week about what it meant.
**Age 50 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 50 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 51 (708 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 51 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 51 (708 TR)** — Ida Broadsale's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Ida Broadsale's child died (age 2)
**Age 51 (708 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 51 (708 TR)** — Corin Broadsale's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Corin Broadsale's child died (age 1)
**Age 52 (709 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 53 (710 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Perin Westmarket
**Age 53 (710 TR)** — She lost Malik Broadsale — still a child, not yet grown. Malik Broadsale died (age 10)
**Age 54 (711 TR)** — She misread someone's expression and said something she would not have said if she had read it right.
**Age 54 (711 TR)** — Antor Broadsale left to make her own way. Antor Broadsale left home (age 25)
**Age 55 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 56 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 56 (713 TR)** — Corin Broadsale left to make her own way. Corin Broadsale left home (age 25)
**Age 57 (714 TR)** — She began writing — not because anyone asked, but because it needed doing.
**Age 57 (714 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Elora Broadsale's illegitimate child born (female, Elora Broadsale age 32)
**Age 58 (715 TR)** — She watched girls younger than she had been when it all started and found herself at a loss for what to tell them.
**Age 59 (716 TR)** — She could not bring herself to go inside. She sat and watched the light change until it had finished doing so.
**Age 59 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 60 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 60 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 60 (717 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

She sat and watched the light change until it had finished doing so. **Age 59 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 60 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 60 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 60 (717 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 61 (718 TR)** — She fought for what was hers through every channel available to her. **Age 61 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 62 (719 TR)** — The court found her guilty. What followed was swift and public. **Age 62 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 62 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 63 (720 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 63 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 63 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 63 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
name: Orla Strongpurse

narrative: **Age 18 (681 TR)** — She worked alongside a artisan through a season, watching and helping.
**Age 19 (682 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the chest or torso that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 20 (683 TR)** — She was caught in a downpour with nowhere to go. She stood in it until it passed and felt, obscurely, better for it.
**Age 21 (684 TR)** — She lent something she valued and did not get it back. She said nothing. The matter sat between her and the other person for a long time.
**Age 22 (685 TR)** — An incident in public damaged her name in the community.
**Age 23 (686 TR)** — She could not bring herself to go inside. She sat and watched the light change until it had finished doing so.
**Age 24 (687 TR)** — She stood in the ruins and decided what came next.
**Age 25 (688 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Tara Heavypurse (merchant)
**Age 26 (689 TR)** — The debt that had dogged her was finally cleared.
**Age 27 (690 TR)** — She stood in the ruins and decided what came next.
**Age 28 (691 TR)** — The cat left a mouse on her pillow. She accepted this as the highest compliment.
**Age 29 (692 TR)** — She saw something that had turned violent and was not her affair. She noted it and walked on. She checked behind her twice.

**Age 30 (693 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere.
**Age 31 (694 TR)** — A person she had met once, briefly, remembered her name. She remembered nothing about them. She felt guilty about this.
**Age 32 (695 TR)** — The death of a relative brought unexpected property or coin into her hands.
**Age 33 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 34 (697 TR)** — An old man told her a story of a siege she had only heard in ballads. She kept thinking about it for weeks.

**Age 35 (698 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it.
**Age 36 (699 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 38 (701 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 39 (702 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Garith Oldcoin

**Age 40 (703 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 42 (705 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 43 (706 TR)** — She thought of what she should have said long after there was any chance to say it.
**Age 44 (707 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 45 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 46 (709 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient.

**Age 47 (710 TR)** — The old woman had been slowly handing over more of the work for three years. When she died, the guild transfer was contested by a cousin who knew nothing of the trade. She fought it through the Mangai and won on the merits. She paid the inheritance fee in full rather than in instalments, which she had not quite been able to afford, and which she did not regret.
**Age 48 (711 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Brand Plaintrader
**Age 49 (712 TR)** — The journey required more of her than she had expected and gave back more than she had hoped.
**Age 50 (713 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 51 (714 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Elora Wainworth
**Age 52 (715 TR)** — She misread someone's expression and said something she would not have said if she had read it right.

**Age 53 (716 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 54 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 55 (718 TR)** — She borrowed something meaning to return it. That was years ago. It has become hers through inertia.
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — She found it by accident. Old coin, mostly, wrapped in rotted cloth. More than she had expected. She told no one for a week.
**Age 57 (720 TR)** — She heard of something happening somewhere distant and found it affected her more than it should have.

name: Britta Rivergate

narrative: **Age 18 (687 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Balar Harborside (merchant)
**Age 19 (688 TR)** — A woman she knew was killed — a neighbour, a trader she dealt with, someone whose face she knew. It was the kind of death that made everyone quieter for a while.
**Age 20 (689 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 21 (690 TR)** — She finished something and found she was satisfied with it. That was rarer than it should have been.
**Age 22 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 23 (692 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.

**Age 24 (693 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination.
**Age 25 (694 TR)** — She was given in marriage, binding two households together. Married Morin Goldcloak (merchant, age 22)

**Age 25 (694 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Arnam Goldcloak. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Arnam Goldcloak born (male)
**Age 26 (695 TR)** — She perfected something in the kitchen that year. She was asked for the recipe regularly and gave it out selectively.
**Age 26 (695 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 35
**Age 27 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 27 (696 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Meric Goldcloak — lived. week 40 Meric Goldcloak born (male)
**Age 28 (697 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 29 (698 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Marek Ilendi

**Age 29 (698 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Elith Goldcloak. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Elith Goldcloak born (female)
**Age 30 (699 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 31 (700 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 31 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 32 (701 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 32 (701 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 32 (701 TR)** — She lost Arnam Goldcloak — still a child, not yet grown. Arnam Goldcloak died (age 7)

**Age 33 (702 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.

**Age 33 (702 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Elva Goldcloak. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Elva Goldcloak born (female)
**Age 34 (703 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection.
**Age 34 (703 TR)** — She lost Elith Goldcloak — still a child, not yet grown. Elith Goldcloak died (age 5)
**Age 35 (704 TR)** — She watched someone who really knew what they were doing. She had thought she knew. She saw now that she had been adequate.
**Age 35 (704 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.

**Age 36 (705 TR)** — The franchise had come available when old Maret died with no heirs. She had moved faster than the men who assumed she would not, and had the backing of two syndics before the others had finished shaking their heads. The vote was closer than she liked. She did not let this show.
**Age 36 (705 TR)** — Morin Goldcloak died after 11 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Morin Goldcloak died (approx. age 33)

**Age 37 (706 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere.
**Age 37 (706 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular.
**Age 38 (707 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 39 (708 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back.
**Age 40 (709 TR)** — The sickness passed but left her diminished for months — thinner, slower to tire, more careful about cold. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 40 (709 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it.
**Age 41 (710 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 42 (711 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done.
**Age 42 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 43 (712 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Perin Packworth
**Age 44 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 44 (713 TR)** — Her son Meric Goldcloak married Dera Dustmantle. Meric Goldcloak married Dera Dustmantle (merchant, age 16)
**Age 45 (714 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Tovan Heavypurse
**Age 45 (714 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Elva Goldcloak apprenticed (age 12)

**Age 46 (715 TR)** — Her daughter's marriage was arranged and the merchet was due to the lord before the wedding. She sent the payment with the reeve and received the lord's written approval in return. The written approval is a document she keeps with the other land documents, because written approval means the marriage cannot later be contested on grounds of non-compliance with the lord's right. She is practical about the lord's rights. They exist. She pays them correctly and keeps the records.
**Age 46 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 46 (715 TR)** — Meric Goldcloak left to make her own way. Meric Goldcloak left home (age 19)
**Age 47 (716 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 47 (716 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty.
**Age 47 (716 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Meric Goldcloak's child born (male, Meric Goldcloak age 20)
**Age 48 (717 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 49 (718 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 49 (718 TR)** — Her daughter Elva Goldcloak married Varen Keenscale. Elva Goldcloak married Varen Keenscale (merchant, age 20)
**Age 50 (719 TR)** — The change had been gradual and then sudden. She could trace exactly when it had stopped being repairable. Tovan Heavypurse became an enemy
**Age 50 (719 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 50 (719 TR)** — Her son Mael Goldcloak married Aerin Coppergate. Mael Goldcloak married Aerin Coppergate (merchant, age 18)
**Age 50 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 51 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 51 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

name: Elora Tradesworth

narrative: **Age 18 (693 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.

**Age 19 (694 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
**Age 21 (696 TR)** — The ferry was not running. She waited. Another passenger talked at her. She listened politely for two hours.

**Age 22 (697 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination.
**Age 23 (698 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 24 (699 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Vorin Packworth (merchant, age 26)
**Age 24 (699 TR)** — She bore a child. Larith Packworth came into the world healthy. week 40 Larith Packworth born (female)
**Age 25 (700 TR)** — She heard something through a door that changed how she thought about two people she knew. She said nothing.
**Age 25 (700 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty.
**Age 26 (701 TR)** — She ate something — plain food, ordinary occasion — that tasted better than anything she had eaten before or since.
**Age 27 (702 TR)** — The creditors came. What she had built was gone.
**Age 27 (702 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 28 (703 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.

**Age 28 (703 TR)** — Something became funny in a way that built and built until everyone was helpless with it. She remembered it for the rest of her life without being able to say what exactly had been funny.
**Age 29 (704 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 29 (704 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.
**Age 30 (705 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway.

**Age 30 (705 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Balar Packworth. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Balar Packworth born (male)
**Age 31 (706 TR)** — The secret came out. She faced the judgment of everyone she knew.
**Age 31 (706 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time. Balar Packworth died (age 1)
**Age 32 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 32 (707 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Sira Packworth. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 Sira Packworth born (female)
**Age 33 (708 TR)** — There were rats. She dealt with them methodically. The cat was no help whatsoever.
**Age 34 (709 TR)** — Hunger, debt, or simple greed — she took what was not hers.

**Age 35 (710 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at.
**Age 35 (710 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 36 (711 TR)** — The harvest failed. She stretched what remained as far as it would go.
**Age 37 (712 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question.
**Age 37 (712 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 38 (713 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 38 (713 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.

**Age 39 (714 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal.
**Age 39 (714 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier.

**Age 40 (715 TR)** — Her late husband had been a freeholder. The new lord's steward arrived with a document she had never seen before claiming her husband had accepted serf obligations six years prior. The document was real; her husband had signed it during a hard winter when the choice was that or losing the holding. She had not known. She appealed to the hundred moot. The moot found that the serf arrangement had applied to her husband and passed to her by inheritance. She accepted this and began saving to buy her way to freeholder status. It took four years. The lord took what she offered and issued the document. She keeps both documents together.

**Age 41 (716 TR)** — Her mother had not wanted to go. She had insisted, because the alternative was managing a dying woman in three rooms with two children underfoot. The Peonian house took her without fuss. She visited every day. Her mother died in spring, quietly, with a sister holding her hand because she had arrived twenty minutes too late.
**Age 42 (717 TR)** — She felt the change in the air before she saw it and understood that the year was turning. She had not been ready.
**Age 42 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (718 TR)** — She attempted something beyond her usual skill and succeeded. She was pleased and a little suspicious of this.
**Age 43 (718 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back.
**Age 44 (719 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 44 (719 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 45 (720 TR)** — She could tell rain from cloud before most felt the wind shift.
**Age 45 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 27** *(class change)* — Lost livelihood to debt — engine routes urban→unguilded, rural→peasant [merchant → unguilded]
narrativeText: **Age 18 (693 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet. **Age 19 (694 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them. **Age 21 (696 TR)** — The ferry was not running. She waited. Another passenger talked at her. She listened politely for two hours. **Age 22 (697 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination. **Age 23 (698 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike. **Age 24 (699 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Vorin Packworth (merchant, age 26) **Age 24 (699 TR)** — She bore a child. Larith Packworth came into the world healthy. week 40 Larith Packworth born (female) **Age 25 (700 TR)** — She heard something through a door that changed how she thought about two people she knew. She said nothing. **Age 25 (700 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty. **Age 26 (701 TR)** — She ate something — plain food, ordinary occasion — that tasted better than anything she had eaten before or since. **Age 27 (702 TR)** — The creditors came. What she had built was gone. **Age 27 (702 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. name: Gara Finecoin

narrative: **Age 18 (699 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Aldric Millworth (merchant, age 16)
**Age 19 (700 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 19 (700 TR)** — She bore a child. Uther Millworth came into the world healthy. week 40 Uther Millworth born (male)
**Age 20 (701 TR)** — She was completely sure of something and incorrect. She found this out in front of other people.
**Age 20 (701 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Cade Millworth lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Cade Millworth born (male)
**Age 21 (702 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Sara Cargoham
**Age 21 (702 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 21
**Age 22 (703 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Kuric (clergy)

**Age 23 (704 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere.
**Age 23 (704 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt.
**Age 24 (705 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Kavan Millworth lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Kavan Millworth born (male)
**Age 25 (706 TR)** — She was up before the household and saw something small and perfect that was gone by the time anyone else was about.
**Age 25 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 26 (707 TR)** — She stood in the ruins and decided what came next.
**Age 26 (707 TR)** — The child was lost before it had become real to anyone else. She marked it privately. week 4
**Age 27 (708 TR)** — She was among the crowd when the sentence was carried out. The silence that fell at the end was not what she expected.
**Age 27 (708 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically.
**Age 28 (709 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 28 (709 TR)** — She watched her parent simply get on with things during a hard stretch. She learned something about it that stayed with her.
**Age 29 (710 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the hip or groin that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 29 (710 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.
**Age 30 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 31 (712 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it.
**Age 31 (712 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 32 (713 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier.
**Age 33 (714 TR)** — She put out the washing. It began to rain before she reached the door. She stood for a moment and then went back for it.
**Age 34 (715 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient.
**Age 34 (715 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back.
**Age 35 (716 TR)** — She was seized by laughter at the worst possible time. She managed to contain it. Barely.
**Age 35 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 36 (717 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 36 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 36 (717 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Cade Millworth's illegitimate child born (male, Cade Millworth age 16)
**Age 37 (718 TR)** — She went down in full view of several people. She stood up, straightened herself, and continued. No one mentioned it. She mentioned it to herself regularly.
**Age 37 (718 TR)** — She lost the child early — before she had said much aloud about it. She said nothing after, either. week 9
**Age 38 (719 TR)** — She smelled something that stopped her dead in the street. It brought back a whole season she had thought she had forgotten.
**Age 38 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (719 TR)** — Her son Uther Millworth married Mira Amberton. Uther Millworth married Mira Amberton (merchant, age 16)
**Age 38 (719 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Uther Millworth's child born (male, Uther Millworth age 19)
**Age 39 (720 TR)** — The change had been gradual and then sudden. She could trace exactly when it had stopped being repairable. Welith Goodbarrel became an enemy
**Age 39 (720 TR)** — Uther Millworth left to make her own way. Uther Millworth left home (age 20)

name: Selda Fairmarket

narrative: **Age 18 (705 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 19 (706 TR)** — She pulled a door that pushed and then pushed it and it still did not open and someone eventually told her it was bolted.
**Age 20 (707 TR)** — Her horse stopped dead on a familiar road and would not be moved for an hour.
**Age 21 (708 TR)** — The journey required more of her than she had expected and gave back more than she had hoped.
**Age 22 (709 TR)** — She was among the crowd when the sentence was carried out. The silence that fell at the end was not what she expected.

**Age 23 (710 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 24 (711 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 25 (712 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 26 (713 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild.

**Age 27 (714 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 28 (715 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.

**Age 29 (716 TR)** — The introduction was managed without any appearance of effort. She had been speaking to someone she did not know, and the Shenasene had simply inserted herself into the conversation at the right moment and redirected it. She left with the name she needed. She understood that she was now in an account book somewhere.

**Age 30 (717 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 31 (718 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Welith Laramin (noble)
**Age 32 (719 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.
**Age 33 (720 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.

name: Teda Goldcloak

**Age 19 (712 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Balar Fartrader (merchant)
**Age 20 (713 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 21 (714 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 22 (715 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. She paid the price.
**Age 23 (716 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets.

**Age 24 (717 TR)** — She had confided things to the woman — things about her household, her finances, her husband's debts. She learned later, and by accident, that those confidences had been accurately relayed somewhere she would not have chosen. The friendship was real. The other work was also real. Both things were true at once.
**Age 25 (718 TR)** — She heard of the death through others — there had been no warning, no leave-taking. Balar Fartrader has died
**Age 26 (719 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient.

**Age 27 (720 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (711 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it. **Age 19 (712 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Balar Fartrader (merchant) **Age 20 (713 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest. **Age 21 (714 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues. **Age 22 (715 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. She paid the price. **Age 23 (716 TR)** — She accompanied a caravan — widow, factor, or simply bold — to distant markets. **Age 24 (717 TR)** — She had confided things to the woman — things about her household, her finances, her husband's debts. She learned later, and by accident, that those confidences had been accurately relayed somewhere she would not have chosen. The friendship was real. The other work was also real. Both things were true at once. **Age 25 (718 TR)** — She heard of the death through others — there had been no warning, no leave-taking. Balar Fartrader has died **Age 26 (719 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient. **Age 27 (720 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.
name: Edris Thickpurse

narrative: **Age 18 (717 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Stevan Oldcoin (merchant, age 19)
**Age 18 (717 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.

**Age 19 (718 TR)** — She had expected the priest to favour the other party, who was male and of better standing. He did not. She received a settlement that was just if not generous and was left with a more complicated view of the church than she had arrived with.

**Age 20 (719 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 20 (719 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.
**Age 21 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would. **Age 21 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
name: Rira Heavypurse

narrative: **Age 18 (665 TR)** — She had paid dues in bad years before. This year was different: the new baby, the tools she had replaced, the roof. She asked the chapter for a loan and was told to present her case to the syndics, which she did, in writing, which she had heard was more effective. The syndics granted the deferral for six months. She paid it back in five.

**Age 19 (666 TR)** — She would not have known him except that he laughed — the same short laugh, the kind that means nothing is funny. The cutpurse from the Endakil festival. She watched him move through the crowd with professional ease and felt something cold and very precise in her chest that was not exactly anger.
**Age 20 (667 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Morath Wealthgate (merchant)
**Age 21 (668 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.

**Age 22 (669 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination.
**Age 23 (670 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 24 (671 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.

**Age 25 (672 TR)** — The inspection had been prompted by a competitor, she was nearly certain. The syndic who conducted it was scrupulously fair but thorough. She passed every check. When the syndic left, she went back to work and did not spend more than ten minutes wondering who had called the inspection, which was about nine minutes longer than she had intended.
**Age 26 (673 TR)** — She borrowed something meaning to return it. That was years ago. It has become hers through inertia.
**Age 27 (674 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Melyn Silverscale
**Age 28 (675 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 29 (676 TR)** — The change had been gradual and then sudden. She could trace exactly when it had stopped being repairable. Melyn Silverscale became an enemy

**Age 30 (677 TR)** — She had heard the Halean houses were different from the common gaming tables. They were. The company was better, the wine was better, and the odds, on examination, were about the same. She enjoyed herself and understood that enjoyment was what she had paid for.

**Age 31 (678 TR)** — She had expected the priest to favour the other party, who was male and of better standing. He did not. She received a settlement that was just if not generous and was left with a more complicated view of the church than she had arrived with.

**Age 32 (679 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 33 (680 TR)** — She had trusted this person. That trust had been a tool someone else picked up and used. Betrayed by Morath Wealthgate
**Age 34 (681 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A serious wound to the knee. It healed, but left a permanent reduction in agility (−1).
**Age 35 (682 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 36 (683 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it.

**Age 37 (684 TR)** — The approach was made in person, by a woman she had never seen, who knew things about her that no one had any business knowing. The price was stated plainly. She paid it. She is still paying it. She has not yet determined who is behind the approach, which tells her they are very good at this.
**Age 38 (685 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 39 (686 TR)** — Through persistence or her late husband's name, she won a place in the guild.
**Age 40 (687 TR)** — She found someone willing to teach and gave that person her full attention. Studied under Soran Richgate
**Age 41 (688 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Soran Richgate

**Age 42 (689 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 43 (690 TR)** — She handed the accounts and keys to a younger hand and stepped back.

**Age 44 (691 TR)** — The old woman had been slowly handing over more of the work for three years. When she died, the guild transfer was contested by a cousin who knew nothing of the trade. She fought it through the Mangai and won on the merits. She paid the inheritance fee in full rather than in instalments, which she had not quite been able to afford, and which she did not regret.
**Age 45 (692 TR)** — She saw a man killed — not in war, but in the world she moved through every day. The image did not leave her easily.
**Age 46 (693 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 47 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 48 (695 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 49 (696 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 50 (697 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 51 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 52 (699 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.
**Age 53 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 54 (701 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 55 (702 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (703 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Breda Dustmantle
**Age 57 (704 TR)** — She remembered one particular market day with unusual clarity — the smells, the light, the sequence of purchases — without knowing why that one.
**Age 58 (705 TR)** — She heard of the death through others — there had been no warning, no leave-taking. Morath Wealthgate has died
**Age 59 (706 TR)** — The sickness passed but left her diminished for months — thinner, slower to tire, more careful about cold. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 60 (707 TR)** — She came across a text she was probably not supposed to read. She read it three times.
**Age 61 (708 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 62 (709 TR)** — Enough time had passed that pride mattered less than the person. She made the first move, or they did. Reconciled with Melyn Silverscale
**Age 63 (710 TR)** — The venture soured. Creditors grew impatient.
**Age 64 (711 TR)** — She set her affairs in order with the same methodical care she had brought to everything. She felt lighter for it.
**Age 65 (712 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read.

**Age 66 (713 TR)** — She is here as a witness, not a party. A woman from the village is contesting her status as a freeholder against the new lord's claim that she is a serf. She knows this woman. She knows the family's history on that land going back two generations. She has been asked to testify to what she knows. She will say exactly what she knows and nothing she doesn't know and she will be as clear as possible about which is which. The moot's ability to get this right depends on witnesses being honest. She intends to be an example of what that looks like.
**Age 68 (715 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 69 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 70 (717 TR)** — She came across someone sleeping where they should not have been. She covered them with something and went away.

**Age 71 (718 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that.
**Age 72 (719 TR)** — She lit a candle for someone she had not thought of in years. She did not examine this.
**Age 73 (720 TR)** — The needle, the knife, the spinning rod — whatever she relied on most — broke the day she needed it.
