name: Estra Borkin

narrative: **Age 18 (669 TR)** — She was asked to deliver a package to a specific house in a town sixty miles out and note, on the way back, what she could observe about the movement of men and horses on the Tashal road. She delivered the package. She observed the movement. Her report contained three pieces of information her officer had not expected, which she knows because of how he reacted to those three pieces. She is aware she is useful in a specific way. She is deciding what to do with that awareness.
**Age 19 (670 TR)** — She was certain of the weather and entirely mistaken. She said nothing about it. Others said enough.

**Age 20 (671 TR)** — She identified it as camp fever on the second day and began the isolation protocols that the army officer had not heard of and was initially resistant to. She explained clearly why isolation was necessary. He allowed it after two days. Those two days probably cost two of the seven men who died. She has thought about how to explain this more quickly next time, which is the productive direction for the thought, and she returns to it when she is not too tired to think productively.
**Age 21 (672 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time.
**Age 22 (673 TR)** — Hunger, debt, or simple greed — she took what was not hers.
**Age 23 (674 TR)** — She remembered one particular market day with unusual clarity — the smells, the light, the sequence of purchases — without knowing why that one.

**Age 24 (675 TR)** — There is a specific kind of problem that comes with being a woman in a company that has not fully decided how to handle women in a company. She handled it. She is not going to describe in detail how she handled it because the details are not the point — the point is that she handled it in a way that did not require the sergeant's intervention and did not damage her operational standing and produced a working arrangement with the person involved that, while not friendly, is functional. She has added this to her list of skills that are not in any training manual.
**Age 25 (676 TR)** — When the coin was offered, she found reasons to look the other way.

**Age 26 (677 TR)** — She learned to present herself as more useful than she was on any given day, because soldiers who appear less useful than the cost of feeding them get reassigned in ways that are worse. She learned to read the officer's mood at fifty yards and adjust accordingly. She learned which medical knowledge was actually useful in the field and which was the kind of thing you learned in training to feel prepared. The gap between those two categories is significant.
**Age 27 (678 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Erlan Kalman (soldier)
**Age 28 (679 TR)** — She gave directions with certainty and then spent an hour hoping the person had figured out the mistake.
**Age 29 (680 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 30 (681 TR)** — There was a specific moment — she can identify it — when she did something in front of enough witnesses that the question of whether she belonged there stopped being asked. Not permanently, not universally; the question is never permanently retired. But in that unit, in that company, among those particular men, there was a before and an after. She did not celebrate. She did the next thing, and the thing after that. She is aware that the bar was set differently for her than for the men around her and that clearing it was not the same as having it set fairly. She cleared it anyway.
**Age 31 (682 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Kavan Tarson (soldier, age 29)

**Age 31 (682 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.
**Age 32 (683 TR)** — A feud that began with a killing reached into her household. She had no say in the quarrel itself, but the consequences arrived at her door regardless. Feud with Koral Braning
**Age 32 (683 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically.
**Age 33 (684 TR)** — Hunger, debt, or simple greed — she took what was not hers.
**Age 33 (684 TR)** — She bore a child. Brand Tarson came into the world healthy. week 40 Brand Tarson born (male)
**Age 34 (685 TR)** — She kept the household alive through ingenuity and stubbornness.
**Age 34 (685 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Morath Tarson lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Morath Tarson born (female)
**Age 35 (686 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 35 (686 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 13

**Age 36 (687 TR)** — The cover held but it was close. A merchant's wife asked her about a village she claimed to be from and knew it in a level of detail she didn't — specific names, specific streets. She deflected smoothly enough but the deflection was noticed; she saw it noticed. She completed the task and returned and asked her officer directly whether she needed to change the cover identity. He said not yet. She is keeping very careful track of what 'not yet' means in practice.
**Age 36 (687 TR)** — The child came too still. She had no husband to share the weight of it. week 24

**Age 37 (688 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 37 (688 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 38 (689 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (689 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself.
**Age 39 (690 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read.

**Age 40 (691 TR)** — The camp economy runs on the sutlers and everyone knows it, including the officers who would prefer not to acknowledge it. She extends credit carefully — who is reliable, who has family money, who is the kind of man who runs when things go wrong. She has been cheated precisely twice and recovered from both by knowing which sergeant to speak to. She is carrying the wages of four dead men in her ledger, which she will attempt to recover from their estates when the campaign ends, and which she is fairly certain she will not recover.
**Age 40 (691 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 41 (692 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 41 (692 TR)** — She bore a child. Voran Tarson came into the world healthy. week 40 Voran Tarson born (male)
**Age 42 (693 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 42 (693 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 42 (693 TR)** — She lost Brand Tarson — still a child, not yet grown. Brand Tarson died (age 9)
**Age 43 (694 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Halden Tarson — lived. week 40 Halden Tarson born (male)
**Age 44 (695 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 45 (696 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 45 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 46 (697 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 46 (697 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.
**Age 47 (698 TR)** — She ran her hand over something and the feeling of it brought back a place and a time she had not thought of in years.
**Age 48 (699 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 49 (700 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection.

**Age 50 (701 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.
**Age 51 (702 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question.
**Age 51 (702 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt.
**Age 52 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 53 (704 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that.
**Age 53 (704 TR)** — Her daughter Morath Tarson married Garan Alsen. Morath Tarson married Garan Alsen (peasant, age 16)
**Age 53 (704 TR)** — Morath Tarson left to make her own way. Morath Tarson left home (age 19)

**Age 54 (705 TR)** — The tactical situation made going back inadvisable. She went anyway. The knight she brought back was alive, which had not been certain from a distance, and was subsequently able to walk, which had also not been certain. He never thanked her directly. He gave her his second sword when he returned to civilian life, which she understood to be the same thing.
**Age 54 (705 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Halden Tarson hired out (age 11)
**Age 55 (706 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.
**Age 55 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (707 TR)** — She thought of what she should have said long after there was any chance to say it.
**Age 56 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 57 (708 TR)** — She was not trained for battlefield wounds. She was trained for illness, for fever, for childbirth. She adapted in the first campaign and has been adapting since. The military surgeons, when there are any, do not always know more than she does. Sometimes they know less. She does the work that needs doing and has stopped spending energy on the question of whether she is supposed to be the one doing it.
**Age 57 (708 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 57 (708 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Morath Tarson's child born (male, Morath Tarson age 23)
**Age 58 (709 TR)** — The debt that had dogged her was finally cleared.
**Age 59 (710 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 59 (710 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 59 (710 TR)** — Her son Halden Tarson married Fara Waling. Halden Tarson married Fara Waling (peasant, age 16)
**Age 60 (711 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 60 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 61 (712 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 61 (712 TR)** — Her son Voran Tarson married Norath Idsen. Voran Tarson married Norath Idsen (soldier, age 18)
**Age 62 (713 TR)** — She set her affairs in order with the same methodical care she had brought to everything. She felt lighter for it.
**Age 62 (713 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.

**Age 63 (714 TR)** — She wrote off four debts this campaign. Two dead, one deserted, one simply gone. She adjusts the prices for everyone else accordingly, which is the honest way to run a sutler's operation: the cost of unrecoverable debt is distributed across the credit that does get repaid. She does not complain about this. It is a known cost of the trade. She does note the names carefully, because some of the deserters end up in other armies, and the road network is smaller than it appears.
**Age 63 (714 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 63 (714 TR)** — Voran Tarson left to make her own way. Voran Tarson left home (age 22)
**Age 64 (715 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 64 (715 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 64 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 65 (716 TR)** — She let go of what she could no longer do and found she still had plenty left.
**Age 65 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 66 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 66 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 67 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 67 (718 TR)** — Halden Tarson lost Horic Tarson. She watched her child grieve and did not know how to carry both sorrows at once. Halden Tarson's child died (age 3)

**Age 68 (719 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 68 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 68 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 69 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 69 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (669 TR)** — She was asked to deliver a package to a specific house in a town sixty miles out and note, on the way back, what she could observe about the movement of men and horses on the Tashal road. She delivered the package. She observed the movement. Her report contained three pieces of information her officer had not expected, which she knows because of how he reacted to those three pieces. She is aware she is useful in a specific way. She is deciding what to do with that awareness. **Age 19 (670 TR)** — She was certain of the weather and entirely mistaken. She said nothing about it. Others said enough. **Age 20 (671 TR)** — She identified it as camp fever on the second day and began the isolation protocols that the army officer had not heard of and was initially resistant to. She explained clearly why isolation was necessary. He allowed it after two days. Those two days probably cost two of the seven men who died. She has thought about how to explain this more quickly next time, which is the productive direction for the thought, and she returns to it when she is not too tired to think productively. **Age 21 (672 TR)** — She went somewhere she had never been, which was unusual enough that she paid careful attention the entire time. **Age 22 (673 TR)** — Hunger, debt, or simple greed — she took what was not hers. **Age 23 (674 TR)** — She remembered one particular market day with unusual clarity — the smells, the light, the sequence of purchases — without knowing why that one. **Age 24 (675 TR)** — There is a specific kind of problem that comes with being a woman in a company that has not fully decided how to handle women in a company. She handled it. She is not going to describe in detail how she handled it because the details are not the point — the point is that she handled it in a way that did not require the sergeant's intervention and did not damage her operational standing and produced a working arrangement with the person involved that, while not friendly, is functional. She has added this to her list of skills that are not in any training manual. **Age 25 (676 TR)** — When the coin was offered, she found reasons to look the other way. **Age 26 (677 TR)** — She learned to present herself as more useful than she was on any given day, because soldiers who appear less useful than the cost of feeding them get reassigned in ways that are worse. She learned to read the officer's mood at fifty yards and adjust accordingly. She learned which medical knowledge was actually useful in the field and which was the kind of thing you learned in training to feel prepared. The gap between those two categories is significant. **Age 27 (678 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Erlan Kalman (soldier) **Age 28 (679 TR)** — She gave directions with certainty and then spent an hour hoping the person had figured out the mistake. **Age 29 (680 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 30 (681 TR)** — There was a specific moment — she can identify it — when she did something in front of enough witnesses that the question of whether she belonged there stopped being asked. Not permanently, not universally; the question is never permanently retired. But in that unit, in that company, among those particular men, there was a before and an after. She did not celebrate. She did the next thing, and the thing after that. She is aware that the bar was set differently for her than for the men around her and that clearing it was not the same as having it set fairly. She cleared it anyway. **Age 31 (682 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Kavan Tarson (soldier, age 29) **Age 31 (682 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes. **Age 32 (683 TR)** — A feud that began with a killing reached into her household. She had no say in the quarrel itself, but the consequences arrived at her door regardless. Feud with Koral Braning **Age 32 (683 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically. **Age 33 (684 TR)** — Hunger, debt, or simple greed — she took what was not hers. **Age 33 (684 TR)** — She bore a child. Brand Tarson came into the world healthy. week 40 Brand Tarson born (male) **Age 34 (685 TR)** — She kept the household alive through ingenuity and stubbornness. **Age 34 (685 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Morath Tarson lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Morath Tarson born (female) **Age 35 (686 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike. **Age 35 (686 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. There was no husband to grieve beside her. week 13 **Age 36 (687 TR)** — The cover held but it was close. A merchant's wife asked her about a village she claimed to be from and knew it in a level of detail she didn't — specific names, specific streets. She deflected smoothly enough but the deflection was noticed; she saw it noticed. She completed the task and returned and asked her officer directly whether she needed to change the cover identity. He said not yet. She is keeping very careful track of what 'not yet' means in practice. **Age 36 (687 TR)** — The child came too still. She had no husband to share the weight of it. week 24 **Age 37 (688 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 37 (688 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 38 (689 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 38 (689 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself. **Age 39 (690 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read. **Age 40 (691 TR)** — The camp economy runs on the sutlers and everyone knows it, including the officers who would prefer not to acknowledge it. She extends credit carefully — who is reliable, who has family money, who is the kind of man who runs when things go wrong. She has been cheated precisely twice and recovered from both by knowing which sergeant to speak to. She is carrying the wages of four dead men in her ledger, which she will attempt to recover from their estates when the campaign ends, and which she is fairly certain she will not recover. **Age 40 (691 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 41 (692 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 41 (692 TR)** — She bore a child. Voran Tarson came into the world healthy. week 40 Voran Tarson born (male) **Age 42 (693 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 42 (693 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 42 (693 TR)** — She lost Brand Tarson — still a child, not yet grown. Brand Tarson died (age 9) **Age 43 (694 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Halden Tarson — lived. week 40 Halden Tarson born (male) **Age 44 (695 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option. **Age 45 (696 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues. **Age 45 (696 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 46 (697 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 46 (697 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine. **Age 47 (698 TR)** — She ran her hand over something and the feeling of it brought back a place and a time she had not thought of in years. **Age 48 (699 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 49 (700 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection. **Age 50 (701 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it. **Age 51 (702 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question. **Age 51 (702 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt. **Age 52 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 53 (704 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that. **Age 53 (704 TR)** — Her daughter Morath Tarson married Garan Alsen. Morath Tarson married Garan Alsen (peasant, age 16) **Age 53 (704 TR)** — Morath Tarson left to make her own way. Morath Tarson left home (age 19) **Age 54 (705 TR)** — The tactical situation made going back inadvisable. She went anyway. The knight she brought back was alive, which had not been certain from a distance, and was subsequently able to walk, which had also not been certain. He never thanked her directly. He gave her his second sword when he returned to civilian life, which she understood to be the same thing. **Age 54 (705 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Halden Tarson hired out (age 11) **Age 55 (706 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted. **Age 55 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. She adapted in the first campaign and has been adapting since. The military surgeons, when there are any, do not always know more than she does. Sometimes they know less. name: Orla Garman

narrative: **Age 18 (675 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Orin Koring (soldier, age 16)
**Age 18 (675 TR)** — The child came at term and did not breathe. She had been so close. week 38

**Age 19 (676 TR)** — The camp economy runs on the sutlers and everyone knows it, including the officers who would prefer not to acknowledge it. She extends credit carefully — who is reliable, who has family money, who is the kind of man who runs when things go wrong. She has been cheated precisely twice and recovered from both by knowing which sergeant to speak to. She is carrying the wages of four dead men in her ledger, which she will attempt to recover from their estates when the campaign ends, and which she is fairly certain she will not recover.
**Age 19 (676 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.
**Age 20 (677 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 20 (677 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically.

**Age 21 (678 TR)** — There is a specific kind of problem that comes with being a woman in a company that has not fully decided how to handle women in a company. She handled it. She is not going to describe in detail how she handled it because the details are not the point — the point is that she handled it in a way that did not require the sergeant's intervention and did not damage her operational standing and produced a working arrangement with the person involved that, while not friendly, is functional. She has added this to her list of skills that are not in any training manual.

**Age 22 (679 TR)** — The order was to burn the village. The people were Morgathian sympathisers, the officer said, which may even have been true. She refused in front of the column and said Larani's name when she did it. There was a silence. The officer did not repeat the order. She has never understood why.
**Age 23 (680 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline.
**Age 23 (680 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.

**Age 24 (681 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness.
**Age 24 (681 TR)** — She lost the child early — before she had said much aloud about it. She said nothing after, either. week 6

**Age 25 (682 TR)** — She learned to present herself as more useful than she was on any given day, because soldiers who appear less useful than the cost of feeding them get reassigned in ways that are worse. She learned to read the officer's mood at fifty yards and adjust accordingly. She learned which medical knowledge was actually useful in the field and which was the kind of thing you learned in training to feel prepared. The gap between those two categories is significant.

**Age 26 (683 TR)** — She had been told that Larani answered prayer in extremity. She had not believed this with any conviction. On the slope above Erone she saw a man with a belly wound walk two hundred yards to the surgeon's tent under his own power. The surgeon confirmed what the wound implied. She has carried this carefully ever since, not sure what to do with it.
**Age 26 (683 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 32
**Age 27 (684 TR)** — She drank more than was wise and behaved in ways she thought about for several days afterwards.
**Age 28 (685 TR)** — She found a name carved somewhere — a wall, a beam, a stone — and thought about the hand that had made it.
**Age 28 (685 TR)** — The child was lost before it had become real to anyone else. She marked it privately. week 4
**Age 29 (686 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 29 (686 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 12

**Age 30 (687 TR)** — The cover held but it was close. A merchant's wife asked her about a village she claimed to be from and knew it in a level of detail she didn't — specific names, specific streets. She deflected smoothly enough but the deflection was noticed; she saw it noticed. She completed the task and returned and asked her officer directly whether she needed to change the cover identity. He said not yet. She is keeping very careful track of what 'not yet' means in practice.
**Age 30 (687 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 31 (688 TR)** — Necessity had long since made her skilled. She went beyond necessity.

**Age 32 (689 TR)** — The mission had been going well — that was the part that was hardest to hear. Twelve people who thought they understood the situation. She filed it under: the thing you assume is working is the thing that kills you.

**Age 33 (690 TR)** — She wrote off four debts this campaign. Two dead, one deserted, one simply gone. She adjusts the prices for everyone else accordingly, which is the honest way to run a sutler's operation: the cost of unrecoverable debt is distributed across the credit that does get repaid. She does not complain about this. It is a known cost of the trade. She does note the names carefully, because some of the deserters end up in other armies, and the road network is smaller than it appears.
**Age 33 (690 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine.
**Age 34 (691 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 35 (692 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Arwen Alson (soldier)
**Age 35 (692 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 36 (693 TR)** — The vow was made in private, to the sword-altar, in language she had not planned and could not quite reproduce afterward. She survived. She has been paying the debt ever since in ways she has not always been able to name — small acts, a certain quality of attention, a refusal of one particular easy choice, repeated.
**Age 36 (693 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 37 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 37 (694 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier.
**Age 38 (695 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.

**Age 39 (696 TR)** — She is a widow visiting her sister's family, which is why she is on this road, which is why she has stopped at this inn, which is why she is asking about the road ahead to the next village. None of this is true. All of it holds. She has learned that the most sustainable cover is one that requires the minimum number of lies — the one that lets the truth do as much of the work as possible.
**Age 39 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 40 (697 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 40 (697 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 41 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 41 (698 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back.

**Age 42 (699 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at.
**Age 42 (699 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 43 (700 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 43 (700 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it.

**Age 44 (701 TR)** — She was asked to deliver a package to a specific house in a town sixty miles out and note, on the way back, what she could observe about the movement of men and horses on the Tashal road. She delivered the package. She observed the movement. Her report contained three pieces of information her officer had not expected, which she knows because of how he reacted to those three pieces. She is aware she is useful in a specific way. She is deciding what to do with that awareness.
**Age 44 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 45 (702 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.

**Age 45 (702 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.

**Age 46 (703 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it.
**Age 46 (703 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 47 (704 TR)** — She was not trained for battlefield wounds. She was trained for illness, for fever, for childbirth. She adapted in the first campaign and has been adapting since. The military surgeons, when there are any, do not always know more than she does. Sometimes they know less. She does the work that needs doing and has stopped spending energy on the question of whether she is supposed to be the one doing it.
**Age 47 (704 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself.
**Age 48 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 49 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 49 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 50 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 51 (708 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 52 (709 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the lower leg or shin that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 52 (709 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.
**Age 53 (710 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. She paid the price.
**Age 53 (710 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride.
**Age 54 (711 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 54 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 55 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (713 TR)** — She kept to it when most would have stopped. The discipline became its own reward.
**Age 57 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 58 (715 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 58 (715 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.

**Age 59 (716 TR)** — She identified it as camp fever on the second day and began the isolation protocols that the army officer had not heard of and was initially resistant to. She explained clearly why isolation was necessary. He allowed it after two days. Those two days probably cost two of the seven men who died. She has thought about how to explain this more quickly next time, which is the productive direction for the thought, and she returns to it when she is not too tired to think productively.
**Age 60 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 60 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 61 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 61 (718 TR)** — Orin Koring died after 43 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Orin Koring died (approx. age 59)

**Age 62 (719 TR)** — Her Pagaelin was better than the captive expected, which bought her thirty seconds of genuine surprise before they closed down again. She used those thirty seconds. Not for information — for observation. She learned more from watching someone decide not to answer than she had from the previous three interrogations.
**Age 63 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (675 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Orin Koring (soldier, age 16) **Age 18 (675 TR)** — The child came at term and did not breathe. She had been so close. week 38 **Age 19 (676 TR)** — The camp economy runs on the sutlers and everyone knows it, including the officers who would prefer not to acknowledge it. She extends credit carefully — who is reliable, who has family money, who is the kind of man who runs when things go wrong. She has been cheated precisely twice and recovered from both by knowing which sergeant to speak to. She is carrying the wages of four dead men in her ledger, which she will attempt to recover from their estates when the campaign ends, and which she is fairly certain she will not recover. **Age 19 (676 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would. **Age 20 (677 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 20 (677 TR)** — She burned a dinner so completely that it became a family saying. Her spouse ate around it heroically. **Age 21 (678 TR)** — There is a specific kind of problem that comes with being a woman in a company that has not fully decided how to handle women in a company. She handled it. She is not going to describe in detail how she handled it because the details are not the point — the point is that she handled it in a way that did not require the sergeant's intervention and did not damage her operational standing and produced a working arrangement with the person involved that, while not friendly, is functional. She has added this to her list of skills that are not in any training manual. **Age 22 (679 TR)** — The order was to burn the village. The people were Morgathian sympathisers, the officer said, which may even have been true. She refused in front of the column and said Larani's name when she did it. There was a silence. The officer did not repeat the order. She has never understood why. **Age 23 (680 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline. **Age 23 (680 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it. **Age 24 (681 TR)** — The Trail was Pagaelin territory in every practical sense regardless of what the maps said. She learned its rhythms — which stretches drew attention, which seasons were dangerous, which small gestures communicated respect without communicating weakness. **Age 24 (681 TR)** — She lost the child early — before she had said much aloud about it. She said nothing after, either. week 6 **Age 25 (682 TR)** — She learned to present herself as more useful than she was on any given day, because soldiers who appear less useful than the cost of feeding them get reassigned in ways that are worse. She learned to read the officer's mood at fifty yards and adjust accordingly. She learned which medical knowledge was actually useful in the field and which was the kind of thing you learned in training to feel prepared. The gap between those two categories is significant. **Age 26 (683 TR)** — She had been told that Larani answered prayer in extremity. She had not believed this with any conviction. On the slope above Erone she saw a man with a belly wound walk two hundred yards to the surgeon's tent under his own power. The surgeon confirmed what the wound implied. She has carried this carefully ever since, not sure what to do with it. **Age 26 (683 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 32 **Age 27 (684 TR)** — She drank more than was wise and behaved in ways she thought about for several days afterwards. **Age 28 (685 TR)** — She found a name carved somewhere — a wall, a beam, a stone — and thought about the hand that had made it. **Age 28 (685 TR)** — The child was lost before it had become real to anyone else. She marked it privately. week 4 **Age 29 (686 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 29 (686 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 12 **Age 30 (687 TR)** — The cover held but it was close. A merchant's wife asked her about a village she claimed to be from and knew it in a level of detail she didn't — specific names, specific streets. She deflected smoothly enough but the deflection was noticed; she saw it noticed. She completed the task and returned and asked her officer directly whether she needed to change the cover identity. He said not yet. She is keeping very careful track of what 'not yet' means in practice. **Age 30 (687 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 31 (688 TR)** — Necessity had long since made her skilled. She went beyond necessity. **Age 32 (689 TR)** — The mission had been going well — that was the part that was hardest to hear. Twelve people who thought they understood the situation. She filed it under: the thing you assume is working is the thing that kills you. **Age 33 (690 TR)** — She wrote off four debts this campaign. Two dead, one deserted, one simply gone. She adjusts the prices for everyone else accordingly, which is the honest way to run a sutler's operation: the cost of unrecoverable debt is distributed across the credit that does get repaid. She does not complain about this. It is a known cost of the trade. She does note the names carefully, because some of the deserters end up in other armies, and the road network is smaller than it appears. **Age 33 (690 TR)** — She led her family confidently in entirely the wrong direction. They did not find this out until they were well off course. They were eventually fine. **Age 34 (691 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 35 (692 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Arwen Alson (soldier) **Age 35 (692 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 36 (693 TR)** — The vow was made in private, to the sword-altar, in language she had not planned and could not quite reproduce afterward. She survived. She has been paying the debt ever since in ways she has not always been able to name — small acts, a certain quality of attention, a refusal of one particular easy choice, repeated. **Age 36 (693 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 37 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 37 (694 TR)** — The levy-men came through the village and her husband was among those taken. She had known it was a possibility. It did not make the morning after easier. **Age 38 (695 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 39 (696 TR)** — She is a widow visiting her sister's family, which is why she is on this road, which is why she has stopped at this inn, which is why she is asking about the road ahead to the next village. None of this is true. All of it holds. She has learned that the most sustainable cover is one that requires the minimum number of lies — the one that lets the truth do as much of the work as possible. **Age 39 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 40 (697 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 40 (697 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days. **Age 41 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 41 (698 TR)** — He came back. Thinner, quieter, with a look she could not quite read. But he came back. **Age 42 (699 TR)** — She heard the cry and had the choice that women always have in these moments: whether joining would help or whether she would be more useful as a witness, which is a calculation she makes in approximately half a second. She positioned herself where she could see the man being pursued, watched where he went, and when the officers arrived she described his route and his description clearly and they found him two streets over. She was thanked by the bailiff. She has been a useful witness in two similar situations. She considers this a form of civic participation she is good at. **Age 42 (699 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 43 (700 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 43 (700 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it. **Age 44 (701 TR)** — She was asked to deliver a package to a specific house in a town sixty miles out and note, on the way back, what she could observe about the movement of men and horses on the Tashal road. She delivered the package. She observed the movement. Her report contained three pieces of information her officer had not expected, which she knows because of how he reacted to those three pieces. She is aware she is useful in a specific way. She is deciding what to do with that awareness. **Age 44 (701 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 45 (702 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 45 (702 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes. **Age 46 (703 TR)** — Serving on the assize jury is not common for women but her holding qualifies her and the bailiff called her. She went. She listened more carefully than most jurors she observed, because she was aware that her presence was being watched by everyone in the room and that any appearance of confusion would be attributed to her sex rather than to the complexity of the case. The cases were not complex. She returned a clear verdict in each. The bailiff thanked her with the specific tone of a man who was expecting difficulty and didn't get it. **Age 46 (703 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 47 (704 TR)** — She was not trained for battlefield wounds. She was trained for illness, for fever, for childbirth. She adapted in the first campaign and has been adapting since. The military surgeons, when there are any, do not always know more than she does. Sometimes they know less. She does the work that needs doing and has stopped spending energy on the question of whether she is supposed to be the one doing it. **Age 47 (704 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself. **Age 48 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 49 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 49 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 50 (707 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 51 (708 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her. **Age 52 (709 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the lower leg or shin that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 52 (709 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day. **Age 53 (710 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. She paid the price. **Age 53 (710 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride. **Age 54 (711 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 54 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 55 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 56 (713 TR)** — She kept to it when most would have stopped. The discipline became its own reward. **Age 57 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 58 (715 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option. **Age 58 (715 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took. **Age 59 (716 TR)** — She identified it as camp fever on the second day and began the isolation protocols that the army officer had not heard of and was initially resistant to. She explained clearly why isolation was necessary. He allowed it after two days. Those two days probably cost two of the seven men who died. She has thought about how to explain this more quickly next time, which is the productive direction for the thought, and she returns to it when she is not too tired to think productively. **Age 60 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 60 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 61 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 61 (718 TR)** — Orin Koring died after 43 years together. The silence in the house that followed was unlike any other silence. Orin Koring died (approx. age 59) **Age 62 (719 TR)** — Her Pagaelin was better than the captive expected, which bought her thirty seconds of genuine surprise before they closed down again. She used those thirty seconds. Not for information — for observation. She learned more from watching someone decide not to answer than she had from the previous three interrogations. **Age 63 (720 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 25 (688 TR)** — Her child produced a phrase so perfectly wrong that the whole household adopted it. Visitors were puzzled. The family didn't explain.
**Age 25 (688 TR)** — She lost Branna Tarkin — still a child, not yet grown. Branna Tarkin died (age 4)
**Age 26 (689 TR)** — A feud that began with a killing reached into her household. She had no say in the quarrel itself, but the consequences arrived at her door regardless. Feud with Loran Wolkin
**Age 27 (690 TR)** — She was among the crowd when the sentence was carried out. The silence that fell at the end was not what she expected.
**Age 27 (690 TR)** — Her child carried something home — a dog, a cat, a bird with a broken wing — that no one had said yes to. It lived in the house for a long time.
**Age 28 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 29 (692 TR)** — She woke and the cradle was empty. The baby had been there at the third-hour feed. She would never know, in a precise sense, what had happened to her child. She would spend years constructing theories, each one worse than the last, none of them helpful.
**Age 30 (693 TR)** — The lord's deer were fat and the larder thin; she took the risk.
**Age 30 (693 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Valith Tarkin hired out (age 8)
**Age 31 (694 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 31 (694 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular.

**Age 32 (695 TR)** — She was not trained for battlefield wounds. She was trained for illness, for fever, for childbirth. She adapted in the first campaign and has been adapting since. She does the work that needs doing and has stopped spending energy on the question of whether she is supposed to be the one doing it.

**Age 33 (696 TR)** — She waited in line. She was next. The thing she had been waiting for was gone.
**Age 33 (696 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Zara Tarkin lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Zara Tarkin born (female)

**Age 34 (697 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. **Age 34 (697 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 34 (697 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time. Osric Tarkin died (age 2)

**Age 36 (699 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Berith Tarkin lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Berith Tarkin born (female)

**Age 37 (700 TR)** — The fever broke on the fourth day. The Esolani who had been attending her — a woman her own age, mild-faced, entirely competent — told her she was past the worst and brought broth. She had not expected to survive. She had a different relationship with the Peonian church after that.
**Age 37 (700 TR)** — Her husband was ill for months. She nursed him, managed the household, and kept the worry mostly to herself. He recovered. She had not been sure he would.

**Age 38 (701 TR)** — She was asked to deliver a package to a specific house in a town sixty miles out and note, on the way back, what she could observe about the movement of men and horses on the Tashal road. **Age 38 (701 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Antor Tarkin lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Antor Tarkin born (male)

**Age 39 (702 TR)** — The cover held but it was close. She deflected smoothly enough but the deflection was noticed; she saw it noticed. She completed the task and returned and asked her officer directly whether she needed to change the cover identity. He said not yet. She is keeping very careful track of what 'not yet' means in practice.
**Age 39 (702 TR)** — The birth took longer than expected. Noral Tarkin lived. She spent the next three days learning who she now was. week 40 Noral Tarkin born (male)
**Age 40 (703 TR)** — She was ill enough to frighten the household. She recovered, though the recovery took longer than the illness. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 40 (703 TR)** — She lost the child before its time. She did not speak of it openly. week 19
**Age 40 (703 TR)** — Her son Idris Tarkin married Mira Wolkin. Idris Tarkin married Mira Wolkin (soldier, age 16)
**Age 41 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 41 (704 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 41 (704 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Idris Tarkin's child born (male, Idris Tarkin age 21)
**Age 42 (705 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 43 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (706 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 43 (706 TR)** — Her daughter Valith Tarkin married Malik Malkin. Valith Tarkin married Malik Malkin (soldier, age 20)
**Age 43 (706 TR)** — Tovan Tarkin left to make her own way. Tovan Tarkin left home (age 20)
**Age 43 (706 TR)** — She lost Noral Tarkin — still a child, not yet grown. Noral Tarkin died (age 4)

**Age 44 (707 TR)** — There was a specific moment — she can identify it — when she did something in front of enough witnesses that the question of whether she belonged there stopped being asked. Not permanently, not universally; the question is never permanently retired. But in that unit, in that company, among those particular men, there was a before and an after. She did not celebrate. She did the next thing, and the thing after that. She is aware that the bar was set differently for her than for the men around her and that clearing it was not the same as having it set fairly. She cleared it anyway.

**Age 44 (707 TR)** — Something became funny in a way that built and built until everyone was helpless with it. She remembered it for the rest of her life without being able to say what exactly had been funny.
**Age 44 (707 TR)** — Idris Tarkin left to make her own way. Idris Tarkin left home (age 24)
**Age 44 (707 TR)** — Her son Tovan Tarkin married Halla Eding. Tovan Tarkin married Halla Eding (soldier, age 19)
**Age 45 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 45 (708 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself.
**Age 45 (708 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 46 (709 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 46 (709 TR)** — Her child was terrified of something that posed no threat — a goose, a shadow on the wall, a particular sound. She managed the situation with patience and some difficulty.
**Age 47 (710 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 47 (710 TR)** — Her child fell into the water. She was in after them before she thought about it. They were both soaked and frightened and unhurt.
**Age 47 (710 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 48 (711 TR)** — A deathbed she attended left her with unanswered questions about her own beliefs.
**Age 48 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 49 (712 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 50 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 50 (713 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride.
**Age 51 (714 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 51 (714 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 51 (714 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 52 (715 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 52 (715 TR)** — Her daughter Zara Tarkin married Noric Kaling. Zara Tarkin married Noric Kaling (soldier, age 18)
**Age 52 (715 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Arden Tarkin's illegitimate child born (male, Arden Tarkin age 17)
**Age 52 (715 TR)** — Her daughter Berith Tarkin married Ranulf Millward. Berith Tarkin married Ranulf Millward (artisan, age 16)
**Age 53 (716 TR)** — A quarrel turned violent; she left someone badly hurt.
**Age 53 (716 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 54 (717 TR)** — Through deed or patience, her name was spoken without scorn once more.
**Age 54 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 54 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 54 (717 TR)** — Arden Tarkin left to make her own way. Arden Tarkin left home (age 19)
**Age 55 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 55 (718 TR)** — He died in the way that men die on the heath — quickly, and with no time given for preparation. She has been continuing since then. Continuing is not the same as being well. It is the thing before being well, if being well eventually comes.
**Age 55 (718 TR)** — Her son Antor Tarkin married Edda Smith. Antor Tarkin married Edda Smith (artisan, age 16)
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — Idris Tarkin lost Warin Tarkin. She watched her child grieve and did not know how to carry both sorrows at once. Idris Tarkin's child died (age 15)
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — Tovan Tarkin's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Tovan Tarkin's child died (age 0)
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (719 TR)** — Antor Tarkin left to make her own way. Antor Tarkin left home (age 18)
**Age 57 (720 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Aldis Bransen
**Age 57 (720 TR)** — Her daughter Arden Tarkin married Noral Horing. Arden Tarkin married Noral Horing (soldier, age 19)
