She can still describe all six errors in sequence. She will never make any of them.
**Age 22 (691 TR)** — A woman she had never seen before paid for her meal and was gone before she could object.
**Age 23 (692 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark.
**Age 24 (693 TR)** — She finished something and found she was satisfied with it. That was rarer than it should have been.
**Age 25 (694 TR)** — A military posting for a woman of the cloth was unusual. She received it as a mark of trust and declined to read it any other way.
**Age 26 (695 TR)** — She was certain of the weather and entirely mistaken. She said nothing about it. Others said enough.
**Age 27 (696 TR)** — She could not bring herself to go inside. She sat and watched the light change until it had finished doing so.
**Age 28 (697 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.

**Age 29 (698 TR)** — She would not have known him except that he laughed — the same short laugh, the kind that means nothing is funny. The cutpurse from the Endakil festival. She watched him move through the crowd with professional ease and felt something cold and very precise in her chest that was not exactly anger.

**Age 31 (700 TR)** — The morning begins with whatever the master left unfinished, which is the rule at every workshop and the reason early mornings in every workshop smell the same. She has been doing this for four years. She knows the rhythm of the place — which tasks come in sequence, which the master trusts her with unsupervised, which she is still not permitted to touch. The permitted list grows slowly. She measures her progress by what she is allowed to touch alone.

**Age 32 (701 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.

**Age 33 (702 TR)** — She studied from a written wordlist compiled by someone who had died at Osel, supplemented by conversations with three different informants who disagreed on half the vocabulary. The disagreements were the most informative part. Language is politics in concentrated form.
**Age 34 (703 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Narith
**Age 35 (704 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 36 (705 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 37 (706 TR)** — She was accepted into a noble household as a lady-in-waiting or steward's assistant.
**Age 38 (707 TR)** — She walked to the shrine. The journey changed something — not in the way she expected, which was perhaps what it was supposed to do.
**Age 39 (708 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 40 (709 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 41 (710 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 42 (711 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Aleth

**Age 43 (712 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 44 (713 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 45 (714 TR)** — She opened her door to someone who needed it.
**Age 46 (715 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 47 (716 TR)** — She lost a small thing she had carried for years. She did not speak of it but felt its absence for a long time.
**Age 48 (717 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 49 (718 TR)** — She went down in full view of several people. She stood up, straightened herself, and continued. No one mentioned it. She mentioned it to herself regularly.
**Age 50 (719 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 51 (720 TR)** — The debt that had dogged her was finally cleared.

narrative: **Age 18 (693 TR)** — A woman she knew was killed — a neighbour, a trader she dealt with, someone whose face she knew. It was the kind of death that made everyone quieter for a while.
**Age 19 (694 TR)** — She was who the village came to when there was no one else.

**Age 20 (695 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.

**Age 23 (698 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.

**Age 24 (699 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them.
**Age 25 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 26 (701 TR)** — The morning begins with whatever the master left unfinished, which is the rule at every workshop and the reason early mornings in every workshop smell the same. She has been doing this for four years. She knows the rhythm of the place — which tasks come in sequence, which the master trusts her with unsupervised, which she is still not permitted to touch. The permitted list grows slowly. She measures her progress by what she is allowed to touch alone.
**Age 27 (702 TR)** — She stayed kneeling long after the prescribed time. Not devotion. Just an absence of reason to move.

**Age 28 (703 TR)** — The mission had been going well — that was the part that was hardest to hear. Twelve people who thought they understood the situation. She filed it under: the thing you assume is working is the thing that kills you.
**Age 29 (704 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 30 (705 TR)** — She was ill enough to frighten the household. She recovered, though the recovery took longer than the illness. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 31 (706 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection.
**Age 32 (707 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 33 (708 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 34 (709 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 35 (710 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.

**Age 36 (711 TR)** — The Ebasethe arrived before dawn and stayed until the crisis passed. She had sent for her because the midwife said to. She had not expected it to matter. The child was alive. She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal.

**Age 37 (712 TR)** — A young squire asked her before the Tirannon vigil whether she was afraid of dying. She said no. The answer came without hesitation. She examined it later and decided it was probably true.
**Age 38 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 39 (714 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this.

**Age 40 (715 TR)** — Months of close reading: liturgical texts, canonical precedent, the letters of the senior clergy. The work was slow and the illumination on the margins of the oldest manuscripts was occasionally distracting.
**Age 41 (716 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 42 (717 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the upper arm that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar.
**Age 43 (718 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.
**Age 44 (719 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 45 (720 TR)** — A named posting — chaplain to a Lady of Paladins chapter. The knights watched her carefully for the first month to see how she would take the work.

She had a different understanding of the Peonian church after that — less abstract, more personal. **Age 37 (712 TR)** — A young squire asked her before the Tirannon vigil whether she was afraid of dying. She said no. The answer came without hesitation. She examined it later and decided it was probably true. **Age 38 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 39 (714 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this. **Age 40 (715 TR)** — Months of close reading: liturgical texts, canonical precedent, the letters of the senior clergy. The work was slow and the illumination on the margins of the oldest manuscripts was occasionally distracting. **Age 41 (716 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete. **Age 42 (717 TR)** — An accident or fall left her with a wound that did not heal cleanly. A minor wound to the upper arm that healed cleanly, leaving only a scar. **Age 43 (718 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon. **Age 44 (719 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 45 (720 TR)** — A named posting — chaplain to a Lady of Paladins chapter. The knights watched her carefully for the first month to see how she would take the work.
name: Halla

narrative: **Age 18 (699 TR)** — Patronage came with influence and obligations. She accepted both and managed the latter with care.
**Age 19 (700 TR)** — She was who the village came to when there was no one else.

**Age 20 (701 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.
**Age 21 (702 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 22 (703 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this.
**Age 23 (704 TR)** — She promised to do something she expected to regret. Keeping it cost her, and the cost was worth it.
**Age 24 (705 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 25 (706 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Edda (clergy)

**Age 26 (707 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them.
**Age 27 (708 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.
**Age 28 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 29 (710 TR)** — The morning begins with whatever the master left unfinished, which is the rule at every workshop and the reason early mornings in every workshop smell the same. She has been doing this for four years. She knows the rhythm of the place — which tasks come in sequence, which the master trusts her with unsupervised, which she is still not permitted to touch. The permitted list grows slowly. She measures her progress by what she is allowed to touch alone.
**Age 30 (711 TR)** — She arrived too early. She walked around the block several times before going in.
**Age 31 (712 TR)** — Dancing was expected of her from childhood. She was accomplished.
**Age 32 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 33 (714 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.
**Age 34 (715 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.

**Age 35 (716 TR)** — She had known him for years before she realised she would do something difficult for him if he asked. He had, she suspected, reached the same conclusion about her. Edda became a close friend
**Age 36 (717 TR)** — Her long years of service were noticed and formally recognised.
**Age 37 (718 TR)** — She was ill enough to frighten the household. She recovered, though the recovery took longer than the illness. The illness passed without lasting harm.
**Age 38 (719 TR)** — She was accepted into a noble household as a lady-in-waiting or steward's assistant.
**Age 39 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

name: Varath

narrative: **Age 18 (705 TR)** — She ruined two yards of figured cloth that the master had been saving for a specific commission. The sound the mistress made when she saw it was not a word. She sat with that sound for a long time afterward — not out of guilt exactly, but out of the specific determination to understand precisely what had gone wrong at every stage of the decision and execution so that she could guarantee she never made those specific errors again. She can still describe all six errors in sequence. She will never make any of them.
**Age 19 (706 TR)** — She watched her fire die. She had somewhere to be. She stayed until the last coal went grey.

**Age 20 (707 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.
**Age 21 (708 TR)** — She was given in marriage, binding two households together. Married Brand (clergy, age 19)
**Age 21 (708 TR)** — Another loss before it had become a child in any way others would recognise. She grieved it nonetheless. week 19
**Age 22 (709 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 23 (710 TR)** — She was accepted into a noble household as a lady-in-waiting or steward's assistant.
**Age 24 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 25 (712 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 25 (712 TR)** — Her husband snored like something structural was collapsing. She learned to sleep through it. She never told him how long this took.
**Age 26 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 26 (713 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Wulf. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40
**Age 27 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 27 (714 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Koral. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40
**Age 28 (715 TR)** — She was seized by laughter at the worst possible time. She managed to contain it. Barely.
**Age 28 (715 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself.
**Age 29 (716 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 29 (716 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Aldric. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40

**Age 30 (717 TR)** — The morning begins with whatever the master left unfinished, which is the rule at every workshop and the reason early mornings in every workshop smell the same. She has been doing this for four years. She knows the rhythm of the place — which tasks come in sequence, which the master trusts her with unsupervised, which she is still not permitted to touch. The permitted list grows slowly. She measures her progress by what she is allowed to touch alone.
**Age 30 (717 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.
**Age 31 (718 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 31 (718 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 32 (719 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 32 (719 TR)** — The child lived. She could see from the beginning that they would need more — more care, more patience, more of everything she had. She did not hesitate. week 40 — lame
**Age 33 (720 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read.
**Age 33 (720 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 30

name: Nora

narrative: **Age 18 (711 TR)** — The magistrate heard her case and found against her, which surprised her until she learned what the other party had paid. She had known in the abstract that the law was purchasable in this city. She knew it differently after that.
**Age 19 (712 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Laric

**Age 20 (713 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.

**Age 21 (714 TR)** — She watched the mistress take apart a piece she had thought was finished and rebuild the central section from scratch in forty minutes. It was the same result, technically — the dimensions matched, the specifications were met. But the new version had something the first version had not had, something she could see when they were side by side but cannot yet name precisely. The mistress saw her looking. She did not explain. She said: when you can say what the difference is, you are ready for the examination.
**Age 22 (715 TR)** — She repaired something that belonged to her neighbour while they were away. No one ever mentioned it.
**Age 23 (716 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 24 (717 TR)** — There were rats. She dealt with them methodically. The cat was no help whatsoever.
**Age 25 (718 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 26 (719 TR)** — Patronage came with influence and obligations. She accepted both and managed the latter with care.

**Age 27 (720 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them.

name: Tarath

narrative: **Age 18 (717 TR)** — She lost a small thing she had carried for years. She did not speak of it but felt its absence for a long time.
**Age 19 (718 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.

**Age 20 (719 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.
**Age 21 (720 TR)** — She married. She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Hadric (clergy, age 21)

She had opinions about the match that were not the primary consideration in making it. Married Hadric (clergy, age 21)
name: Neda

narrative: **Age 18 (665 TR)** — She saw a situation developing that she might have done something about. She walked on. The question of whether she was right to has not fully settled.
**Age 19 (666 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this.

**Age 20 (667 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.
**Age 21 (668 TR)** — A traveller asked her name, repeated it carefully as if memorising it, then went on his way.
**Age 22 (669 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.
**Age 23 (670 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Vara
**Age 24 (671 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted.

**Age 25 (672 TR)** — The morning begins with whatever the master left unfinished, which is the rule at every workshop and the reason early mornings in every workshop smell the same. She has been doing this for four years. She knows the rhythm of the place — which tasks come in sequence, which the master trusts her with unsupervised, which she is still not permitted to touch. The permitted list grows slowly. She measures her progress by what she is allowed to touch alone.
**Age 26 (673 TR)** — The sickness passed but left her diminished for months — thinner, slower to tire, more careful about cold. The illness passed without lasting harm.

**Age 27 (674 TR)** — The mission had been going well — that was the part that was hardest to hear. Twelve people who thought they understood the situation. She filed it under: the thing you assume is working is the thing that kills you.
**Age 28 (675 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Calla Keenscale (merchant)

**Age 29 (676 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 31 (678 TR)** — A cutting she stuck in the ground as an afterthought came to something. She showed it to everyone who came by.
**Age 32 (679 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.

**Age 33 (680 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice.
**Age 34 (681 TR)** — A woman she knew was killed — a neighbour, a trader she dealt with, someone whose face she knew. It was the kind of death that made everyone quieter for a while.
**Age 35 (682 TR)** — The mark was permanent — ear, thumb, or cheek. She would carry it the rest of her life.
**Age 36 (683 TR)** — A named posting — chaplain to a Lady of Paladins chapter. The knights watched her carefully for the first month to see how she would take the work.
**Age 37 (684 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (685 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark.
**Age 39 (686 TR)** — Patronage came with influence and obligations. She accepted both and managed the latter with care.
**Age 40 (687 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection.
**Age 41 (688 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 42 (689 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 43 (690 TR)** — Her long years of service were noticed and formally recognised.
**Age 44 (691 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done.

**Age 45 (692 TR)** — The arrangement was explained to her in pleasant terms by a woman she had not seen before. A contribution to neighbourhood safety. The woman smiled throughout. She paid and told herself it was the cost of doing business in this city, which was true but not the whole truth.
**Age 46 (693 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 47 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 48 (695 TR)** — She lit a candle for someone she had not thought of in years. She did not examine this.
**Age 49 (696 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline.
**Age 50 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 51 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 52 (699 TR)** — A deathbed she attended left her with unanswered questions about her own beliefs.
**Age 53 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 54 (701 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 55 (702 TR)** — She walked to the shrine. The journey changed something — not in the way she expected, which was perhaps what it was supposed to do.
**Age 56 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 57 (704 TR)** — A tune she caught half of at a festival lodged in her head for the rest of the season.

**Age 58 (705 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it.

**Age 59 (706 TR)** — A child asked her why Larani did not protect good people from bad things. She gave the proper answer. The child nodded and walked away. She felt the inadequacy of the proper answer for the rest of the day.
**Age 60 (707 TR)** — She had learned to be careful. This time she had not been careful enough. The look on the stranger's face told her everything.
**Age 61 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 63 (710 TR)** — She began writing — not because anyone asked, but because it needed doing.
**Age 64 (711 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Reda

**Age 65 (712 TR)** — She had managed the household, the accounts, the children's marriages, and the succession of a substantial property for four decades, and she stopped. What she found on the other side of it was time. She had forgotten what time felt like when it belonged to her. She is still learning.
**Age 66 (713 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Lara
**Age 67 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 68 (715 TR)** — She set her affairs in order with the same methodical care she had brought to everything. She felt lighter for it.
**Age 69 (716 TR)** — She used the last of something she had been saving and felt the loss of it more than made sense.
**Age 70 (717 TR)** — She let go of what she could no longer do and found she still had plenty left.
**Age 71 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 72 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 73 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage. **Age 21 (668 TR)** — A traveller asked her name, repeated it carefully as if memorising it, then went on his way. **Age 22 (669 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon. **Age 23 (670 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Vara **Age 24 (671 TR)** — A finger was pointed at her — whether justly or not, her name was tainted. **Age 25 (672 TR)** — The morning begins with whatever the master left unfinished, which is the rule at every workshop and the reason early mornings in every workshop smell the same. She has been doing this for four years. She knows the rhythm of the place — which tasks come in sequence, which the master trusts her with unsupervised, which she is still not permitted to touch. The permitted list grows slowly. She measures her progress by what she is allowed to touch alone. **Age 26 (673 TR)** — The sickness passed but left her diminished for months — thinner, slower to tire, more careful about cold. The illness passed without lasting harm. **Age 27 (674 TR)** — The mission had been going well — that was the part that was hardest to hear. Twelve people who thought they understood the situation. She filed it under: the thing you assume is working is the thing that kills you. **Age 28 (675 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Calla Keenscale (merchant) **Age 29 (676 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 31 (678 TR)** — A cutting she stuck in the ground as an afterthought came to something. She showed it to everyone who came by. **Age 32 (679 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it. **Age 33 (680 TR)** — The other apprentice got the easier tasks, the better hours, the master's patience. She got the corrections and the night work and the jobs that were beneath notice if they went right and humiliating if they went wrong. She understood that this was partly preference and partly a test — the masters who treat their female apprentices harder are testing whether they will leave, because the easy ones always leave and the hard ones stay and become the best practitioners. She stayed. She is still deciding whether the theory justifies the practice. **Age 34 (681 TR)** — A woman she knew was killed — a neighbour, a trader she dealt with, someone whose face she knew. It was the kind of death that made everyone quieter for a while. **Age 35 (682 TR)** — The mark was permanent — ear, thumb, or cheek. She would carry it the rest of her life. **Age 36 (683 TR)** — A named posting — chaplain to a Lady of Paladins chapter. The knights watched her carefully for the first month to see how she would take the work. **Age 37 (684 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 38 (685 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark. **Age 39 (686 TR)** — Patronage came with influence and obligations. She accepted both and managed the latter with care. **Age 40 (687 TR)** — Loss drove her inward; she spent months in solitary reflection. **Age 41 (688 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet. **Age 42 (689 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 43 (690 TR)** — Her long years of service were noticed and formally recognised. **Age 44 (691 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done. **Age 45 (692 TR)** — The arrangement was explained to her in pleasant terms by a woman she had not seen before. A contribution to neighbourhood safety. The woman smiled throughout. She paid and told herself it was the cost of doing business in this city, which was true but not the whole truth. **Age 46 (693 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 47 (694 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 48 (695 TR)** — She lit a candle for someone she had not thought of in years. She did not examine this. **Age 49 (696 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline. **Age 50 (697 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 51 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 52 (699 TR)** — A deathbed she attended left her with unanswered questions about her own beliefs. **Age 53 (700 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 54 (701 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 55 (702 TR)** — She walked to the shrine. The journey changed something — not in the way she expected, which was perhaps what it was supposed to do. **Age 56 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 57 (704 TR)** — A tune she caught half of at a festival lodged in her head for the rest of the season. **Age 58 (705 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it. **Age 59 (706 TR)** — A child asked her why Larani did not protect good people from bad things. She gave the proper answer. The child nodded and walked away. She felt the inadequacy of the proper answer for the rest of the day. **Age 60 (707 TR)** — She had learned to be careful. This time she had not been careful enough. The look on the stranger's face told her everything. **Age 61 (708 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 63 (710 TR)** — She began writing — not because anyone asked, but because it needed doing. **Age 64 (711 TR)** — They parted on bad terms over something that had started as almost nothing. She replayed the conversation for months. Fell out with Reda **Age 65 (712 TR)** — She had managed the household, the accounts, the children's marriages, and the succession of a substantial property for four decades, and she stopped. What she found on the other side of it was time. She had forgotten what time felt like when it belonged to her. She is still learning. **Age 66 (713 TR)** — She chose to end the enmity. It cost her pride and gained her something she could not name. Reconciled with Lara **Age 67 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 68 (715 TR)** — She set her affairs in order with the same methodical care she had brought to everything. She felt lighter for it. **Age 69 (716 TR)** — She used the last of something she had been saving and felt the loss of it more than made sense. **Age 70 (717 TR)** — She let go of what she could no longer do and found she still had plenty left. **Age 71 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 72 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 73 (720 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
name: Delia

narrative: **Age 18 (671 TR)** — Patronage came with influence and obligations. She accepted both and managed the latter with care.
**Age 19 (672 TR)** — She found a sealed letter tucked into something she had bought secondhand. She kept it for a year before burning it unread.

**Age 20 (673 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.

**Age 21 (674 TR)** — She completed the commission and the merchant accepted it and paid the full rate. The merchant did not know she was the apprentice rather than the journeyman. She decided not to clarify this, which is an ethical position she has revisited several times since. Her conclusion: the work was good enough. The classification of who did it is a guild matter, not a customer matter. She told the master. The master said: correct.

**Age 22 (675 TR)** — The mission had been going well — that was the part that was hardest to hear. Twelve people who thought they understood the situation. She filed it under: the thing you assume is working is the thing that kills you.
**Age 23 (676 TR)** — A secret came to her unbidden. Keeping it was both burden and power. Confided in Tavin

**Age 24 (677 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 25 (678 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this.
**Age 26 (679 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 27 (680 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 28 (681 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.

**Age 29 (682 TR)** — The poison was in the wine, and she had drunk only a little before the taste turned her. Or perhaps she had not drunk enough. For a week she did not know which and spent the time in careful consideration of every decision she had made in the past two years that could have made her worth killing.
**Age 30 (683 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 31 (684 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.

**Age 32 (685 TR)** — She studied from a written wordlist compiled by someone who had died at Osel, supplemented by conversations with three different informants who disagreed on half the vocabulary. The disagreements were the most informative part. Language is politics in concentrated form.

**Age 33 (686 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them.
**Age 34 (687 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 35 (688 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 36 (689 TR)** — She was accepted into a noble household as a lady-in-waiting or steward's assistant.

**Age 38 (691 TR)** — Months of close reading: liturgical texts, canonical precedent, the letters of the senior clergy. The work was slow and the illumination on the margins of the oldest manuscripts was occasionally distracting.
**Age 39 (692 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Terath (clergy)
**Age 40 (693 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Davin
**Age 41 (694 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 42 (695 TR)** — She found a place that was simply right and made a habit of sitting there.
**Age 43 (696 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (697 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 45 (698 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 46 (699 TR)** — She walked to the shrine. The journey changed something — not in the way she expected, which was perhaps what it was supposed to do.
**Age 47 (700 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 48 (701 TR)** — She came back to herself slowly, and not entirely the same self that had been taken down.
**Age 49 (702 TR)** — She was who the village came to when there was no one else.
**Age 50 (703 TR)** — Refusing to be a victim again, she found someone willing to teach her.
**Age 51 (704 TR)** — She gave to the church or the poor. It mattered to her that it was done.
**Age 52 (705 TR)** — Determined to regain her strength, she threw herself into physical discipline.
**Age 53 (706 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 54 (707 TR)** — A deathbed she attended left her with unanswered questions about her own beliefs.
**Age 55 (708 TR)** — A friend — companion, neighbour, or kin — died badly. The loss left a mark.
**Age 56 (709 TR)** — She travelled further from home than ever before, seeing new faces and hearing new tongues.
**Age 57 (710 TR)** — Her long years of service were noticed and formally recognised.
**Age 58 (711 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 59 (712 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 60 (713 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 61 (714 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 62 (715 TR)** — She woke to a sound she could not account for. She lay still and listened until it stopped. Nothing explained it in the morning.
**Age 63 (716 TR)** — She began writing — not because anyone asked, but because it needed doing.
**Age 64 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 66 (719 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 67 (720 TR)** — She was corrected on a prayer she had said since childhood. She had been saying a word wrong all that time.

name: Rira

narrative: **Age 18 (677 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Roric (clergy, age 24)
**Age 18 (677 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected.
**Age 19 (678 TR)** — She was completely sure of something and incorrect. She found this out in front of other people.
**Age 19 (678 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself.

**Age 20 (679 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.

**Age 21 (680 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them.
**Age 21 (680 TR)** — The child was lost before it had become real to anyone else. She marked it privately. week 4
**Age 22 (681 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read.

**Age 23 (682 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it.
**Age 23 (682 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Rarith. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40
**Age 24 (683 TR)** — A fever kept her bedridden for weeks; she emerged gaunt but alive. The illness left a lasting mark: stamina −1.
**Age 24 (683 TR)** — She bore a child. Alara came into the world healthy. week 40
**Age 25 (684 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 25 (684 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days.
**Age 26 (685 TR)** — She received kindness from a direction she had not anticipated. She thought about it for a long time.
**Age 26 (685 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it.
**Age 27 (686 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway.
**Age 27 (686 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride.
**Age 28 (687 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 28 (687 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Noral — lived. week 40
**Age 29 (688 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this.
**Age 29 (688 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 37
**Age 30 (689 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question.
**Age 30 (689 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Harlan. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40
**Age 30 (689 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time.
**Age 31 (690 TR)** — She was who the village came to when there was no one else.
**Age 31 (690 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 32 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 32 (691 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Pedar. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40
**Age 32 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 33 (692 TR)** — She ruined two yards of figured cloth that the master had been saving for a specific commission. The sound the mistress made when she saw it was not a word. She sat with that sound for a long time afterward — not out of guilt exactly, but out of the specific determination to understand precisely what had gone wrong at every stage of the decision and execution so that she could guarantee she never made those specific errors again. She can still describe all six errors in sequence. She will never make any of them.
**Age 33 (692 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 34 (693 TR)** — Rumour or accusation — fair or unfair — put her standing at risk.
**Age 34 (693 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 35 (694 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.
**Age 35 (694 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Alara fostered (age 11)
**Age 36 (695 TR)** — The gossip thinned. People forgot, or found newer griefs to occupy them. She was not forgiven so much as left alone.
**Age 37 (696 TR)** — Something that had always been there stopped. She noticed the quiet before she identified what had gone.
**Age 37 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 37 (696 TR)** — She lost Pedar — still a child, not yet grown.
**Age 38 (697 TR)** — She promised to do something she expected to regret. Keeping it cost her, and the cost was worth it.
**Age 39 (698 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting.
**Age 39 (698 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it.
**Age 39 (698 TR)** — Her daughter Rarith married Horic Elman.
**Age 39 (698 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Rarith's child born (female, Rarith age 16)
**Age 40 (699 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest.
**Age 41 (700 TR)** — She delivered an apology she had been composing for years. The other person was surprised and then moved.
**Age 41 (700 TR)** — Another loss before it had become a child in any way others would recognise. She grieved it nonetheless. week 20
**Age 42 (701 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Geira. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40
**Age 43 (702 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 44 (703 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 44 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 44 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 45 (704 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 45 (704 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it.
**Age 46 (705 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 46 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.

**Age 47 (706 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was.
**Age 47 (706 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left.
**Age 47 (706 TR)** — Her daughter Alara married Lucan.
**Age 48 (707 TR)** — The journey required more of her than she had expected and gave back more than she had hoped.
**Age 49 (708 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 50 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 50 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 51 (710 TR)** — Her mother had not wanted to go. She had insisted, because the alternative was managing a dying woman in three rooms with two children underfoot. The Peonian house took her without fuss. She visited every day. Her mother died in spring, quietly, with a sister holding her hand because she had arrived twenty minutes too late.
**Age 51 (710 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular.
**Age 51 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 52 (711 TR)** — The approach was made in person, by a woman she had never seen, who knew things about her that no one had any business knowing. The price was stated plainly. She paid it. She is still paying it. She has not yet determined who is behind the approach, which tells her they are very good at this.
**Age 52 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.

**Age 53 (712 TR)** — She studied from a written wordlist compiled by someone who had died at Osel, supplemented by conversations with three different informants who disagreed on half the vocabulary. The disagreements were the most informative part. Language is politics in concentrated form.
**Age 53 (712 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day.
**Age 53 (712 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Tera's illegitimate child born (male, Tera age 20)

**Age 54 (713 TR)** — She completed the commission and the merchant accepted it and paid the full rate. The merchant did not know she was the apprentice rather than the journeyman. She decided not to clarify this, which is an ethical position she has revisited several times since. Her conclusion: the work was good enough. The classification of who did it is a guild matter, not a customer matter. She told the master. The master said: correct.
**Age 54 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 54 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 54 (713 TR)** — Rarith's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Rarith's child died (age 0)
**Age 55 (714 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 55 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.

**Age 56 (715 TR)** — Months of close reading: liturgical texts, canonical precedent, the letters of the senior clergy. The work was slow and the illumination on the margins of the oldest manuscripts was occasionally distracting.
**Age 56 (715 TR)** — Her daughter Tera married Rhoric Voring.
**Age 56 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 56 (715 TR)** — Tera left to make her own way.
**Age 57 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 57 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 57 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 57 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 57 (716 TR)** — Tera's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Tera's child died (age 1)
**Age 58 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued.
**Age 58 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 59 (718 TR)** — The mark was permanent — ear, thumb, or cheek. She would carry it the rest of her life.
**Age 59 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
**Age 60 (719 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Aela
**Age 61 (720 TR)** — She had learned to be careful. This time she had not been careful enough. The look on the stranger's face told her everything.
**Age 61 (720 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
narrativeText: **Age 18 (677 TR)** — She entered her husband's household. The first months were an exercise in understanding the rules of a different world. Married Roric (clergy, age 24) **Age 18 (677 TR)** — The match her family arranged proved more fortunate than she had expected. **Age 19 (678 TR)** — She was completely sure of something and incorrect. She found this out in front of other people. **Age 19 (678 TR)** — She found something her husband had kept that she had not known about. It was ordinary. She was more curious about why he had kept it than about the thing itself. **Age 20 (679 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage. **Age 21 (680 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them. **Age 21 (680 TR)** — The child was lost before it had become real to anyone else. She marked it privately. week 4 **Age 22 (681 TR)** — She grieved thoroughly and privately. When she came through it she was quieter than she had been, and harder to read. **Age 23 (682 TR)** — She noticed the lightness at her belt on the way home and knew. A market crowd, a moment of distraction. She had been careful. It had not been enough. She counted what she still had left and did not dwell on it. **Age 23 (682 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Rarith. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 **Age 24 (683 TR)** — A fever kept her bedridden for weeks; she emerged gaunt but alive. The illness left a lasting mark: stamina −1. **Age 24 (683 TR)** — She bore a child. Alara came into the world healthy. week 40 **Age 25 (684 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete. **Age 25 (684 TR)** — She and her husband argued about something entirely trivial. Neither of them could remember exactly what it was. The silence lasted four days. **Age 26 (685 TR)** — She received kindness from a direction she had not anticipated. She thought about it for a long time. **Age 26 (685 TR)** — Her child attempted a household task without help or permission. They were very proud. She let them be proud while she quietly fixed it. **Age 27 (686 TR)** — She found companionship elsewhere. The risks were real, and she took them anyway. **Age 27 (686 TR)** — She discovered her husband had a skill she had never known about. She had lived with him for years. She watched him demonstrate it with quiet pride. **Age 28 (687 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 28 (687 TR)** — The birth was hard and left her weakened for months. But the child — Noral — lived. week 40 **Age 29 (688 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this. **Age 29 (688 TR)** — The child came too still. She had carried it through the quickening, had felt it move, and then nothing. week 37 **Age 30 (689 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question. **Age 30 (689 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Harlan. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 **Age 30 (689 TR)** — She lost the infant before it had lived. She did not speak of it for a long time. **Age 31 (690 TR)** — She was who the village came to when there was no one else. **Age 31 (690 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 32 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 32 (691 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Pedar. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 **Age 32 (691 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 33 (692 TR)** — She ruined two yards of figured cloth that the master had been saving for a specific commission. The sound the mistress made when she saw it was not a word. She sat with that sound for a long time afterward — not out of guilt exactly, but out of the specific determination to understand precisely what had gone wrong at every stage of the decision and execution so that she could guarantee she never made those specific errors again. She can still describe all six errors in sequence. She will never make any of them. **Age 33 (692 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 34 (693 TR)** — Rumour or accusation — fair or unfair — put her standing at risk. **Age 34 (693 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 35 (694 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon. **Age 35 (694 TR)** — She sent one of her children out to be apprenticed or fostered. It was the right provision. The house was quieter for it. Alara fostered (age 11) **Age 36 (695 TR)** — The gossip thinned. People forgot, or found newer griefs to occupy them. She was not forgiven so much as left alone. **Age 37 (696 TR)** — Something that had always been there stopped. She noticed the quiet before she identified what had gone. **Age 37 (696 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 37 (696 TR)** — She lost Pedar — still a child, not yet grown. **Age 38 (697 TR)** — She promised to do something she expected to regret. Keeping it cost her, and the cost was worth it. **Age 39 (698 TR)** — She was called upon to speak where women rarely did — and was not found wanting. **Age 39 (698 TR)** — Her parent told the old story again. She had heard it more times than she could count. She listened anyway and found she was glad of it. **Age 39 (698 TR)** — Her daughter Rarith married Horic Elman. **Age 39 (698 TR)** — She held the grandchild and felt the full weight and joy of years. Rarith's child born (female, Rarith age 16) **Age 40 (699 TR)** — She read what texts she could find, and the night sky taught her the rest. **Age 41 (700 TR)** — She delivered an apology she had been composing for years. The other person was surprised and then moved. **Age 41 (700 TR)** — Another loss before it had become a child in any way others would recognise. She grieved it nonetheless. week 20 **Age 42 (701 TR)** — Her first child arrived. Geira. She had not understood, until the moment it was placed in her arms, how much of herself she would give to it. week 40 **Age 43 (702 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option. **Age 44 (703 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted. **Age 44 (703 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 44 (703 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 45 (704 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 45 (704 TR)** — She walked a long way with someone she loved without talking much. The quiet was good. She held onto the memory of it. **Age 46 (705 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 46 (705 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 47 (706 TR)** — She carries a knife. Every woman with any sense carries a knife. A guardsman at the town gate told her knives were not permitted in the market quarter without a licence. She asked him to show her the ordinance. He could not produce it. She waited. He eventually admitted he had misremembered. She thanked him and entered the market quarter. She noted the guardsman's name in her memory. A week later she checked the town charter at the guild house. There is no such ordinance. There never was. **Age 47 (706 TR)** — She and one of her children had grown apart, and then apart further, until there was nothing left. **Age 47 (706 TR)** — Her daughter Alara married Lucan. **Age 48 (707 TR)** — The journey required more of her than she had expected and gave back more than she had hoped. **Age 49 (708 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 50 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 50 (709 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 51 (710 TR)** — Her mother had not wanted to go. She had insisted, because the alternative was managing a dying woman in three rooms with two children underfoot. The Peonian house took her without fuss. She visited every day. Her mother died in spring, quietly, with a sister holding her hand because she had arrived twenty minutes too late. **Age 51 (710 TR)** — Her child overheard part of a conversation and reported it to everyone with complete confidence. The misunderstanding was spectacular. **Age 51 (710 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 52 (711 TR)** — The approach was made in person, by a woman she had never seen, who knew things about her that no one had any business knowing. The price was stated plainly. She paid it. She is still paying it. She has not yet determined who is behind the approach, which tells her they are very good at this. **Age 52 (711 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 53 (712 TR)** — She studied from a written wordlist compiled by someone who had died at Osel, supplemented by conversations with three different informants who disagreed on half the vocabulary. The disagreements were the most informative part. Language is politics in concentrated form. **Age 53 (712 TR)** — She sat up all night with someone sick. Nothing dramatic happened. By dawn they were through the worst and she went about her day. **Age 53 (712 TR)** — One of her children had a child outside marriage. Tera's illegitimate child born (male, Tera age 20) **Age 54 (713 TR)** — She completed the commission and the merchant accepted it and paid the full rate. The merchant did not know she was the apprentice rather than the journeyman. She decided not to clarify this, which is an ethical position she has revisited several times since. Her conclusion: the work was good enough. The classification of who did it is a guild matter, not a customer matter. She told the master. The master said: correct. **Age 54 (713 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 54 (713 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 54 (713 TR)** — Rarith's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Rarith's child died (age 0) **Age 55 (714 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour. **Age 55 (714 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 56 (715 TR)** — Months of close reading: liturgical texts, canonical precedent, the letters of the senior clergy. The work was slow and the illumination on the margins of the oldest manuscripts was occasionally distracting. **Age 56 (715 TR)** — Her daughter Tera married Rhoric Voring. **Age 56 (715 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 56 (715 TR)** — Tera left to make her own way. **Age 57 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 57 (716 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 57 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 57 (716 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 57 (716 TR)** — Tera's infant did not survive. She felt the grief twice — for the child and for her own child's loss. Tera's child died (age 1) **Age 58 (717 TR)** — Another difficult year. Life continued. **Age 58 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had. **Age 59 (718 TR)** — The mark was permanent — ear, thumb, or cheek. She would carry it the rest of her life. **Age 59 (718 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same. **Age 60 (719 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Aela **Age 61 (720 TR)** — She had learned to be careful. This time she had not been careful enough. The look on the stranger's face told her everything. **Age 61 (720 TR)** — He endured. The details were different; the shape was the same.
name: Ulra

narrative: **Age 18 (683 TR)** — Music was part of a proper upbringing. She took the lessons seriously.
**Age 19 (684 TR)** — She dreamed something that she decided not to speak of. She has never spoken of it.

**Age 20 (685 TR)** — The mistress cuffed her across the back of the head hard enough to make her ears ring. The reason was a miscounted measure that had ruined two yards of good cloth. She did not cry — she had learned by this point that crying produced worse outcomes than silence. She fixed the count, restarted the section, and did not make the same error again. She has thought about the incident since mostly in terms of what the mistress actually communicated: that the cloth mattered more than the learning, and that the learning was her problem to solve, not the mistress's problem to manage.
**Age 21 (686 TR)** — Dancing was expected of her from childhood. She was accomplished.

**Age 22 (687 TR)** — The lock had been forced cleanly. They had known what they were looking for. Her coin box, the good cloth, the thing her mother had left her. She found herself straightening objects that had been moved and then stopped because she could not stop thinking about the hands that had moved them.

**Age 23 (688 TR)** — She ruined two yards of figured cloth that the master had been saving for a specific commission. The sound the mistress made when she saw it was not a word. She sat with that sound for a long time afterward — not out of guilt exactly, but out of the specific determination to understand precisely what had gone wrong at every stage of the decision and execution so that she could guarantee she never made those specific errors again. She can still describe all six errors in sequence. She will never make any of them.

**Age 25 (690 TR)** — She studied from a written wordlist compiled by someone who had died at Osel, supplemented by conversations with three different informants who disagreed on half the vocabulary. The disagreements were the most informative part. Language is politics in concentrated form.

**Age 26 (691 TR)** — A remote posting: a hamlet on the edge of the kingdom, or a lord’s estate where the last priest had left without explanation. The people needed the rites performed and she performed them.
**Age 27 (692 TR)** — Justice was dispensed quickly: coin taken or skin marked. She paid the price.
**Age 28 (693 TR)** — She knelt and gave her answers. The Hesa-Eran bound her to the order. She had known since she was twelve it would come to this.
**Age 29 (694 TR)** — Matakea. The full title. She had spent seven years as Ashesa working toward this and the ceremony itself was over in an afternoon.

**Age 30 (695 TR)** — A child asked her why Larani did not protect good people from bad things. She gave the proper answer. The child nodded and walked away. She felt the inadequacy of the proper answer for the rest of the day.
**Age 31 (696 TR)** — The cat left a mouse on her pillow. She accepted this as the highest compliment.
**Age 32 (697 TR)** — She struck up an acquaintance that would prove valuable in years to come. Made contact: Arith (clergy)
**Age 33 (698 TR)** — She bought cheap and sold dear, and the ledger proved it.
**Age 34 (699 TR)** — Rumour or accusation — fair or unfair — put her standing at risk.
**Age 35 (700 TR)** — She was up before the household and saw something small and perfect that was gone by the time anyone else was about.
**Age 36 (701 TR)** — A deathbed she attended left her with unanswered questions about her own beliefs.
**Age 37 (702 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 38 (703 TR)** — She began to doubt the teachings she had followed without question.
**Age 40 (705 TR)** — She took a younger woman under her wing and passed on what she knew. Mentored Aldric
**Age 41 (706 TR)** — Crisis drew her back to her faith with new fervour.
**Age 42 (707 TR)** — The loss required time she had not expected to spend on it. She gave the time. There was no other option.
**Age 43 (708 TR)** — She walked to the shrine. The journey changed something — not in the way she expected, which was perhaps what it was supposed to do.
**Age 44 (709 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 45 (710 TR)** — Her childbearing years had passed. She felt the change in herself, and adapted.
**Age 46 (711 TR)** — Patronage came with influence and obligations. She accepted both and managed the latter with care.
**Age 47 (712 TR)** — She was among the crowd when the sentence was carried out. The silence that fell at the end was not what she expected.
**Age 48 (713 TR)** — The recovery was long. She measured it in small returns — the walk she could finish, the task she could complete.
**Age 49 (714 TR)** — Brigands relieved her of coin and dignity alike.
**Age 51 (716 TR)** — A named posting — chaplain to a Lady of Paladins chapter. The knights watched her carefully for the first month to see how she would take the work.
**Age 52 (717 TR)** — The year passed much as the last had.
**Age 53 (718 TR)** — She watched the web of favours and enmities carefully, learning where to place her feet.
**Age 54 (719 TR)** — She stayed up until the candle was gone. She was not sure what she had been doing for the last hour.
**Age 55 (720 TR)** — The change had been gradual and then sudden. She could trace exactly when it had stopped being repairable. Aldric became an enemy

name: Vara

narrative: **Age 18 (712 TR)** — Necessity made him the nearest thing to a healer in his community.
**Age 19 (713 TR)** — He made a habit of it — the early mornings, the regular exertion. It accumulated.

**Age 20 (714 TR)** — The master struck him across the ear for a piece of work that was not good enough. Not a severe beating — a correction, delivered with the flat of the hand and the specific economy of someone for whom this was a routine instructional tool rather than an expression of anger. The work had not been good enough. He accepted this. He also noted, privately, the specific quality of the master's expression: not angry, not sadistic, but impatient in the way of someone who expected better and was expressing that expectation physically because the verbal expression had not been sufficient. He made the work better. The master did not comment on the improvement.
**Age 21 (715 TR)** — A killing in the neighbourhood pulled him in — as witness, as kin, as someone who knew both sides. He had to choose where he stood, or at least appear to. Feud with Roric Edsen
**Age 22 (716 TR)** — A gamble on goods paid off handsomely.
**Age 23 (717 TR)** — He travelled far with a caravan, seeing parts of Kaldor few of his neighbours ever would.
**Age 24 (718 TR)** — He enters every room the same way: two paces in, stop, count the doors and windows. It takes a heartbeat. He has done it so long he no longer thinks about it.
**Age 25 (719 TR)** — Hunger, debt, or simple greed — he took what was not his.

**Age 26 (720 TR)** — The job was handed to him without explanation — a merchant's strongbox, a specific night, a contact at the eastern gate. He did not know until afterward, when his Shadow named what he had become. There was no ceremony. There were only consequences.

name: Harlan Warson

narrative: **Age 18 (718 TR)** — The Serolan's man arrived with the assessment and he disagreed with the figure. The disagreement was conducted formally and went nowhere. He paid a revised amount that was still more than he thought just, and the Serolan's man left satisfied in the particular way of someone who has extracted more than they expected.
**Age 19 (719 TR)** — The purse is heavier than it looks. He can feel the specific weight of it through the lining of his coat, separate from his own coin, with its own gravity.

**Age 20 (720 TR)** — The master struck him across the ear for a piece of work that was not good enough. Not a severe beating — a correction, delivered with the flat of the hand and the specific economy of someone for whom this was a routine instructional tool rather than an expression of anger. He made the work better. The master did not comment on the improvement.
